Prowess — High-Level Schema

9 domains · 42 tables · Click any card to jump to detailed schema

Tables
9
Domains

Migrations
~68
RLS Policies

Auth Paths
📦 Reference & Lookup▾
Reference
currencies
ISO currency master — single source of truth for all monetary fields across the system.
code UNIQUEactive soft-disable
Reference
exchange_rates
Date-effective FX rates. One rate per currency pair per day.
UNIQUE(from, to, date)CHECK from≠to
Reference
picklists
Dropdown category definitions e.g. LOCATION, DESIGNATION, NATIONALITY, MARITAL_STATUS.
code UNIQUE
Reference
picklist_values
Options for each picklist. Self-ref parent_value_id enables Country→State→City hierarchies.
parent_value_id self-refactive soft-disable
Reference
projects
Projects that expense line items can be billed against.
code UNIQUEis_active flag
🔍 Lookup Views (mig 147 — SECURITY INVOKER, active rows only, ESS-accessible)
View
vw_projects_lookup
Active projects for expense line item dropdowns. Requires projects.lookup permission.
WHERE active=trueid, name, datesuseProjectsLookup()
View
vw_currencies_lookup
Active currencies for expense currency dropdowns. Requires currencies.lookup permission.
WHERE active=trueid, code, name, symboluseCurrenciesLookup()
View
vw_picklist_values_lookup
Active picklist values joined with picklist code. All transactional dropdowns (category, designation, etc.). Requires picklists.lookup.
WHERE active=truepicklist_code textusePicklistValuesLookup()
View
vw_departments_lookup
Non-deleted departments for dropdown selectors. Requires departments.lookup permission.
WHERE deleted_at IS NULLid, dept_id, nameuseDepartmentsLookup()
👥 HR / Employees▾
HR
departments
Company departments. Current head resolved via department_heads WHERE to_date IS NULL.
code UNIQUEparent_id self-refsoft delete
HR
department_heads
Historical record of department head assignments. to_date IS NULL = current head.
UNIQUE(dept, from_date)
HR
employees
Core employee record. manager_id self-ref drives org chart and approval routing.
business_email UNIQUEmanager_id self-refsoft delete
HR · Satellite
employee_personal
1-to-1 with employees. nationality · marital_status · gender · dob · photo_url. RLS-gated separately.
employee_id PK+FKpicklist UUIDs
HR · Satellite
employee_contact
1-to-1. country_code · mobile · personal_email. RLS-gated separately from employment.
employee_id PK+FK
HR · Satellite
employee_employment
1-to-1. designation · job_title · probation_end_date. Admin-only by default.
employee_id PK+FK⚠ admin-only edit
HR · Satellite
employee_addresses
Residential and mailing addresses. Supports multiple address types per employee.
employee_id FKaddress_type
HR · Satellite
emergency_contacts
Multiple emergency contacts per employee. name · relationship · phone · email.
employee_id FKmultiple rows
HR · Satellite
identity_records
National IDs, visas and other identity documents per employee.
id_typeexpiry tracking
HR · Satellite
passports
Passport details per employee. passport_number · issuing_country · issue_date · expiry_date.
employee_id FKvisa_expiry
HR · Satellite
employee_dependents
Effective-dated dependent records per employee. Active row = is_active=true AND effective_to='9999-12-31'. Multiple dependents per employee via dependent_code grouping.
dependent_code auto-generatedeffective datingaudit trigger
HR · Satellite
employee_dependent_attachments
Supporting documents for dependent records. Linked by dependent_code (not dependent_id version) so attachments persist across effective-dated amendments.
dependent_codeis_active soft-deletehr-attachments bucket
🔑 Auth & Identity▾
Auth
profiles
Auth user profile. Linked to employee record on invite accept via email match.
id = auth.users.idemployee_id FK nullable
Auth
super_admins
Break-glass UUID allowlist. is_super_admin() bypasses ALL permission checks. service_role writes only.
profile_id PK🔒 immutable
Auth
roles
Named roles assigned to users e.g. Employee, Manager, Finance, Admin.
code UNIQUE
Auth
user_roles
Many-to-many: user ↔ role. Tracks assignment source (manual vs auto). Audited on every change.
(profile_id, role_id) PKis_activeaudit trigger
🛡️ Permissions (RBP)▾
Permissions
modules
Top-level grouping for permissions e.g. expense, employee, workflow, security.
code UNIQUEsort_order
Permissions
permissions
Atomic permission codes e.g. expense.submit, employee.edit, sec_admin_access.view.
code UNIQUEmodule_id FK
Permissions
permission_sets
Named bundles of permissions e.g. "ESS Bundle", "Finance Bundle". Assigned to roles.
name
Permissions
permission_set_items
Which permissions belong to a set. Adding a permission here instantly grants it to all assigned roles.
(set_id, perm_id) composite PKaudit trigger
Permissions
permission_set_assignments
Assigns a permission set to a role, scoped to a target group. target_group_id is always set — NULL never occurs in practice (super admin bypasses this table entirely via is_super_admin()).
role_id FKtarget_group_id always setaudit trigger
Permissions
target_groups
Scope definitions: self / everyone / direct_l1 / direct_l2 / same_dept / same_country / custom.
scope_typeis_system undeletable
Permissions
target_group_members
Pre-computed membership cache for everyone/custom scopes. Synced by pg_cron job.
synced by pg_cron(member_id, group_id) IDX
💳 Expense Management▾
Expense
expense_reports
Report header. Full lifecycle: draft → submitted → approved | rejected.
status enumsoft delete (draft only)employee_id FKaudit trigger
Expense
line_items
Individual expense line items. Each has date, category, currency, amount, project, notes.
report_id FKcurrency_id FKproject_id FKaudit trigger
Expense
attachments
Receipts and supporting documents. Linked to a report or a specific line item.
report_id FKline_item_id nullableSupabase Storage URLaudit trigger
⚙️ Workflow Engine▾
Workflow
workflow_templates
Configurable approval templates with full versioning. One active template per code at a time.
UNIQUE(code) WHERE is_activeUNIQUE(code, version)
Workflow
workflow_steps
Ordered steps within a template. approver_type routes to: MANAGER / ROLE / DEPT_HEAD / SPECIFIC_USER.
UNIQUE(template, step_order)approver_type
Workflow
workflow_instances
Live execution per business record. Only one in_progress instance per record at a time.
UNIQUE active per recordmodule_codesubmitter_id FK
Workflow
workflow_tasks
Per-step approval tasks. Assigned to a specific user. Written only via wf_approve() / wf_reject() RPCs.
assigned_to FKRPC-only writes
Workflow
wf_sla_events
SLA breach and escalation events triggered when tasks exceed their deadline.
instance_id FKevent_type
Workflow
wf_delegations
Temporary delegation of approval authority from one user to another with a date range.
delegator / delegate FKdate range
Workflow
notifications
In-app notifications. read_at = NULL means unread. Carries contextual payload (report_id etc).
user_id FKread_at nullablemetadata jsonb
Workflow
workflow_step_conditions
Optional conditions on a workflow step. When all conditions match, the step is auto-skipped. Multiple rows are ANDed.
step_id FKoperator CHECKskip_step bool
Workflow
workflow_action_log
Immutable, append-only event log for every workflow state change: submit, approve, reject, reassign, withdraw, cancel.
instance_id FK🔒 append-onlymetadata jsonb
Workflow
workflow_notification_templates
Mustache-style message templates. Placeholders like {{submitter_name}} resolved from queue payload at delivery time.
UNIQUE(code)title_tmpl + body_tmpl
Workflow
workflow_notification_queue
Outbound notification staging. Background job renders template + payload, writes to notifications, marks as sent.
instance_id FKstatus: pending/sent/failedpayload jsonb
Workflow
workflow_assignment_audit
Immutable audit log of all workflow assignment changes. Written by trigger only. Tracks old/new template and effective dates.
assignment_id FK🔒 trigger-only writeschanged_by FK
Workflow
workflow_pending_changes
Proposed changes submitted through the workflow engine for non-expense modules (employee edits, dept changes). Stores diff as proposed_data jsonb.
module_codeinstance_id FKproposed_data jsonbstatus CHECK
📊 Views (read-only, no RLS)
View
vw_wf_pending_tasks
Tasks the current user (auth.uid()) still needs to act on. Drives the Approver Inbox. Includes SLA status: on_track / due_soon / overdue.
WHERE assigned_to = auth.uid()sla_status computed
View
vw_wf_my_requests
All workflow instances submitted by the current user, with current step and approver name. Drives the My Requests panel.
WHERE submitted_by = auth.uid()current_approver_name
View
vw_wf_operations
System-wide pending tasks for workflow admins. Adds human-readable display_id (EXP-YYYYMMDD-XXXXXX), dept, SLA status, overdue flag.
admin-onlydisplay_id computedSLA: normal/overdue/critical
View
vw_notification_monitor
Joins notification queue with in-app and email delivery status. Exposes can_retry flag and overall_status. Used by the notification monitor UI.
inapp_status + email_statuscan_retry computedretry_count
View
pending_invite_reminders
Employees whose invite was sent but not accepted. Polled by the sync job to fire reminder emails. reminder_sent_at set after each reminder.
status = sentinvite_accepted_at IS NULL
📋 Audit▾
Audit
audit_log
General action log. Permission changes, role assignments, admin actions. Append-only.
INSERT only via triggers🔒 immutable
Audit
employee_audit_log
Row-level change trail for employees + all 7 satellite tables. Stores old_data / new_data as jsonb.
old_data / new_data jsonb8 triggers attached🔒 immutable
⏱️ Background Jobs▾
Jobs
job_run_log
pg_cron job execution history. Tracks status, duration, rows affected, and any error messages.
job_code IDXstarted_at IDXstatus: success/failed/running

Key Foreign-Key Relationships

profiles.employee_id→employees.idLinks auth user to employee record
user_roles.profile_id→profiles.id (auth.users)Which user holds which roles
user_roles.role_id→roles.idRole being assigned
permission_set_assignments.role_id→roles.idWhich role gets this permission set
permission_set_assignments.permission_set_id→permission_sets.idThe named permission bundle
permission_set_assignments.target_group_id→target_groups.idAlways set — determines which employees this role can act on
permission_set_items.permission_set_id→permission_sets.idItems within a permission bundle
permission_set_items.permission_id→permissions.idSpecific permission code
permissions.module_id→modules.idGroups permissions by module
target_group_members.group_id→target_groups.idCached membership
target_group_members.member_id→employees.idWhich employee is in the group
employee_personal/contact/employment.employee_id→employees.idSatellite tables tied to core record
expense_reports.employee_id→employees.idReport owner
line_items.report_id→expense_reports.idLine items belong to a report
workflow_instances.template_id→workflow_templates.idWhich template governs this instance
workflow_tasks.instance_id→workflow_instances.idApproval tasks for an instance
employees.dept_id→departments.idDepartment assignment
employees.manager_id→employees.idSelf-referential org hierarchy
audit_log / employee_audit_log→employees.id, auth.usersWho changed what and when

Permission Architecture (3-Layer)

🔒
DB Layer
user_can(module, action, owner_id)
Called by every RLS policy.
Path A = super admin bypass.
Path B = admin module (p_owner NULL).
Path D = scope-aware PSA chain.
🖥️
UI Gate Layer
get_my_permissions()
Called on login. Cached in React context.
ProtectedRoute blocks pages.
Super admin → returns ALL codes.
👥
Data Scope Layer
get_target_population()
Determines which employees are visible.
mode: all / scoped / none.
Super admin → always mode: all.

Prowess — Detailed Field Schema

Hover any field for type, constraints & description

📦 Reference Domain 5 tables + 4 views

currenciesReferenceISO currency master — single source of truth for all money values▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()Surrogate primary key
codeUNIQUEtextNOT NULL—ISO 4217 code e.g. INR, USD, SAR
nametextNOT NULL—Human-readable name e.g. Indian Rupee
symboltextNOT NULL—Display symbol: ₹ $ ﷼
activebooleanNOT NULLtrueSoft-disable flag — inactive currencies hidden in dropdowns
created_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()Record creation timestamp
updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()Auto-maintained by trigger
exchange_ratesReferenceDaily FX rates between currency pairs for expense conversion▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()Surrogate PK
from_currency_idFKIDXuuidNOT NULL—Source currency → currencies.id
to_currency_idFKuuidNOT NULL—Target currency → currencies.id
ratenumeric(18,6)NOT NULL—Conversion multiplier: 1 FROM = rate × TO
effective_dateUNIQUE w/ pairdateNOT NULL—Date the rate is valid. UNIQUE(from, to, date) → one rate per pair per day
created_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()Record creation
CHECK: from_currency_id <> to_currency_id (no self-conversion)
picklistsReferenceDropdown category definitions — each row is a named dropdown group▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
picklist_idUNIQUEtextNOT NULL—Machine key: LOCATION, DESIGNATION, EXPENSE_CATEGORY
nametextNOT NULL—Human label shown in admin UI
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
picklist_valuesReferenceValues within each picklist. Supports cascading dropdowns (Country→State→City)▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
picklist_idFKuuidNOT NULL—Parent picklist → picklists.id (CASCADE on delete)
valuetextNOT NULL—The dropdown option text
parent_value_idFKuuidnullableNULLSelf-reference for cascading (e.g. State's parent is Country value)
sort_orderintegernullableNULLDisplay order within the picklist
activebooleanNOT NULLtrueSoft-disable without losing historical data
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
projectsReferenceProject master list — used as a cost centre / WBS tag on expense line items▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
nametextNOT NULL—Project display name
codeUNIQUEtextnullableNULLOptional short code for reporting
activebooleanNOT NULLtrueInactive projects hidden from dropdowns
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—

🔍 Reference Lookup Views mig 147 · SECURITY INVOKER · ESS-accessible

vw_projects_lookupViewActive projects for expense line item dropdowns — requires projects.lookup permission▸
FieldTypeSourceNotes
idPKuuidprojects.idStore as FK on line_items — never store project name string
nametextprojects.nameDisplay label in dropdown
start_datedateprojects.start_dateFor date-aware filtering in expense forms
end_datedateprojects.end_dateFor date-aware filtering in expense forms
Filter: WHERE active = true | Permission: projects.lookup (ESS) | Frontend hook: useProjectsLookup()
vw_currencies_lookupViewActive currencies for expense currency dropdowns — requires currencies.lookup permission▸
FieldTypeSourceNotes
idPKuuidcurrencies.idStore as FK on transactions — prefer UUID over code string
codetextcurrencies.codeISO 4217 e.g. USD, INR, SAR
nametextcurrencies.nameHuman-readable name e.g. Indian Rupee
symboltextcurrencies.symbolDisplay symbol: ₹ $ ﷼
Filter: WHERE active = true | Permission: currencies.lookup (ESS) | Frontend hook: useCurrenciesLookup()
vw_picklist_values_lookupViewActive picklist values with picklist code — all transactional dropdowns (category, designation, etc.)▸
FieldTypeSourceNotes
idPKuuidpicklist_values.idStore as FK on transaction rows
picklist_codetextpicklists.picklist_idFilter key e.g. EXPENSE_CATEGORY, DESIGNATION, NATIONALITY
valuetextpicklist_values.valueDisplay label
ref_idtextpicklist_values.ref_idOptional short code for API integration
parent_value_iduuidpicklist_values.parent_value_idCascading dropdowns (Country → State → City)
Filter: WHERE active = true (JOIN of picklist_values + picklists) | Permission: picklists.lookup — gates both tables via SECURITY INVOKER JOIN | Hook: usePicklistValuesLookup()
vw_departments_lookupViewNon-deleted departments for dropdown selectors — requires departments.lookup permission▸
FieldTypeSourceNotes
idPKuuiddepartments.idStore as FK on employee records
dept_idtextdepartments.dept_idHuman-readable code e.g. DEPT001
nametextdepartments.nameDisplay label in dropdown
Filter: WHERE deleted_at IS NULL | Permission: departments.lookup (ESS) | Frontend hook: useDepartmentsLookup()

🏢 Organisation Domain 3 tables

departmentsOrganisationCompany department structure. Employees FK here for org chart.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
nameUNIQUEtextNOT NULL—Department display name
codetextnullableNULLShort code for reporting/exports
parent_idFKuuidnullableNULLSelf-reference — supports nested department hierarchy
activebooleanNOT NULLtrueSoft-disable inactive departments
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
department_headsOrganisationHistorical record of who headed each department. One active head at a time.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
department_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ departments.id
employee_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ employees.id. The person who is/was head
from_dateUNIQUE w/ deptdateNOT NULL—Start of tenure. UNIQUE(department_id, from_date)
to_datedatenullableNULLNULL = currently active. Set when superseded.
employeesOrganisationCore employee record — identity, status, org placement. Satellite tables hold PII.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
employee_numberUNIQUEtextnullableNULLHuman-readable ID e.g. EMP-001. Set on activation.
first_name / last_nametextNOT NULL—Legal name fields
business_emailUNIQUEIDXtextnullableNULLWork email — used to auto-link profile on sign-up
statusIDXtextNOT NULL'Draft'Lifecycle: Draft → Incomplete → Active → Inactive. RLS routes by this value.
dept_idFKuuidnullableNULL→ departments.id. Determines same_department scope.
manager_idFKuuidnullableNULL→ employees.id (self-ref). Used for direct_l1/l2 target scoping.
work_countrytextnullableNULLISO country code. Used for same_country target group scope.
hire_date / termination_datedatenullableNULLEmployment period boundaries
invite_sent_at / invite_accepted_attimestamptznullableNULLInvite lifecycle — stamped by handle_new_user() trigger
deleted_attimestamptznullableNULLSoft delete. All RLS policies filter deleted_at IS NULL.
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
⚡ Trigger: revoke_ess_on_deactivation — fires on status → Inactive
⚡ Trigger: audit_employees — writes to employee_audit_log on any change

👤 Employee Satellite Domain 7 tables

ℹ️ All satellite tables share the same pattern: employee_id FK → employees.id, one row per employee (1:1), separate RLS per module permission. This enables HR to view personal info without seeing employment details, and vice versa.
employee_personalEmployeePII: DOB, gender, marital status. Gated by personal_info module.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
employee_idFKUNIQUEuuidNOT NULL—→ employees.id. UNIQUE = one row per employee (1:1)
date_of_birthdatenullableNULLSensitive PII — only visible under personal_info.view permission
gendertextnullableNULLSensitive — HR/Admin only access
marital_status / nationality / blood_grouptextnullableNULLAdditional personal fields
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
employee_contactEmployeeContact details: personal email, phone. Gated by contact_info module.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
employee_idFKUNIQUEuuidNOT NULL—→ employees.id (1:1)
personal_emailtextnullableNULLPersonal (non-work) email
phone / mobiletextnullableNULLPhone numbers
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
employee_employmentEmployeeEmployment terms: designation, employment type, cost center, salary band▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
employee_idFKUNIQUEuuidNOT NULL—→ employees.id (1:1)
designation / job_titletextnullableNULLRole title — visible in org chart
employment_typetextnullableNULLFull-time / Part-time / Contract
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
employee_addressesEmployeeResidential / permanent addresses. Multiple rows per employee (address types).▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
employee_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ employees.id (1:many — multiple address types)
address_typetextNOT NULL—Residential / Permanent / Other
line1 / line2 / city / state / country / pincodetextnullableNULLStandard address fields
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
emergency_contactsEmployeeNext-of-kin contacts. Multiple per employee. Gated by emergency_contacts module.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
employee_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ employees.id
name / relationship / phonetextnullableNULLContact person details
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
identity_recordsEmployeeGovernment IDs: PAN, Aadhaar, SSN, etc. Highly sensitive — gated by identity_documents module.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
employee_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ employees.id
id_typetextNOT NULL—PAN / AADHAAR / SSN / PASSPORT_NUMBER
id_numbertextNOT NULL—⚠️ Sensitive — government ID number. Highest risk field.
expiry_datedatenullableNULLExpiry for documents that expire
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
passportsEmployeePassport details for travel. Gated by passport module.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
employee_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ employees.id
passport_numbertextNOT NULL—⚠️ Sensitive government document number
country_of_issue / issue_date / expiry_datetext/datenullableNULLPassport details
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
employee_dependentsEmployeeEffective-dated dependent records per employee. Active = is_active=true AND effective_to='9999-12-31'. Mig 289.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
dependent_codeUNIQUE (partial)textNOT NULL—Auto-generated by fn_generate_dependent_code() trigger. Format: {emp_code}_DEP_{NN}. All versions of the same dependent share this code. UNIQUE partial index on (dependent_code) WHERE effective_to='9999-12-31'.
employee_idFKIDXuuidNOT NULL—→ employees.id ON DELETE CASCADE
relationship_typetextNOT NULL—ref_id from DEPENDENT_RELATIONSHIP_TYPE picklist (DR001=Spouse, DR002=Child, DR003=Parent, DR004=Sibling, DR005=Step-Child, DR006=Domestic Partner, DR007=Ward, DR008=Other)
dependent_nametextNOT NULL—CHECK length(trim(dependent_name)) > 0
date_of_birthdateNOT NULL—CHECK date_of_birth <= CURRENT_DATE
gendertextNOT NULL—CHECK gender IN ('Male','Female') — plain text, not picklist
insurance_eligiblebooleanNOT NULLfalse—
effective_fromdateNOT NULL—Start of this version's coverage period.
effective_todateNOT NULL'9999-12-31'Open-ended row. Active = effective_to='9999-12-31' AND is_active=true. CHECK effective_to >= effective_from.
is_activebooleanNOT NULLtruefalse + effective_to='9999-12-31' = terminal removal record.
inactive_at / inactive_bytimestamptz / uuidnullableNULLStamped when is_active flipped to false. inactive_by → profiles.id.
created_by / updated_byFKuuidNOT NULL—→ profiles.id
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
employee_dependent_attachmentsEmployeeSupporting documents for dependent records. Linked by dependent_code so attachments survive effective-dated amendments. Mig 289.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
dependent_codeIDXtextNOT NULL—Logical identity link — not FK-enforced, so attachments persist across version rows.
employee_idFKIDXuuidNOT NULL—→ employees.id. Denormalised for RLS efficiency.
dependent_idFKuuidnullableNULL→ employee_dependents.id (nullable). Version context — records which row triggered the upload.
document_typetextnullableNULLref_id from DEPENDENT_DOCUMENT_TYPE picklist (DD001=Birth Certificate, DD002=Marriage Certificate, DD003=Passport Copy, DD004=National ID Copy, DD005=Insurance Proof, DD006=Adoption Certificate, DD007=Other)
file_nametextNOT NULL—Sanitized storage filename
original_file_nametextNOT NULL—User's original filename
file_pathtextNOT NULL—Storage path in hr-attachments bucket: dependents/{employee_id}/{dependent_code}/{file}
mime_typetextNOT NULL——
file_sizebigintNOT NULL—CHECK file_size > 0. Bytes.
is_activebooleanNOT NULLtrueSoft-delete without removing storage file.
uploaded_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
uploaded_by / created_by / updated_byFKuuidNOT NULL—→ profiles.id
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—

🔑 Auth & Identity Domain 5 tables

profilesAuthBridges auth.users → employees. One row per Supabase auth user, created by handle_new_user() trigger on sign-up. Display name (full_name, avatar_url) lives in auth.users metadata — not stored here.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKFK→auth.usersuuidNOT NULL—1:1 with auth.users.id. CASCADE DELETE — removing the auth user removes the profile.
employee_idFKUNIQUEuuidnullableNULL→ employees.id (ON DELETE SET NULL). Auto-linked by handle_new_user() when business_email matches. NULL = auth user has no employee record. Used by get_workflow_participants() to resolve approver display names via employees.
is_activebooleanNOT NULLtrueDeactivated profiles cannot log in. Inactive employees lose system access without deleting the Supabase auth user.
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
rolesAuthRole definitions. Admin configures which permission sets belong to each role.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
codeUNIQUEtextNOT NULL—Machine key: admin, manager, finance, employee, ess
nametextNOT NULL—Human label shown in UI
descriptiontextnullableNULLPurpose of this role
created_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
user_rolesAuthJunction: which user holds which role. Supports expiry and soft-revoke.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
profile_idPK (composite)FKuuidNOT NULL—→ auth.users.id. The user receiving the role.
role_idPK (composite)FKuuidNOT NULL—→ roles.id. The role being granted.
is_activeIDXbooleanNOT NULLtrueSoft-revoke without deleting history. All user_can() checks require is_active=true.
expires_attimestamptznullableNULLNULL = permanent. Set for contractors / temp access. user_can() checks expires_at > now().
granted_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()When the role was granted
granted_byFKuuidnullableNULL→ auth.users.id. Who granted this role.
assignment_sourcetextNOT NULL'manual'manual = admin set it · system = auto-sync (sync_system_roles)
updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()Auto-maintained by trigger
⚡ Trigger: audit_user_roles (migration 143)
super_adminsAuthUUID allowlist. Break-glass accounts that bypass ALL permission checks. Service-role writes only.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
profile_idPKFK→auth.usersuuidNOT NULL—The super admin user. Cannot be modified via app — Supabase dashboard only.
granted_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
granted_bytextnullableNULLFree-text note e.g. "initial seed — migration 112"
🔒 RLS: SELECT only for authenticated · No INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policies → service_role only
employee_invitesAuthTracks invite state. Auto-accepted when employee signs up matching business_email.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
employee_idFKUNIQUEuuidNOT NULL—→ employees.id. One invite per employee.
emailtextNOT NULL—Must match employees.business_email for auto-link to work
statustextNOT NULL'pending'pending → accepted (auto via trigger)
invited_byFKuuidnullableNULL→ auth.users.id. Admin who sent the invite.
created_at / accepted_attimestamptznullableNULLLifecycle timestamps

🛡️ Permissions Domain 7 tables

modulesPermissionsFeature modules that permissions are grouped under. e.g. employee_details, personal_info.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
codeUNIQUEtextNOT NULL—Machine key used in user_can() calls: employee_details, personal_info, passport, etc.
nametextNOT NULL—Human label in Permission Matrix UI
activebooleanNOT NULLtrueInactive modules hidden from Permission Matrix
sort_orderintegernullableNULLDisplay order in Permission Matrix
permissionsPermissionsIndividual permission codes. Pattern: module_code.action e.g. employee_details.view▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
codeUNIQUEtextNOT NULL—Full permission code: employee_details.view, passport.edit, etc.
module_idFKuuidnullableNULL→ modules.id. Groups this permission under a module.
actiontextnullableNULLview / edit / create / delete / history / manage. NULL = legacy non-RBP permission.
name / descriptiontextnullableNULLHuman label and explanation in Permission Catalog
sort_orderintegernullableNULLDisplay order within module
permission_setsPermissionsNamed bundles of permissions. e.g. "Manager – Direct Team". Reusable across roles.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
nametextNOT NULL—Admin-chosen display name
descriptiontextnullableNULLPurpose description shown in Permission Matrix
created_byFKuuidnullableNULL→ profiles.id. Who created this set. SET NULL on profile delete.
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
⚡ Trigger: trg_pset_updated_at · Trigger: audit_permission_set_items (migration 143)
permission_set_itemsPermissionsJunction: which permissions belong to a set. Composite PK — no standalone id.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
permission_set_idPK (composite)FKuuidNOT NULL—→ permission_sets.id (CASCADE on delete)
permission_idPK (composite)FKuuidNOT NULL—→ permissions.id (CASCADE on delete)
permission_set_assignmentsPermissionsLinks a permission set to a role + optional target group. UNIQUE(set, role, target_group).▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
permission_set_idFKIDXuuidNOT NULL—→ permission_sets.id (CASCADE)
role_idFKIDXuuidNOT NULL—→ roles.id (CASCADE)
target_group_idFKIDXuuidnullableNULL→ target_groups.id (RESTRICT). NULL = admin-module grant (no scoping). Non-null = EV module scoped to that group.
created_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
UNIQUE (permission_set_id, role_id, target_group_id)⚡ Trigger: audit_permission_set_assignments (migration 143)
target_groupsPermissionsDefines which employees a permission applies to. Scope types determine membership resolution.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
labeltextNOT NULL—Admin-visible name e.g. "All Active Employees"
scope_typetextNOT NULL—everyone / self / direct_l1 / direct_l2 / same_department / same_country / custom
filter_rulesjsonbnullableNULLJSON criteria for custom scope type
is_systembooleanNOT NULLfalseSystem groups cannot be deleted via UI (e.g. Everyone)
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
target_group_membersPermissionsPre-computed membership cache for everyone/custom scopes. Re-synced by pg_cron job.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
group_idPK (composite)FKuuidNOT NULL—→ target_groups.id (CASCADE)
member_idPK (composite)FKIDXuuidNOT NULL—→ employees.id (CASCADE). The employee who is a member.
⚠️ direct_l1/l2/same_dept/same_country scopes are NOT cached here — resolved live in user_can() for freshness.

💰 Expense Domain 3 tables

expense_reportsExpenseOne report per submission. Soft-delete only. Draft-only deletion enforced by CHECK.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
employee_idFKIDXuuidNOT NULL—→ employees.id. Report owner.
nametextNOT NULL—Report title e.g. "May 2026 Travel Expenses"
statusexpense_statusNOT NULL'draft'draft → submitted → approved | rejected
base_currency_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ currencies.id. Base currency for total calculation.
submitted_attimestamptznullableNULLStamped by wf_submit() RPC
approved_at / approved_bytimestamptz / uuidnullableNULLSet on workflow completion
rejected_at / rejected_by / rejection_reasonmixednullableNULLSet on rejection with reason text
deleted_attimestamptznullableNULLSoft delete. CHECK: deleted_at IS NULL OR status='draft'
CHECK: deleted_at IS NULL OR status = 'draft' — hard block on deleting submitted reports
line_itemsExpenseIndividual expense entries within a report. Amount, currency, category, project.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
report_idFKIDXuuidNOT NULL—→ expense_reports.id (CASCADE on delete)
descriptiontextNOT NULL—What was spent on
amountnumeric(12,2)NOT NULL—Amount in the line item's currency
currency_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ currencies.id. Line item currency.
categorytextnullableNULLExpense category from picklist
project_idFKuuidnullableNULL→ projects.id. Cost centre tagging.
expense_datedateNOT NULL—Date the expense was incurred
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
attachmentsExpenseReceipt files linked to line items. Stored in Supabase Storage bucket.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
line_item_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ line_items.id (CASCADE)
storage_pathtextNOT NULL—Supabase Storage object path
file_name / file_size / mime_typetext/bigintnullableNULLFile metadata for display
created_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—

⚙️ Workflow Domain 15 tables + 5 views

workflow_templatesWorkflowDefines approval flows. Versioned. One active template per module code at a time.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
codetextNOT NULL—Module identifier e.g. expense_report
versionUNIQUE w/ codeintegerNOT NULL1Version number. UNIQUE(code, version).
name / descriptiontextnullableNULLTemplate display info
is_activeUNIQUE WHERE activebooleanNOT NULLfalseUNIQUE(code) WHERE is_active=true → only one active per module
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
workflow_stepsWorkflowOrdered steps within a template. approver_type drives routing logic.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
template_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ workflow_templates.id (CASCADE)
step_orderUNIQUE w/ templateintegerNOT NULL—1-based step sequence. UNIQUE(template_id, step_order).
approver_typetextNOT NULL—MANAGER / ROLE / DEPT_HEAD / SPECIFIC_USER / RULE_BASED
approver_role / approver_profile_idtext/uuidnullableNULLUsed when approver_type = ROLE or SPECIFIC_USER
sla_hoursintegernullableNULLSLA window in hours. Breach triggers escalation.
is_cc_stepbooleanNOT NULLfalseCC steps notify but auto-advance without requiring action
approval_modetextnullableNULLMulti-approver completion rule (mig 200). NULL = ROLE fans out, first to approve wins; all other types single task. 'ALL_OF' = every active role holder must approve before advancing. CHECK (approval_mode IN ('ALL_OF') OR approval_mode IS NULL) — ANY_OF was removed in mig 204.
allow_editbooleanNOT NULLfalseWhen true, the active task assignee may edit the module record mid-flight via wf_approver_update_pending_changes (mig 193).
workflow_step_approversWorkflowCo-approver list for multi-approver steps (mig 200). Deprecated by Option A (mig 204) — rows cleared, table kept for rollback.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
step_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ workflow_steps.id (CASCADE). The multi-approver step this row belongs to.
approver_typetextNOT NULL—MANAGER / ROLE / DEPT_HEAD / SPECIFIC_USER / RULE_BASED / SELF
approver_roletextnullableNULLRole code when approver_type IN ('ROLE', 'RULE_BASED')
approver_profile_idFKuuidnullableNULL→ profiles.id when approver_type = 'SPECIFIC_USER'
sort_orderintegerNOT NULL0Display order within the step's co-approver list
created_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
⚠ Deprecated — Option A Simplification (mig 204)
workflow_step_approvers was added in mig 200 to hold explicit co-approver lists for multi-approver steps. In mig 204 (Option A), the model switched to role-driven fan-out — the engine now reads user_roles at runtime to resolve all active role holders for ROLE-type steps. All rows in this table were deleted; the table was kept in place to avoid a breaking DDL change in case of rollback. No new rows are written by the engine. The UI no longer exposes a co-approver list editor.
workflow_instancesWorkflowOne instance per submitted record. UNIQUE(module_code, record_id) WHERE in_progress.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
template_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ workflow_templates.id. Snapshot of template version used.
module_codeUNIQUE w/ recordtextNOT NULL—Module this workflow belongs to e.g. expense_report
record_idUNIQUE w/ moduleuuidNOT NULL—The record being approved e.g. expense_report.id
statusIDXtextNOT NULL'in_progress'in_progress / approved / rejected / withdrawn / cancelled
current_stepintegerNOT NULL1Which step is currently awaiting action
submitter_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ profiles.id. Who submitted.
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
UNIQUE (module_code, record_id) WHERE status = 'in_progress' — one active flow per record
⚠ RLS Design Note — Circular Dependency (mig 174)
wf_instances_select needs three access paths: submitter, wf_manage.view, and assigned approver. The approver clause originally used an inline EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM workflow_tasks WHERE assigned_to = auth.uid()). This created a circular dependency: wf_tasks_select queries workflow_instances (to let submitters see their tasks), and wf_instances_select queries workflow_tasks (to let approvers see instances) — each policy triggering the other infinitely.

Fix (mig 174): The inline EXISTS was replaced with is_wf_task_assignee(instance_id) — a SECURITY DEFINER function that reads workflow_tasks as the function owner, bypassing RLS and breaking the loop. The function still uses auth.uid() internally so no permissions are widened.

The approver clause must stay: vw_wf_pending_tasks (the approver inbox view) is a plain CREATE VIEW that JOINs workflow_instances. If approvers cannot SELECT that table, the JOIN returns nothing and the inbox goes blank. Removing the clause was investigated and ruled out — see mig 174 comment.
workflow_tasksWorkflowAction items for approvers. One task per step per instance. Status tracks approval decision.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
instance_idFKIDXuuidNOT NULL—→ workflow_instances.id (CASCADE)
step_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ workflow_steps.id. Which step this task belongs to.
assigned_toFKIDXuuidNOT NULL—→ profiles.id. The approver for this task.
statustextNOT NULL'pending'pending / approved / rejected / skipped / returned
commentstextnullableNULLApprover comments on the decision
acted_attimestamptznullableNULLWhen the approver took action
⚠ RLS Cross-Reference — Circular Dependency Partner (mig 154 / 174)
wf_tasks_select (mig 154) contains an inline EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM workflow_instances WHERE submitted_by = auth.uid()) to let submitters see all tasks on their instance. This crossed with the approver EXISTS in wf_instances_select (mig 129) — each policy querying the other table — causing PostgreSQL error 42P17 (infinite recursion).

Fix is on the instances side (mig 174): wf_instances_select was rebuilt using is_wf_task_assignee() SECURITY DEFINER, breaking the cycle. wf_tasks_select's submitter EXISTS subquery on workflow_instances is safe — it does not re-trigger wf_tasks_select because the chain only forms when both sides have an inline cross-reference simultaneously. See mig 174 for full analysis.
workflow_delegationsWorkflowTemporary delegation of approval authority. Guards against date overlaps.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
delegator_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ profiles.id. Person delegating their authority.
delegate_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ profiles.id. Person receiving authority.
start_date / end_datedateNOT NULL—Active window. Overlap guard enforced by DB constraint.
module_codetextnullableNULLNULL = all modules. Set to restrict to one module e.g. expense_report.
is_activebooleanNOT NULLtrueSoft-cancel without deleting
workflow_assignmentsWorkflowMaps employees to specific approvers for workflow routing overrides.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
employee_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ employees.id. The employee being routed.
approver_profile_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ profiles.id. Override approver.
module_codetextnullableNULLNULL = all modules. Specific = only that module.
created_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
sla_eventsWorkflowScheduled SLA breach events. Processed by pg_cron. Escalations triggered here.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
task_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ workflow_tasks.id. Which task's SLA this tracks.
due_attimestamptzNOT NULL—When the SLA window expires
processedbooleanNOT NULLfalseWhether pg_cron has processed this event
notificationsWorkflowUser-facing notification inbox. Created by workflow events and system actions.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
user_idFKIDXuuidNOT NULL—→ auth.users.id. Notification recipient.
type / title / bodytextNOT NULL—Notification content and category
read_attimestamptznullableNULLNULL = unread. Set when user opens notification.
metadatajsonbnullableNULLContextual payload e.g. {report_id, instance_id}
created_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
workflow_step_conditionsWorkflowConditional routing rules evaluated against instance metadata. When skip_step=true and all conditions match, the step is bypassed automatically.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
step_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ workflow_steps.id (CASCADE). The step this condition applies to.
field_pathtextNOT NULL—Key path in instance.metadata jsonb e.g. 'total_amount'
operatortextNOT NULL—CHECK: gt / gte / lt / lte / eq / neq / in / not_in
valuetextNOT NULL—Comparison value. Numeric comparisons cast at runtime.
skip_stepbooleanNOT NULLfalseWhen true: step is skipped if ALL conditions on it evaluate to true.
created_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
workflow_action_logWorkflowImmutable, append-only event log for every workflow state change. Never update or delete.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
instance_idFKIDXuuidNOT NULL—→ workflow_instances.id. Parent workflow instance.
task_idFKuuidnullableNULL→ workflow_tasks.id. NULL for instance-level events (submit, withdraw).
actor_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ profiles.id. Who performed the action.
actiontextNOT NULL—submitted / approved / rejected / reassigned / withdrawn / completed / cancelled
step_orderintegernullableNULLWhich step this action occurred on.
notestextnullableNULLApprover comments.
metadatajsonbnullableNULLExtra context snapshot at time of action.
created_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
workflow_notification_templatesWorkflowMustache-style message templates for workflow notifications. Placeholders resolved from queue payload at delivery time.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
codeUNIQUEtextNOT NULL—Template identifier e.g. 'wf.task_assigned'. UNIQUE.
title_tmpltextNOT NULL—Notification title with {{placeholder}} tokens.
body_tmpltextNOT NULL—Notification body with {{placeholder}} tokens.
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
workflow_notification_queueWorkflowOutbound notification staging table. Background job renders template + payload, delivers to notifications table, marks as sent.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
instance_idFKuuidNOT NULL—→ workflow_instances.id (CASCADE).
template_codetextNOT NULL—Matches workflow_notification_templates.code.
target_profileFKuuidNOT NULL—→ profiles.id. Notification recipient.
payloadjsonbNOT NULL{}Template variables e.g. {submitter_name, report_name}.
statustextNOT NULL'pending'CHECK: pending / sent / failed
error_messagetextnullableNULLPopulated on failure for diagnostics.
created_at / processed_attimestamptznullableNULLprocessed_at set when status → sent or failed.
workflow_assignment_auditWorkflowImmutable audit log of all workflow assignment changes. Written by trigger only. Tracks old vs new template and effective dates.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
assignment_idFKIDXuuidnullableNULL→ workflow_assignments.id. NULL if assignment was deleted.
module_code / assignment_typetextNOT NULL—What module and type of assignment changed.
actiontextNOT NULL—CHECK: INSERT / UPDATE / DEACTIVATE
old_template_id / new_template_idFKuuidnullableNULL→ workflow_templates.id. Before and after template.
old/new_effective_from/todatenullableNULLBefore and after effective date range.
changed_byFKuuidnullableNULL→ profiles.id. Who made the change.
changed_atIDXtimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
reasontextnullableNULLOptional reason for the change.
workflow_pending_changesWorkflowProposed changes for non-expense modules pending workflow approval. Stores the diff as proposed_data jsonb; applied on wf_approve.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
module_codeIDXtextNOT NULL—Which module this change belongs to e.g. 'employees'.
record_iduuidnullableNULLThe original record being changed. NULL for 'create' actions.
instance_idFKIDXuuidnullableNULL→ workflow_instances.id. Set after wf_submit(). Links to the driving workflow.
actiontextNOT NULL'update'CHECK: create / update / delete
proposed_datajsonbNOT NULL{}Full proposed field values to apply when approved.
current_datajsonbnullableNULLSnapshot of field values at submission time, filtered to proposed_data keys. Added mig 176. NULL for rows submitted before mig 176, create/delete actions, or modules with no satellite table. Used by Approver Inbox to render before/after diff (strikethrough old value).
submitted_byFKIDXuuidnullableNULL→ profiles.id. Who submitted the change request.
statustextNOT NULL'pending'CHECK: pending / approved / rejected / withdrawn
created_at / resolved_attimestamptznullableNULLresolved_at set when status → approved/rejected/withdrawn.

📊 Workflow Views 5 views

Read-only database views — no RLS (security enforced by the underlying table policies). Filterable via the search bar above.

vw_wf_pending_tasksViewTasks the current user (auth.uid()) needs to act on. Drives the Approver Inbox. Filtered by assigned_to = auth.uid().▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
task_id / instance_iduuid——workflow_tasks.id and workflow_instances.id
assigned_touuid——Always = auth.uid() (view filter)
step_name / template_name / template_codetext——Human-readable step and template info
module_code / record_id / metadatatext/uuid/jsonb——Source record context from workflow_instances
submitted_by_name / submitted_by_emailtext——Requester details joined from employees
due_at / task_created_attimestamptz——Timing fields for SLA display
sla_statustext——Computed: on_track / due_soon (within 4h) / overdue
vw_wf_my_requestsViewAll workflow instances submitted by the current user. Drives the My Requests panel. Includes current approver name.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
id / status / module_code / record_iduuid/text——Core instance fields from workflow_instances
template_code / template_nametext——Template display info joined from workflow_templates
current_stepinteger——Which step is currently active
submitted_at / updated_at / completed_attimestamptz——Lifecycle timestamps
current_approver_id / current_approver_nameuuid/text——Who is currently blocking the workflow (NULL if completed)
current_task_duetimestamptz——Due date of the current pending task
vw_wf_operationsViewSystem-wide pending tasks for workflow admins. Adds human-readable display_id, department, SLA classification, and overdue flags.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
task_id / instance_iduuid——workflow_tasks.id and workflow_instances.id
display_idtext——Computed: EXP-YYYYMMDD-XXXXXX. Prefix from module_code (EXP/LVE/TRV/PO/WF).
template_code / template_name / module_codetext——What kind of workflow this is
step_order / step_name / sla_hoursinteger/text——Current step details and configured SLA
assignee_id / assignee_name / assignee_job_titleuuid/text——Who is blocking the workflow
submitter_id / submitter_nameuuid/text——Who submitted the request
department_id / department_nameuuid/text——Submitter's department (for ops filtering)
submitted_attimestamptz——When the instance was created
sla_statustext——Computed: normal / overdue / critical (overdue AND past 1× SLA window)
vw_notification_monitorViewJoins notification queue with in-app and email delivery status. Used by the Notification Monitor UI to diagnose delivery failures.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
queue_id / notification_id / instance_iduuid——Identity from workflow_notification_queue
display_id / template_nametext——Human-readable instance ID and template code
recipient_id / recipient_name / recipient_email / recipient_depttext/uuid——Recipient details joined from profiles + employees
inapp_status / inapp_errortext——In-app delivery: pending / sent / failed + error message
email_status / email_sent_at / email_errortext/timestamptz——Email delivery status from notifications table
retry_count / max_retriesinteger——Delivery retry tracking
payloadjsonb——Full payload for diagnostics
overall_status / can_retrytext/bool——Computed: unified status and whether retry is possible
pending_invite_remindersViewEmployees whose invite was sent but not yet accepted. Polled by the sync job to fire reminder emails via signInWithOtp.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
employee_iduuid——employees.id
business_email / employee_nametext——Contact details for the reminder email
invite_iduuid——employee_invites.id
sent_at / reminder_sent_attimestamptz——When original invite and last reminder were sent
attempt_nointeger——How many reminder attempts have been made
Filter: status='sent' AND reminder_sent_at IS NOT NULL AND invite_accepted_at IS NULL

📋 Audit Domain 2 tables

audit_logAuditGeneral action log. Permission changes, login events, admin actions. Append-only.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
user_idFKuuidnullableNULL→ auth.users.id (SET NULL on delete). Who performed the action.
actiontextNOT NULL—Namespaced verb: user_role.assigned / permission_set_item.added / etc.
entity_typetextNOT NULL—Table name: user_roles / permission_set_assignments / etc.
entity_iduuidnullableNULLPK of the changed row (profile_id for user_roles; set_id for PSI)
metadatajsonbnullableNULLFull row snapshot at time of change
ip_addresstextnullableNULLClient IP (app layer populated)
created_attimestamptzNOT NULLnow()No updated_at — immutable by design
🔒 INSERT only via SECURITY DEFINER triggers · UPDATE/DELETE policy = false (immutable)
employee_audit_logAuditEmployee data change trail. INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on employees + 7 satellite tables.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
table_nametextNOT NULL—employees / employee_personal / employee_contact / etc.
record_iduuidNOT NULL—PK of the changed row
employee_idFKIDXuuidnullableNULL→ employees.id (SET NULL if deleted). Links satellite changes to the employee.
operationtextNOT NULL—CHECK IN ('INSERT','UPDATE','DELETE')
old_data / new_datajsonbnullableNULLFull row before/after. old_data=NULL on INSERT; new_data=NULL on DELETE.
changed_byFKIDXuuidnullableNULL→ auth.users.id (SET NULL). auth.uid() captured by SECURITY DEFINER trigger.
changed_atIDXtimestamptzNOT NULLnow()—
🔒 INSERT = SECURITY DEFINER triggers only (false for authenticated) · UPDATE/DELETE = never

⏱️ Jobs Domain 1 table

job_run_logJobsExecution history for background jobs (pg_cron). One row per job run.▸
FieldTypeNullDefaultDescription
idPKuuidNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()—
job_codeIDXtextNOT NULL—Machine name: sync_target_groups / process_sla_events / etc.
statustextNOT NULL—success / failed / running
started_atIDXtimestamptzNOT NULL—Job start time
finished_attimestamptznullableNULLNULL if still running or crashed
rows_affectedintegernullableNULLHow many records were processed
error_messagetextnullableNULLError detail when status=failed

Part 3 — ER Diagram (High-Level, by Domain)

One node per domain grouping. Lines show the primary FK relationships between domains. Rendered with Mermaid.js.

erDiagram AUTH_DOMAIN { uuid id PK text email } ORGANISATION_DOMAIN { uuid id PK uuid profile_id FK } EMPLOYEE_SATELLITE { uuid employee_id FK } PERMISSIONS_DOMAIN { uuid id PK uuid profile_id FK } REFERENCE_DOMAIN { uuid id PK } EXPENSE_DOMAIN { uuid id PK uuid employee_id FK } WORKFLOW_DOMAIN { uuid id PK text module_code } AUDIT_DOMAIN { uuid id PK } JOBS_DOMAIN { uuid id PK text job_code } AUTH_DOMAIN ||--o{ ORGANISATION_DOMAIN : "profile_id" AUTH_DOMAIN ||--o{ PERMISSIONS_DOMAIN : "profile_id" ORGANISATION_DOMAIN ||--o{ EMPLOYEE_SATELLITE : "employee_id" ORGANISATION_DOMAIN ||--o{ EXPENSE_DOMAIN : "employee_id" ORGANISATION_DOMAIN ||--o{ WORKFLOW_DOMAIN : "submitter_id" REFERENCE_DOMAIN ||--o{ EXPENSE_DOMAIN : "currency / project" WORKFLOW_DOMAIN ||--o{ AUDIT_DOMAIN : "workflow events" PERMISSIONS_DOMAIN ||--o{ AUDIT_DOMAIN : "permission changes" ORGANISATION_DOMAIN ||--o{ AUDIT_DOMAIN : "employee changes"

Domain Dependency Summary

From DomainTo DomainKey Column(s)Cardinality
Reference Domain
currencies user_can('exchange_rates_mgmt','view',NULL)
OR user_can('currencies','lookup',NULL)		 user_can('exchange_rates_mgmt','edit',NULL) user_can('exchange_rates_mgmt','edit',NULL) user_can('exchange_rates_mgmt','edit',NULL) Mgmt: mig 136. +Lookup policy: mig 147 (ESS granted). 🖥 exchange_rates_mgmt.view → "Exchange Rates" sidebar tab
📖 Exchange Rates admin tab permission — OR any ESS employee to see active currencies in dropdowns 📖 Exchange Rates admin only 📖 Exchange Rates admin only 📖 Exchange Rates admin only
exchange_rates user_can('exchange_rates_mgmt','view',NULL) user_can('exchange_rates_mgmt','edit',NULL) user_can('exchange_rates_mgmt','edit',NULL) user_can('exchange_rates_mgmt','edit',NULL) Shares gate with currencies (mig 136). No lookup view. 🖥 exchange_rates_mgmt.view → same "Exchange Rates" tab
📖 Exchange Rates admin tab permission. No lookup view — not used in dropdowns 📖 Exchange Rates admin only 📖 Exchange Rates admin only 📖 Exchange Rates admin only
picklists user_can('reference','view',NULL)
OR user_can('picklists','lookup',NULL)		 user_can('reference','edit',NULL) user_can('reference','edit',NULL) user_can('reference','edit',NULL) Mgmt: mig 137. +Lookup policy: mig 147 (ESS granted). 🖥 reference.view → "Reference Data" sidebar tab
📖 Reference Data admin tab permission — OR any ESS employee to access picklist categories for dropdowns 📖 Reference Data admin only 📖 Reference Data admin only 📖 Reference Data admin only
picklist_values user_can('reference','view',NULL)
OR user_can('picklists','lookup',NULL)		 user_can('reference','edit',NULL) user_can('reference','edit',NULL) user_can('reference','edit',NULL) Mgmt: mig 137. +Lookup policy mig 147 — same gate as picklists (SECURITY INVOKER JOIN). 🖥 reference.view → same "Reference Data" tab
📖 Same as picklists — Reference Data admin or ESS for dropdown lookup. Both must pass (SECURITY INVOKER JOIN) 📖 Reference Data admin only 📖 Reference Data admin only 📖 Reference Data admin only
projects user_can('projects_mgmt','view',NULL)
OR user_can('projects','lookup',NULL)		 user_can('projects_mgmt','edit',NULL) user_can('projects_mgmt','edit',NULL) user_can('projects_mgmt','edit',NULL) Mgmt: mig 138. +Lookup policy: mig 147 (ESS granted). 🖥 projects_mgmt.view → "Projects" sidebar tab
📖 Projects admin tab permission — OR any ESS employee to see active projects for expense line item coding 📖 Projects admin only 📖 Projects admin only 📖 Projects admin only
↳ Lookup Views (mig 147 — SECURITY INVOKER, read-only)
vw_projects_lookup SECURITY INVOKER →
user_can('projects','lookup',NULL)
(base table RLS fires)		 ❌ Read-only view ❌ Read-only view ❌ Read-only view mig 147. WHERE active=true. Exposes id, name, start_date, end_date. ⚠️ projects.lookup alone does NOT open the Projects sidebar tab
📖 Any ESS employee — base table projects_select_lookup policy enforces it 📖 Not allowed — views are read-only 📖 Not allowed — views are read-only 📖 Not allowed — views are read-only
vw_currencies_lookup SECURITY INVOKER →
user_can('currencies','lookup',NULL)
(base table RLS fires)		 ❌ Read-only view ❌ Read-only view ❌ Read-only view mig 147. WHERE active=true. Exposes id, code, name, symbol. ⚠️ currencies.lookup alone does NOT open the Exchange Rates tab
📖 Any ESS employee — base table currencies_select_lookup policy enforces it 📖 Not allowed — views are read-only 📖 Not allowed — views are read-only 📖 Not allowed — views are read-only
vw_picklist_values_lookup SECURITY INVOKER →
user_can('picklists','lookup',NULL)
(RLS on picklists AND picklist_values)		 ❌ Read-only view ❌ Read-only view ❌ Read-only view mig 147. WHERE pv.active=true. Exposes id, picklist_code, value, ref_id, parent_value_id
📖 Any ESS employee — both picklists and picklist_values lookup policies must pass (this view JOINs them) 📖 Not allowed — views are read-only 📖 Not allowed — views are read-only 📖 Not allowed — views are read-only
vw_departments_lookup SECURITY INVOKER →
user_can('departments','lookup',NULL)
(base table RLS fires)		 ❌ Read-only view ❌ Read-only view ❌ Read-only view mig 147. WHERE deleted_at IS NULL. Exposes id, dept_id, name. ⚠️ departments.lookup alone does NOT open the Department tab
📖 Any ESS employee — base table departments_select_lookup policy enforces it 📖 Not allowed — views are read-only 📖 Not allowed — views are read-only 📖 Not allowed — views are read-only
🖥 Sidebar tab mapping: "Projects" tab → projects_mgmt.view  |  "Reference Data" tab → reference.view  |  "Exchange Rates" tab → exchange_rates_mgmt.view. The .lookup permissions (mig 147) only unlock transactional dropdown views for ESS employees — they never open admin sidebar tabs.
📋 Reference Lookup permissions (departments.lookup, picklists.lookup, projects.lookup, currencies.lookup) control data visibility throughout the entire application — not just a single screen. An ESS employee without departments.lookup sees an empty department dropdown everywhere in the app.
Organisation Domain
departments user_can('departments','view',NULL)
OR user_can('departments','lookup',NULL)		 user_can('departments','edit',NULL) user_can('departments','edit',NULL) user_can('departments','edit',NULL) Mig 134 (user_can). +Lookup policy: mig 147 (ESS granted). 🖥 departments.view → "Manage Department" in Permission Matrix
📖 Department admin tab permission — OR any ESS employee to see departments in dropdowns 📖 Department admin only 📖 Department admin only 📖 Department admin only
department_heads user_can('departments','view',NULL) user_can('departments','edit',NULL) user_can('departments','edit',NULL) user_can('departments','edit',NULL) Mig 134: SELECT uses same departments.view gate. Historical record — one active head at a time
📖 Same gate as departments — department admin only. No lookup view needed (not used in ESS dropdowns) 📖 Department admin only 📖 Department admin only 📖 Department admin only
employees deleted_at IS NULL AND (
status='Active' AND user_can('employee_details','view',id)
OR status='Inactive' AND user_can('inactive_employees','view',id)
OR status IN ('Draft','Incomplete') AND user_can('hire_employee','view',id)
)		 user_can('hire_employee','create',NULL) user_can('personal_info','edit',id)
OR status='Active' AND user_can('employee_details','edit',id)
OR status='Active' AND user_can('inactive_employees','create',id)
OR status='Inactive' AND user_can('inactive_employees','edit',id)
OR status IN ('Draft','Incomplete') AND user_can('hire_employee','edit',id)		 status='Active' AND user_can('employee_details','delete',id)
OR status='Inactive' AND user_can('inactive_employees','delete',id)
OR status IN ('Draft','Incomplete') AND user_can('hire_employee','delete',id)		 Active → "Employee master" Delete. Inactive → "Inactive Employees" Delete. Pipeline → "Hire Employee" Delete

Step 4 in get_workflow_participants() — queried to get full_name + job_title for each approver displayed in the WorkflowSubmitModal preview.
📖 Fully permission-driven — no hardcoding (mig 109 removed id=get_my_employee_id() bypass). ESS employees see their own record via employee_details.view scoped to "self" (user_can Path C). "Employee master" UI = employee_details module 📖 Fully controlled by the Permission Matrix — no hardcoding. Active employees: "Employee master" View. Inactive: "Inactive Employees" View. Pipeline (Draft/Incomplete): "Hire Employee" View. Scope (target group) controls who you can see — a manager only sees their team 📖 Only users with "Hire Employee" Create permission can add new employees 📖 All edits through the matrix — no own-record shortcut. Own profile fields (avatar etc.) → "Personal Info" edit. Active employees → "Employee master" edit. Deactivate → "Inactive Employees" create. Reactivate → "Inactive Employees" edit. Pipeline → "Hire Employee" edit
Employee Satellite Domain
employee_personal user_can('personal_info','view',employee_id) user_can('personal_info','edit',employee_id) user_can('personal_info','edit',employee_id) user_can('personal_info','edit',employee_id) Module: personal_info. name, gender, DOB, marital status — highly personal fields. Path C fires for own employee_id — ESS sees own data. (mig 109)
📖 "Personal Info" View permission in Permission Matrix — scoped to self for ESS (you see your own), or broader scope for HR/admin 📖 "Personal Info" Edit permission in Permission Matrix 📖 "Personal Info" Edit permission in Permission Matrix 📖 "Personal Info" Edit permission in Permission Matrix
employee_contact user_can('contact_info','view',employee_id) user_can('contact_info','edit',employee_id) user_can('contact_info','edit',employee_id) user_can('contact_info','edit',employee_id) Module: contact_info. work email, personal email, phone numbers. Path C fires for own employee_id — ESS sees own data. (mig 109)
📖 "Contact" View permission in Permission Matrix — scoped to self for ESS (you see your own), or broader scope for HR/admin 📖 "Contact" Edit permission in Permission Matrix 📖 "Contact" Edit permission in Permission Matrix 📖 "Contact" Edit permission in Permission Matrix
employee_employment user_can('employment','view',employee_id) user_can('employment','edit',employee_id) user_can('employment','edit',employee_id) user_can('employment','edit',employee_id) Module: employment. job title, department, employment type, start date. Path C fires for own employee_id — ESS sees own data. (mig 109)
📖 "Employment" View permission in Permission Matrix — scoped to self for ESS (you see your own), or broader scope for HR/admin 📖 "Employment" Edit permission in Permission Matrix 📖 "Employment" Edit permission in Permission Matrix 📖 "Employment" Edit permission in Permission Matrix
employee_addresses user_can('address','view',employee_id) user_can('address','edit',employee_id) user_can('address','edit',employee_id) user_can('address','edit',employee_id) Module: address. home, postal and work addresses. Path C fires for own employee_id — ESS sees own data. (mig 109)
📖 "Address" View permission in Permission Matrix — scoped to self for ESS (you see your own), or broader scope for HR/admin 📖 "Address" Edit permission in Permission Matrix 📖 "Address" Edit permission in Permission Matrix 📖 "Address" Edit permission in Permission Matrix
emergency_contacts user_can('emergency_contacts','view',employee_id) user_can('emergency_contacts','edit',employee_id) user_can('emergency_contacts','edit',employee_id) user_can('emergency_contacts','edit',employee_id) Module: emergency_contacts. next-of-kin contacts — multiple per employee. Path C fires for own employee_id — ESS sees own data. (mig 109)
📖 "Emergency Contacts" View permission in Permission Matrix — scoped to self for ESS (you see your own), or broader scope for HR/admin 📖 "Emergency Contacts" Edit permission in Permission Matrix 📖 "Emergency Contacts" Edit permission in Permission Matrix 📖 "Emergency Contacts" Edit permission in Permission Matrix
identity_records user_can('identity_documents','view',employee_id) user_can('identity_documents','edit',employee_id) user_can('identity_documents','edit',employee_id) user_can('identity_documents','edit',employee_id) Module: identity_documents. PAN, Aadhaar, SSN and other government IDs — highly sensitive. Path C fires for own employee_id — ESS sees own data. (mig 109)
📖 "Identity Docs" View permission in Permission Matrix — scoped to self for ESS (you see your own), or broader scope for HR/admin 📖 "Identity Docs" Edit permission in Permission Matrix 📖 "Identity Docs" Edit permission in Permission Matrix 📖 "Identity Docs" Edit permission in Permission Matrix
passports user_can('passport','view',employee_id) user_can('passport','edit',employee_id) user_can('passport','edit',employee_id) user_can('passport','edit',employee_id) Module: passport. passport number, issuing country, expiry, visa info. Path C fires for own employee_id — ESS sees own data. (mig 109)
📖 "Passport" View permission in Permission Matrix — scoped to self for ESS (you see your own), or broader scope for HR/admin 📖 "Passport" Edit permission in Permission Matrix 📖 "Passport" Edit permission in Permission Matrix 📖 "Passport" Edit permission in Permission Matrix
Auth Domain
profiles id = auth.uid() OR has_role('admin') OR (employee_id IS NOT NULL AND user_can('employee_details','view',employee_id)) Only via handle_new_user() trigger id = auth.uid() OR has_role('admin') ❌ Never Auth table — created by handle_new_user() trigger. profile.employee_id links to employees table.

SELECT broadened in mig 178: users who can see an employee via employee_details.view can also read that employee's profile row (needed for Approver Inbox Reassign typeahead and any feature that needs a profile_id from an employee search).

Step 3 in get_workflow_participants() — queried to resolve profile-type approver IDs into display names for the WorkflowSubmitModal approver preview.
📖 Own profile always readable. Admins read all. Any user with employee_details.view on a target employee can also read that employee's profile row — mirrors employees_select (mig 178) 📖 Created automatically when you sign up — never inserted manually 📖 You can always edit your own profile. Only super admins can update other profiles 📖 Profiles are never deleted — they persist even if the employee is deactivated
super_admins is_super_admin() only service_role only service_role only service_role only Immutable from app layer. Super admin list is managed directly in the database — not through the UI
📖 Only super admins can see who else is a super admin 📖 Cannot be done from the app — service role only (direct DB access) 📖 Cannot be done from the app — service role only 📖 Cannot be done from the app — service role only
Permissions Domain
modules ✅ Any authenticated user service_role only service_role only service_role only Read-only reference — modules are seeded by migrations
📖 Any logged-in user can read the module list — it is read-only reference data that drives the UI 📖 System only — modules are seeded by migrations, never created from the app 📖 System only 📖 System only
permissions ✅ Any authenticated user service_role only service_role only service_role only Read-only reference — permissions are seeded by migrations
📖 Any logged-in user can read the permissions catalogue — the Permission Matrix UI uses this to build its rows 📖 System only — permissions are seeded by migrations, never created from the app 📖 System only 📖 System only
roles auth.uid() IS NOT NULL AND active = true user_can('sec_role_assignments','edit',NULL) user_can('sec_role_assignments','edit',NULL) user_can('sec_role_assignments','edit',NULL) Role definitions. SELECT open to all authenticated users (Permission Matrix UI needs to list roles). Write gated by Role assignments (sec_role_assignments.edit) in Permission Matrix
📖 Any logged-in user can see the list of active roles — the app needs this to render role dropdowns 📖 Only users with "Role assignments" permission in the Permission Matrix 📖 Only users with "Role assignments" permission 📖 Only users with "Role assignments" permission
user_roles profile_id = auth.uid()
OR user_can('sec_role_assignments','view',NULL)		 user_can('sec_role_assignments','edit',NULL) user_can('sec_role_assignments','edit',NULL) user_can('sec_role_assignments','edit',NULL) Audit trigger fires on all DML — every change is logged
📖 Security admins with "Assign Access" permission — OR you can always see your own role assignments ✅ Any authenticated user is_super_admin() OR user_can('sec_permission_matrix','edit',NULL) is_super_admin() OR user_can('sec_permission_matrix','edit',NULL) is_super_admin() OR user_can('sec_permission_matrix','edit',NULL)
permission_sets user_can('security','manage_roles') user_can('security','manage_roles') user_can('security','manage_roles') user_can('security','manage_roles') Permission set definitions — e.g. ESS, Admin, Finance, HR
📖 Any logged-in user can see the list of active roles — the app needs this to render role dropdowns 📖 Only users with "Role assignments" permission in the Permission Matrix 📖 Only users with "Role assignments" permission 📖 Only users with "Role assignments" permission
permission_set_items ✅ Any authenticated user is_super_admin() OR user_can('sec_permission_matrix','edit',NULL) ❌ No UPDATE — delete + insert to change is_super_admin() OR user_can('sec_permission_matrix','edit',NULL) No UPDATE policy by design — prevents partial edits. Delete + insert is the only change path
📖 Security admins only — these are the individual permission checkboxes inside a permission set 📖 Security admins only — ticking a checkbox in the Permission Matrix inserts a row here 📖 Not allowed — to change a permission, delete the row and re-insert 📖 Security admins only — unticking a checkbox in the Permission Matrix deletes a row here
permission_set_assignments ✅ Any authenticated user is_super_admin() OR user_can('sec_permission_matrix','edit',NULL) ❌ No UPDATE — delete + re-assign to change scope or target group is_super_admin() OR user_can('sec_permission_matrix','edit',NULL) Audit trigger fires on all DML. No UPDATE by design. Gated by Permission matrix (sec_permission_matrix.edit)
📖 Security admins only — these are the assignments of a permission set to an employee (with target group and scope) 📖 Security admins only — assigning a permission set to an employee creates a row here 📖 Not allowed — delete and re-assign to change scope or target group 📖 Security admins only — revoking a permission set deletes the row here
target_groups ✅ Any authenticated user user_can('sec_target_groups','edit',NULL) user_can('sec_target_groups','edit',NULL) user_can('sec_target_groups','edit',NULL) AND NOT is_system Target group definitions. SELECT open to all — user_can() Path D reads group membership. Write gated by Target groups (sec_target_groups.edit). System groups (is_system=true) cannot be deleted
📖 Any authenticated user — view-only admins see groups in read mode; needed internally by user_can() Path D 📖 Edit admins only (sec_target_groups.edit) — create a new custom target group 📖 Edit admins only — update group label or criteria 📖 Edit admins only — system groups (Everyone, Self) cannot be deleted even with edit permission
target_group_members ✅ Any authenticated user Managed by sync job (service_role) ❌ No UPDATE Managed by sync job (service_role) Membership is kept in sync automatically — adding an employee to a department auto-updates their group membership
📖 Any authenticated user — needed by user_can() Path D to resolve group membership; view-only admins can read the criteria 📖 Automatically managed by a background sync job — not inserted manually from the app 📖 Not allowed — sync job manages membership 📖 Automatically managed by a background sync job — not deleted manually from the app
Expense Domain
expense_reports user_can('expense_reports','view',employee_id) user_can('expense_reports','create',employee_id) user_can('expense_reports','edit',employee_id) AND (self OR workflow approver) user_can('expense_reports','delete',employee_id) AND self AND status='draft' Fully Permission Matrix controlled. Path C (self) and Path D (broader scope) via user_can(). Status progression: Draft → Submitted → Approved/Rejected
📖 Employees see their own reports (Path C). Broader access (e.g. finance/manager scope) granted via Permission Matrix 📖 Requires expense_reports.create permission — granted via Permission Matrix to employees who can submit expenses 📖 Requires expense_reports.edit — self can edit own draft; workflow approvers can edit in-flight reports if step allows it 📖 Requires expense_reports.delete — self only, draft only. Submitted reports cannot be deleted
line_items Via parent expense report RLS Via parent report (draft only) Via parent report Via parent report (draft only) No independent RLS — inherits entirely from parent expense_reports policy
📖 Access follows the parent expense report — if you can see the report, you can see all its line items 📖 You can add line items to your own draft expense reports 📖 Access follows the parent report 📖 You can delete line items from your own draft reports only
attachments Via parent expense report RLS Via parent report (draft only) ❌ No UPDATE Via parent report (draft only) No independent RLS — inherits from parent expense_reports policy
📖 Access follows the parent expense report — if you can see the report, you can see its attachments 📖 You can attach files to your own draft expense reports 📖 Attachments cannot be edited — delete and re-upload to replace 📖 You can remove attachments from your own draft reports only
Workflow Domain
workflow_templates user_can('wf_templates','view') user_can('wf_templates','edit') user_can('wf_templates','edit') user_can('wf_templates','edit') UNIQUE(code) WHERE is_active — only one active template per code at a time
📖 wf_templates.view permission required — templates are admin-only resources 📖 wf_templates.edit only — templates define the approval flow structure 📖 wf_templates.edit only 📖 wf_templates.edit only
workflow_steps user_can('wf_templates','view') user_can('wf_templates','edit') user_can('wf_templates','edit') user_can('wf_templates','edit') Steps belong to a template — ordered sequence of approver stages. ⚠ ESS users cannot SELECT directly (mig 153). WorkflowSubmitModal preview served via get_workflow_participants() SECURITY DEFINER RPC (mig 166).

Step 2 in get_workflow_participants() — queries steps for the resolved template. This is the table ESS users are blocked on; the RPC bypasses this via SECURITY DEFINER.
📖 wf_templates.view required — template steps are admin-only config. ESS users access the approver preview via get_workflow_participants() RPC instead (mig 166) 📖 wf_templates.edit only — steps are the individual approver stages within a template 📖 wf_templates.edit only 📖 wf_templates.edit only
workflow_instances Submitter OR approvers OR wf_manage Via wf_submit() RPC only Via wf_approve/reject/cancel RPCs only user_can('workflow','admin') Unique: only 1 in_progress instance per record at a time. Status machine: in_progress → approved/rejected/cancelled. Users only see their own instances; approvers see only what is assigned to them; no data can be created or changed except through the controlled RPC functions.
📖 You see instances where you are the submitter or an assigned approver. Workflow managers see all 📖 Created automatically when you submit an expense report — never inserted directly 📖 Updated only through approve, reject or cancel actions — never edited directly 📖 Workflow admins only — instances are rarely deleted
workflow_tasks Assigned user OR submitter OR user_can('wf_manage','view') Via wf_submit() RPC only Via wf_approve() RPC only user_can('workflow','admin') Tasks are the individual to-do items that land in an approver's queue
📖 You see tasks assigned to you or instances you submitted. Workflow Managers (wf_manage.view) see all tasks 📖 Created automatically when a workflow instance moves to your approval step 📖 Updated automatically when you approve or reject — not edited directly 📖 Workflow admins only
wf_sla_events user_can('wf_performance','view',NULL) OR assignee Triggers / service_role Triggers / service_role user_can('wf_performance','view',NULL) SLA breach events — used for reporting and escalation monitoring. Flow: step goes overdue → trigger fires OR scheduled job detects it → INSERT wf_sla_events (breach recorded) → step eventually completed → UPDATE wf_sla_events (resolved, duration logged) → wf_performance.view users see it on dashboards.
📖 Performance admins (wf_performance.view) see all; assignee of the related task sees their own SLA events 📖 Written automatically by database triggers when deadlines are breached 📖 Updated automatically by system processes 📖 Workflow admins only
wf_delegations (delegator AND wf_inbox.view) OR delegate OR wf_delegations.view (delegator_id = auth.uid() AND wf_inbox.view) OR wf_delegations.edit (delegator_id = auth.uid() AND wf_inbox.view) OR wf_delegations.edit (delegator_id = auth.uid() AND wf_inbox.view) OR wf_delegations.edit Out-of-office delegation — redirects approval tasks to a substitute. Self-service requires wf_inbox.view (approvers only). Delegate (passive recipient) always sees rows covering them. Admin edit required for cross-user operations. Path B — NULL target, no target population.
📖 Approvers see their own delegations; delegates see rows where they cover; Manage Delegations admins see all 📖 Approvers (wf_inbox.view) can create their own delegation. Admins (wf_delegations.edit) can create for anyone 📖 Approvers can edit their own delegations. Admins (wf_delegations.edit) can edit any 📖 Approvers can cancel their own delegations. Admins (wf_delegations.edit) can delete any
notifications Own notifications only Triggers / service_role Own (mark read only) Own notifications only Personal notification inbox — created by system triggers, managed by the user
📖 You can only see your own notifications — nobody else can see them 📖 Created automatically by workflow triggers when an action requires your attention 📖 You can mark your own notifications as read — no other updates allowed 📖 You can dismiss your own notifications
workflow_step_conditions user_can('wf_templates','view') user_can('wf_templates','edit') user_can('wf_templates','edit') user_can('wf_templates','edit') Conditional routing rules attached to workflow steps
📖 Access follows template view permission — wf_templates.view required to see conditions 📖 wf_templates.edit only — conditions define when a step is skipped or escalated 📖 wf_templates.edit only 📖 wf_templates.edit only
workflow_action_log user_can('wf_logs','view') Via wf_submit/approve/reject RPCs only ❌ Never ❌ Never Append-only audit log by design — provides a permanent record of every workflow action
📖 Users with workflow log view permission — used for audit trails of who approved what 📖 Written automatically when a workflow action (submit/approve/reject) is performed 📖 Never — the log is permanent and immutable 📖 Never — the log is permanent and immutable
workflow_notification_templates user_can('wf_notification_config','view') user_can('wf_notification_config','edit') user_can('wf_notification_config','edit') user_can('wf_notification_config','edit') Email and in-app notification content templates. Engine functions are SECURITY DEFINER — they bypass RLS for delivery.
📖 wf_notification_config.view required — Manage Notifications page access. Engine reads via SECURITY DEFINER, bypassing RLS. 📖 wf_notification_config.edit only — templates define the notification content and format 📖 wf_notification_config.edit only 📖 wf_notification_config.edit only
workflow_notification_queue user_can('wf_notifications','view') Triggers / service_role service_role (retry processor) ❌ Never Outbound notification delivery queue — processed by a background job
📖 Users with workflow notification view permission — used to monitor the delivery queue 📖 Added automatically when a notification needs to be sent 📖 Updated by the background retry processor if delivery fails 📖 Never deleted — queue entries are kept for audit purposes
