workflow_assignments auth.uid() IS NOT NULL user_can('wf_assignments','edit',NULL) user_can('wf_assignments','edit',NULL) user_can('wf_assignments','edit',NULL) Maps a workflow template to a module/entity — controls which template runs for which employee during submission. SELECT is open to all authenticated users so submission can resolve the correct template. Write gated by Manage Workflow Assignments (wf_assignments.edit)

Step 1 in get_workflow_participants() — finds the active template for the given module code.
📖 Any authenticated user — needed during workflow submission to resolve the template assigned to an employee 📖 Workflow Admin only (wf_assignments.edit) — creates a new template-to-entity routing rule 📖 Workflow Admin only — updates assignment details (e.g. priority, effective dates) 📖 Workflow Admin only — removes a routing rule
workflow_assignment_audit user_can('wf_assignments','view') Trigger only (wa_audit_trigger_fn) ❌ Never ❌ Never Every time an admin assigns or removes a workflow template from a module, this table silently records who did it, when, and what changed. It's the accountability layer for template assignment management. wf_assignments.view gates both the live assignments screen and this audit log.
📖 Users with workflow assignment view permission — used to see a history of who was assigned what 📖 Written automatically by the assignment audit trigger — never inserted manually 📖 Never — immutable audit record 📖 Never — immutable audit record
workflow_pending_changes submitted_by = auth.uid() OR user_can('wf_manage','view',NULL) OR EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM workflow_tasks wt WHERE wt.instance_id = workflow_pending_changes.instance_id AND wt.assigned_to = auth.uid()) user_can('wf_manage','edit') user_can('wf_manage','edit') user_can('workflow','admin') Change requests awaiting workflow approval — e.g. employee data changes requiring manager sign-off
📖 Submitter sees own requests. Assigned approvers see instances they are tasked on (mig 157). Workflow managers see all. (mig 157) 📖 Hardened in mig 160 — only users with wf_manage.edit permission can create pending change requests 📖 Users with workflow manage edit permission can update pending changes 📖 Workflow admin can delete / cancel pending change records
Views: vw_wf_pending_tasks, vw_wf_my_requests, vw_wf_operations, vw_notification_monitor, pending_invite_reminders — no RLS on views; security enforced by underlying table policies + WHERE auth.uid() filters built into the view definitions.
Audit Domain
audit_log user_id = auth.uid() OR user_can('sys_audit_log','view',NULL) OR (user_can('sec_role_assignments','view',NULL) AND entity_type IN ('user_roles','permission_set_assignments','permission_set_items')) SECURITY DEFINER triggers only ❌ Never ❌ Never Immutable by design — provides a tamper-proof record of system-level changes
📖 Your own activity always. sys_audit_log.view = full access. sec_role_assignments.view = scoped to role/permission entity types only (mig 159 fix) 📖 Written automatically by SECURITY DEFINER database triggers — never inserted manually from the app 📖 Never — audit logs are permanent and immutable by design 📖 Never — audit logs are permanent and immutable by design
employee_audit_log table_name-routed: user_can('employee_details'|'inactive_employees'|'hire_employee','history',employee_id) for employees rows (by status); user_can('<module>','history',employee_id) for each satellite table SECURITY DEFINER triggers only (WITH CHECK false) ❌ Never ❌ Never Immutable by design — every change to employee records is permanently logged here. Access is module-scoped and status-routed. No self-service — employees cannot see their own audit trail directly (use get_record_history() RPC instead).
📖 History is module-scoped. Active employee rows → employee_details.history. Inactive → inactive_employees.history. Draft → hire_employee.history. Each satellite table (personal_info, employment, contact, etc.) routes to its own module.history permission. No blanket access — you only see history for modules you have history permission on 📖 Written automatically by SECURITY DEFINER triggers whenever employee data changes 📖 Never — audit logs are permanent and immutable 📖 Never — audit logs are permanent and immutable
Jobs Domain
job_run_log user_can('jobs_manage','view',NULL) SECURITY DEFINER job functions only (no RLS INSERT policy) SECURITY DEFINER job functions only ❌ Never Scheduled job execution log — pg_cron writes here when jobs run via SECURITY DEFINER functions
📖 jobs_manage.view holders only — for monitoring scheduled job health and execution history (mig 141 upgraded from legacy admin role) 📖 Written automatically by SECURITY DEFINER job functions — no RLS INSERT policy for authenticated users 📖 Updated by SECURITY DEFINER job functions to record job completion or failure 📖 Job logs are never deleted — kept permanently for audit purposes

Part 4 — ER Diagram (Detailed, Table-Level)

Every production table shown. PK and key FK columns listed. Scroll horizontally if needed. The diagram is grouped by domain using subgraphs.

erDiagram currencies { uuid id PK text iso_code UK text name text symbol } picklists { uuid id PK text code UK text name } picklist_values { uuid id PK uuid picklist_id FK uuid parent_id FK text value } projects { uuid id PK text code UK text name boolean is_active } exchange_rates { uuid id PK uuid from_currency_id FK uuid to_currency_id FK date effective_date numeric rate } departments { uuid id PK text name text code UK uuid parent_id FK } department_heads { uuid id PK uuid department_id FK uuid employee_id FK date from_date date to_date } employees { uuid id PK text business_email UK text employee_number UK uuid department_id FK uuid manager_id FK uuid currency_id FK text status } employee_personal { uuid employee_id PK text nationality text marital_status } employee_contact { uuid employee_id PK text mobile text personal_email } employee_employment { uuid employee_id PK text designation text job_title date hire_date } employee_addresses { uuid id PK uuid employee_id FK text address_type text city text country } emergency_contacts { uuid id PK uuid employee_id FK text contact_name text phone } identity_records { uuid id PK uuid employee_id FK text id_type text id_number } passports { uuid id PK uuid employee_id FK text passport_number date expiry_date } profiles { uuid id PK uuid employee_id FK text full_name text avatar_url } super_admins { uuid profile_id PK } modules { uuid id PK text code UK text name } permissions { uuid id PK uuid module_id FK text code UK text action } roles { uuid id PK text code UK text name } user_roles { uuid profile_id PK uuid role_id PK boolean is_active text assignment_source } permission_sets { uuid id PK text name } permission_set_items { uuid permission_set_id PK uuid permission_id PK } permission_set_assignments { uuid id PK uuid permission_set_id FK uuid role_id FK uuid target_group_id FK } target_groups { uuid id PK text name text scope_type boolean is_system } target_group_members { uuid id PK uuid group_id FK uuid member_id FK } expense_reports { uuid id PK uuid employee_id FK text status date report_date } line_items { uuid id PK uuid report_id FK uuid currency_id FK uuid project_id FK numeric amount } attachments { uuid id PK uuid report_id FK uuid line_item_id FK text file_url } workflow_templates { uuid id PK text code UK int version boolean is_active } workflow_steps { uuid id PK uuid template_id FK int step_order text approver_type } workflow_instances { uuid id PK uuid template_id FK text module_code uuid submitter_id FK text status } workflow_tasks { uuid id PK uuid instance_id FK uuid assigned_to FK text status } wf_sla_events { uuid id PK uuid instance_id FK text event_type } wf_delegations { uuid id PK uuid delegator_id FK uuid delegate_id FK } notifications { uuid id PK uuid user_id FK text type text title } workflow_step_conditions { uuid id PK uuid step_id FK text field_path text operator text value boolean skip_step } workflow_action_log { uuid id PK uuid instance_id FK uuid task_id FK uuid actor_id FK text action } workflow_notification_templates { uuid id PK text code UK text title_tmpl text body_tmpl } workflow_notification_queue { uuid id PK uuid instance_id FK text template_code uuid target_profile FK text status jsonb payload } workflow_assignment_audit { uuid id PK uuid assignment_id FK text module_code text action uuid old_template_id FK uuid new_template_id FK uuid changed_by FK } workflow_pending_changes { uuid id PK text module_code uuid record_id uuid instance_id FK text action jsonb proposed_data text status } audit_log { uuid id PK uuid user_id FK text action text entity_type } employee_audit_log { uuid id PK uuid employee_id FK uuid changed_by FK text operation } job_run_log { uuid id PK text job_code text status timestamptz started_at } employee_dependents { uuid id PK text dependent_code UK uuid employee_id FK text relationship_type text dependent_name date date_of_birth date effective_from date effective_to boolean is_active } employee_dependent_attachments { uuid id PK text dependent_code uuid employee_id FK uuid dependent_id FK text document_type text file_name text file_path } currencies ||--o{ exchange_rates : "from_currency" currencies ||--o{ exchange_rates : "to_currency" currencies ||--o{ employees : "currency" currencies ||--o{ line_items : "currency" picklists ||--o{ picklist_values : "picklist_id" picklist_values ||--o{ picklist_values : "parent_id" projects ||--o{ line_items : "project_id" departments ||--o{ departments : "parent_id" departments ||--o{ employees : "department_id" departments ||--o{ department_heads : "department_id" employees ||--o{ department_heads : "employee_id" employees ||--o{ employees : "manager_id" employees ||--|| employee_personal : "employee_id" employees ||--|| employee_contact : "employee_id" employees ||--|| employee_employment : "employee_id" employees ||--o{ employee_addresses : "employee_id" employees ||--o{ emergency_contacts : "employee_id" employees ||--o{ identity_records : "employee_id" employees ||--o{ passports : "employee_id" employees ||--o{ employee_dependents : "employee_id" employees ||--o{ employee_dependent_attachments : "employee_id" employee_dependents ||--o{ employee_dependent_attachments : "dependent_id" employees ||--|| profiles : "employee_id" employees ||--o{ expense_reports : "employee_id" profiles ||--o{ user_roles : "profile_id" profiles ||--o| super_admins : "profile_id" profiles ||--o{ workflow_instances : "submitter_id" profiles ||--o{ workflow_tasks : "assigned_to" profiles ||--o{ wf_delegations : "delegator_id" profiles ||--o{ wf_delegations : "delegate_id" modules ||--o{ permissions : "module_id" permissions ||--o{ permission_set_items : "permission_id" roles ||--o{ user_roles : "role_id" roles ||--o{ permission_set_assignments : "role_id" permission_sets ||--o{ permission_set_items : "permission_set_id" permission_sets ||--o{ permission_set_assignments : "permission_set_id" target_groups ||--o{ permission_set_assignments : "target_group_id" target_groups ||--o{ target_group_members : "group_id" expense_reports ||--o{ line_items : "report_id" expense_reports ||--o{ attachments : "report_id" line_items ||--o{ attachments : "line_item_id" workflow_templates ||--o{ workflow_steps : "template_id" workflow_templates ||--o{ workflow_instances : "template_id" workflow_instances ||--o{ workflow_tasks : "instance_id" workflow_instances ||--o{ wf_sla_events : "instance_id" workflow_instances ||--o{ workflow_action_log : "instance_id" workflow_instances ||--o{ workflow_notification_queue : "instance_id" workflow_instances ||--o{ workflow_pending_changes : "instance_id" workflow_steps ||--o{ workflow_step_conditions : "step_id" workflow_tasks ||--o{ workflow_action_log : "task_id" workflow_templates ||--o{ workflow_assignment_audit : "old_template_id" workflow_templates ||--o{ workflow_assignment_audit : "new_template_id" profiles ||--o{ workflow_action_log : "actor_id" profiles ||--o{ workflow_notification_queue : "target_profile" profiles ||--o{ workflow_assignment_audit : "changed_by" profiles ||--o{ workflow_pending_changes : "submitted_by" audit_log }o--|| profiles : "user_id" employee_audit_log }o--|| employees : "employee_id"

Part 5 — Row-Level Security Policies

Every table's RLS posture: who can SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, and which function gates each operation. All policies call user_can() or is_super_admin() — never raw role checks.

user_can() path → Path B p_owner=NULL — permission existence only, no scope. Admin/config tables. Path D p_owner=employee_id — scope-aware: self / direct_l1 / direct_l2 / dept / country. No user_can() Direct auth check — no permission matrix involved.
TableSELECTINSERTUPDATEDELETENotes
Reference Domain

Path B		 ⚙ POLICY

OR user_can('currencies','lookup',NULL)		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('exchange_rates_mgmt','edit',NULL)		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('exchange_rates_mgmt','edit',NULL)		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('exchange_rates_mgmt','edit',NULL)		 Mgmt: mig 136. +Lookup mig 147 (ESS). 🖥 exchange_rates_mgmt.view

departments
Path B		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('departments','view',NULL)
OR user_can('departments','lookup',NULL)		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('departments','edit',NULL)		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('departments','edit',NULL)		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('departments','edit',NULL)		 Mig 134. +Lookup mig 147 (ESS). 🖥 departments.view

Path B		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('departments','view',NULL)		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('departments','edit',NULL)		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('departments','edit',NULL)		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('departments','edit',NULL)		 Mig 134. Historical record — one active head at a time.

employees
Path D		 ⚙ POLICY
deleted_at IS NULL AND (
  status='Active' AND user_can('employee_details','view',id)
  OR status='Inactive' AND user_can('inactive_employees','view',id)
  OR status IN ('Draft','Incomplete') AND user_can('hire_employee','view',id))		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('hire_employee','create',NULL)		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('personal_info','edit',id)
OR user_can('employee_details','edit',id)
OR user_can('inactive_employees','create/edit',id)
OR user_can('hire_employee','edit',id)		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('employee_details','delete',id)
OR user_can('inactive_employees','delete',id)
OR user_can('hire_employee','delete',id)		 "Employee master" = employee_details module. ESS self-access via Path C. Hardcoded bypass removed mig 109.
Step 4 in get_workflow_participants() — queried to get full_name + job_title for each approver displayed in the WorkflowSubmitModal preview.
📖 PLAIN Fully permission-driven. Active: "Employee master" View. Inactive: "Inactive Employees" View. Pipeline: "Hire Employee" View. Scope (target group) controls who you actually see 📖 PLAIN Only "Hire Employee" Create permission can add new employees 📖 PLAIN All edits through the matrix. Own profile → "Personal Info" edit. Active → "Employee master" edit. Deactivate/Reactivate → "Inactive Employees". Pipeline → "Hire Employee" 📖 PLAIN "Employee master" / "Inactive Employees" / "Hire Employee" Delete — depends on status
🖥  Screen: "Employee list, profiles & org chart" — visible to users who have the employee_details.view permission

employee_dependents
Path D		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('dependents','view',employee_id)
OR user_can('dependents','edit',employee_id)
OR (employee_id=get_my_employee_id() AND has_permission('dependents.view'))		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('dependents','create',employee_id)
OR (self AND has_permission('dependents.create'))		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('dependents','edit',employee_id)
OR (self AND has_permission('dependents.edit'))		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('dependents','delete',employee_id)
HR/admin only — ESS must use remove_dependent() RPC		 Module: dependents. Effective-dated rows. Permissions: dependents.view/create/edit/delete. ESS accesses own data via self path. Audit trigger attached. (mig 289 / 296)
📖 PLAIN HR/admin with view or edit permission scoped to employee's group — OR employee viewing their own dependents 📖 PLAIN HR/admin with dependents.create — OR self-create for ESS 📖 PLAIN HR/admin with dependents.edit — OR self-edit for ESS (needed for effective_to close-out during amendments) 📖 PLAIN HR/admin with dependents.delete only — ESS uses remove_dependent() soft-delete RPC
employee_dependent_attachments
Path D		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('dependents','view',employee_id)
OR user_can('dependents','edit',employee_id)
OR (self AND has_permission('dependents.view'))		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('dependents','edit',employee_id)
OR (self AND has_permission('dependents.edit'))		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('dependents','edit',employee_id)
OR (self AND has_permission('dependents.edit'))		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('dependents','delete',employee_id)
HR/admin only		 Mirrors dependent policy. Linked by dependent_code (logical identity), not dependent_id version. Soft-delete via is_active. Audit trigger attached. (mig 289 / 296)
📖 PLAIN Mirrors employee_dependents SELECT — same access paths 📖 PLAIN Edit permission required (HR/admin or ESS self-manage) 📖 PLAIN Soft-delete via is_active=false — same as INSERT gate 📖 PLAIN Hard delete HR/admin only
🖥  Screen: "Dependents" portlet in MyProfile, EmployeeEditPanel, AddEmployee, WorkflowReview — visible to users who have the dependents.view permission (mig 289)
Auth Domain
profiles
No user_can()		 ⚙ POLICY
auth.uid() = id (own)
OR user_can('employee','view_directory')		 ⚙ POLICY
Only via handle_new_user() trigger		 ⚙ POLICY
Own profile always; admin for others		 ⚙ POLICY
❌ Never		 Created by handle_new_user() trigger. profiles.employee_id links to employees table
Step 3 in get_workflow_participants() — queried to resolve profile-type approver IDs into display names for the WorkflowSubmitModal approver preview.
📖 PLAIN Own profile always readable. Admins read all. Users with employee_details.view permission on a target employee can also read their profile row — mirrors employees_select and allows features like Reassign typeahead to find colleagues (mig 178) 📖 PLAIN Created automatically when you sign up — never manually 📖 PLAIN You can always edit your own profile. Admins can update others 📖 PLAIN Profiles are never deleted
⚠️  Note: employee.view_directory was a legacy permission (mig 021, old has_permission() era) — it was removed from profiles RLS in mig 127. The Employee Directory screen is gated by employee_details.view on the employees table, not here. profiles has no Permission Matrix module — SELECT is self-only + admin by default, with a third path added in mig 178: any user with employee_details.view on a target employee can also read their profile row (consistent with employees_select; enables Reassign typeahead and future features without SECURITY DEFINER RPCs).
super_admins
No user_can()		 ⚙ POLICY
is_super_admin() only		 ⚙ POLICY
service_role only		 ⚙ POLICY
service_role only		 ⚙ POLICY
service_role only		 Immutable from app layer — managed directly in the database
📖 PLAIN Only super admins can see who else is a super admin 📖 PLAIN Cannot be done from the app — direct DB access only 📖 PLAIN Cannot be done from the app 📖 PLAIN Cannot be done from the app
Permissions Domain
modules
Path B		 ⚙ POLICY
✅ Any authenticated user		 ⚙ POLICY
service_role only		 ⚙ POLICY
service_role only		 ⚙ POLICY
service_role only		 Read-only reference — seeded by migrations
📖 PLAIN Any logged-in user can read — read-only reference data 📖 PLAIN System only — seeded by migrations 📖 PLAIN System only 📖 PLAIN System only

Path B		 ⚙ POLICY
✅ Any authenticated user		 ⚙ POLICY

❌ No UPDATE — delete + insert to change		 ⚙ POLICY
is_super_admin() OR user_can('sec_permission_matrix','edit',NULL)		 No UPDATE policy by design — prevents partial edits. Delete + insert is the only change path
📖 PLAIN Any logged-in user can read — user_can() reads this on every permission check 📖 PLAIN Security admins only — ticking a checkbox in the Permission Matrix inserts a row here 📖 PLAIN Not allowed — to change a permission, delete the row and re-insert 📖 PLAIN Security admins only — unticking a checkbox deletes the row here

📖 PLAIN Security admins only — these are assignments of a permission set to an employee (with target group and scope) 📖 PLAIN Security admins only — assigning a permission set to an employee creates a row here 📖 PLAIN Not allowed — delete and re-assign to change scope or target group 📖 PLAIN Security admins only — revoking a permission set deletes the row here

audit_log
Path B		 ⚙ POLICY
user_id = auth.uid() OR user_can('sys_audit_log','view',NULL) OR (user_can('sec_role_assignments','view',NULL) AND entity_type IN ('user_roles','permission_set_assignments','permission_set_items'))		 ⚙ POLICY
SECURITY DEFINER triggers only		 ⚙ POLICY
❌ Never		 ⚙ POLICY
❌ Never		 Immutable by design — tamper-proof record of system-level changes. Central admin screen (sys_audit_log.view) not yet built — sys_audit_log is seeded in the DB but intentionally excluded from the Permission Matrix UI until the screen exists. Contextual record history served via get_record_history() SECURITY DEFINER RPC — no extra permissions needed for employees or approvers.
📖 PLAIN You see your own activity. sys_audit_log.view holders see all (central audit screen — not yet built; permission exists in DB but not exposed in Permission Matrix UI). sec_role_assignments.view holders see role/permission entries only (entity_type scoped to user_roles, permission_set_assignments, permission_set_items — not the full log). Contextual record history for any record served via get_record_history() RPC — no extra permissions needed. 📖 PLAIN Written automatically by SECURITY DEFINER triggers — never manually 📖 PLAIN Never — permanent and immutable 📖 PLAIN Never — permanent and immutable
🖥  Screen: "Audit Trail" (central admin screen — not yet built) — will require sys_audit_log.view. Contextual history per record served via get_record_history() SECURITY DEFINER RPC.
employee_audit_log
Path D		 ⚙ POLICY
table_name-routed: employees rows → user_can('employee_details'|'inactive_employees'|'hire_employee','history',employee_id) by status; satellite rows → user_can('<module>','history',employee_id)		 ⚙ POLICY
WITH CHECK (false) — SECURITY DEFINER triggers only		 ⚙ POLICY
❌ Never		 ⚙ POLICY
❌ Never		 Every change to employee records is permanently logged here. Access is granular — routed by table_name and employee status to the matching module's .history permission. No self-service clause — employees cannot see their own audit trail directly (use get_record_history() RPC instead).
📖 PLAIN Access is module-scoped. Active employee rows → need employee_details.history. Inactive → inactive_employees.history. Draft → hire_employee.history. Each satellite (personal_info, contact_info, employment, address, emergency_contacts, identity_documents, passport) gates to its own module.history permission. You only see history for modules your role has access to — no blanket "see all history" permission 📖 PLAIN Written automatically by SECURITY DEFINER triggers whenever employee data changes — no app-level inserts permitted 📖 PLAIN Never — permanent and immutable 📖 PLAIN Never — permanent and immutable
🖥  Screen: "Employee change history tab" — gated per-module: active records require employee_details.history, inactive require inactive_employees.history, satellite tabs gate to their own module.history permission
Jobs Domain
job_run_log
Path B		 ⚙ POLICY
user_can('jobs_manage','view',NULL)		 ⚙ POLICY
SECURITY DEFINER job functions only (no RLS INSERT policy)		 ⚙ POLICY
SECURITY DEFINER job functions only		 ⚙ POLICY
❌ Never		 Scheduled job execution log — pg_cron writes here when jobs run via SECURITY DEFINER functions. Read access gated to jobs_manage.view (mig 141 — upgraded from legacy admin role check).
📖 PLAIN Only users with the jobs_manage.view permission can read job logs — monitor scheduled job health and execution history 📖 PLAIN Written automatically by SECURITY DEFINER job functions — never manually 📖 PLAIN Updated by SECURITY DEFINER job functions to record completion or failure 📖 PLAIN Never deleted — kept permanently for audit
🖥  Screen: "Scheduled Jobs monitor" — visible to users who have jobs_manage.view permission

user_can() Authorization Paths

Path A — Super Admin Bypass
IF is_super_admin() THEN RETURN true
UUID allowlist in super_admins table. Bypasses ALL permission checks. Writes only via service_role.
Path B — Admin Module
p_owner IS NULL → PSA chain, no target scoping
Called for ownerless tables (departments, picklists, permission sets). Checks the PSA chain without joining target_groups — permission exists at all = access granted.
Path D — Scope-Aware Check
PSA → target_groups → scope_type resolution
Main data path for all employee-scoped records. Resolves self / everyone / custom / direct_l1 / direct_l2 / same_department / same_country against p_owner. permission_set_assignments is the sole source of truth.
Path C & Legacy Path D — Removed ✓ Cleaned up
role_permissions table dropped (mig 146)
Path C (self short-circuit) removed in mig 114. Legacy role_permissions fallback and sync triggers removed in mig 146. role_permissions table dropped.

📖 Policy Function Glossary

Every unique pattern you'll see in the POLICY cells above — explained simply, with a real-world analogy.

user_can('module', 'action', NULL)
Path B — Module-level permission check
Asks: "Does this user hold the module.action permission at all?" No scope involved — it's a yes/no based purely on what's in their permission set. The NULL means "don't filter by target group."
🏢 Analogy: Your office keycard is programmed for "Finance Room access." The door doesn't check which desk you're going to — it just checks if Finance Room is on your card. If yes, door opens for every desk inside.
user_can('module', 'action', employee_id)
Path D — Scoped permission check
Asks: "Does this user have module.action AND does their target group include this specific employee?" Both must be true. Same permission code, but evaluated per-row against the requesting user's allowed scope (self / team / department / country).
🏢 Analogy: Your manager badge says "can view expense reports." But at the door of each report, the database checks: "Is this employee actually in your team?" Your badge alone isn't enough — the report also has to belong to someone you manage.
user_can('module', 'lookup', NULL)
Lookup — Read-only dropdown access
A lighter version of view. Grants SELECT on reference tables (currencies, picklists, projects, departments) just enough to populate dropdowns and autocomplete fields. You can't manage the data — you can only read the list.
🏢 Analogy: You're filling out a form and need to pick a department from a dropdown. You're not the Departments Admin — you can't add or edit departments — but the system still lets you read the list so your form works.
is_super_admin()
Super admin hard bypass
Checks if the calling user's ID is in the super_admins table. If yes, the RLS policy passes immediately — no permission matrix check needed. Only a handful of tables use this as a clause (system config, super_admins itself). Not the same as having a powerful role.
🏢 Analogy: The building master key. Works on every lock regardless of which department you belong to. Very few people have it, and it's managed separately from the normal keycard system.
auth.uid() = id  /  user_id = auth.uid()
Self-access — own row only
Compares the logged-in user's Supabase auth ID against a column on the row. If they match, access is granted — no permission needed. Used on tables like profiles, audit_log, workflow_tasks so users can always see their own data.
🏢 Analogy: Your payslip has your name on it. You can see it because it's yours — regardless of what permissions your role has. No badge needed; it just checks if your name matches.
auth.uid() IS NOT NULL
Any authenticated user
The loosest gate — if you're logged in at all, you pass. Used for tables where all employees need basic read access (e.g. user_roles SELECT so user_can() can look up your own permissions, target_groups to show non-system groups in the UI).
🏢 Analogy: The company cafeteria. If you have an employee badge (you're logged in), you can enter. There's no special "cafeteria permission" — showing up with a valid badge is enough.
service_role only
Backend internal writes — humans cannot trigger directly
Only the Supabase service role key (used by backend jobs, edge functions, pg_cron) can write to this table. Regular authenticated users have no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policy — any attempt is blocked at the DB level. The service role bypasses RLS entirely.
🏢 Analogy: The server room. Only IT systems staff with a special "service" badge can get in. Even the CEO can't walk in with their normal badge. Human employees never touch those servers directly.
Triggers / service_role
Written by DB triggers or backend jobs — never by a user action
The row is created automatically in response to something else happening (e.g. a trigger fires when you update a field, or a cron job runs nightly). There's no INSERT RLS policy for users because no user is supposed to ever write to this table directly.
🏢 Analogy: The building's automatic door-entry log. When you swipe your badge, the system writes a log entry automatically. You never type into that log yourself — it just happens behind the scenes.
Only via handle_new_user() trigger
Row created automatically on first sign-up
The handle_new_user() function is a Postgres trigger attached to Supabase's auth.users table. The moment a new user account is created, Postgres automatically calls it to insert a matching row in profiles. No human INSERT is ever needed.
🏢 Analogy: When HR adds a new employee to the HR system, the payroll system automatically creates their payslip record. Nobody in payroll has to manually create it — it's triggered by the hire event.
Managed by sync job (service_role)
Background job keeps this table in sync — not user-driven
A scheduled job (pg_cron or an edge function) periodically refreshes this table based on other data. Used for derived/denormalised tables that would be too expensive to compute on every query. Users read from it, but the backend owns all writes.
🏢 Analogy: The company org chart poster on the wall. It's printed and updated by IT every Monday. Employees read it, but nobody walks up and scribbles on it — the update happens on a schedule behind the scenes.
SECURITY DEFINER
Function runs as its owner — not as you
When a function is marked SECURITY DEFINER, it executes with the permissions of whoever created the function (typically a superuser), not the permissions of whoever is calling it. This lets the function read tables you'd normally never have access to — but only in a controlled, scoped way defined inside the function body.
🏢 Analogy: You ask the bank teller to check your balance. The teller uses their master terminal to look it up — you don't get access to the terminal yourself. The teller controls exactly what they show you. You asked; they did the lookup on your behalf.
SECURITY INVOKER
Function runs as you — your RLS still applies
The default for Postgres functions. When called, the function uses the calling user's own permissions. So if you try to JOIN two tables inside an SECURITY INVOKER view/function and you don't have SELECT on one of them, the join fails. Your RLS policies are still fully enforced inside the function.
🏢 Analogy: You log into the HR portal yourself and try to pull a colleague's record. The system checks your own permissions as you navigate. Nothing is elevated — if you don't have access, you get blocked just like anywhere else.
WITH CHECK (false)
Explicit block — no user can ever INSERT/UPDATE this table
WITH CHECK is the write-side of an RLS policy — it validates the row you're trying to write. Setting it to false means the check always fails, making it impossible for any authenticated user to write a row here directly. Used on audit tables and system logs where only triggers/service_role should ever write.
🏢 Analogy: The audit trail binder in the filing cabinet has a lock that no employee key fits. You can read the pages, but nobody can slip a new page in themselves. Only the official process can add pages.
Via parent expense report RLS
Child table inherits access from parent row
This table has no independent RLS check — instead, accessing it requires going through a JOIN to its parent table (e.g. expense_reports), and the parent's RLS policy does the gating. If you can see the parent report, you can see its child rows. If you can't, you can't — even if you know the child row's ID.
🏢 Analogy: A folder inside a locked filing cabinet drawer. The drawer itself has a lock (the parent's RLS). There's no separate lock on the folder inside. If you have the key to the drawer, you get the folder too.
Via parent report (draft only)
Child table inherits parent access — but only while the parent is a draft
Same as the parent-RLS pattern above, but with an additional status gate: the INSERT/UPDATE only works if the parent record is still in draft status. Once submitted/approved, the parent is locked and child rows can no longer be modified through normal user actions.
🏢 Analogy: You can add receipts to an expense claim while it's still on your desk (draft). Once you've handed it to your manager (submitted), the envelope is sealed — you can't slip more receipts in, even though you're the one who created it.
Via wf_submit() / wf_approve() / wf_reject() RPC only
Only a specific SECURITY DEFINER function is allowed to write here
The table itself allows any authenticated user to INSERT (open gate) — but in practice the only code that calls INSERT is the wf_submit() (or approve/reject) RPC function. The function is SECURITY DEFINER, so it runs under elevated permissions and can enforce business rules (valid workflow, correct state transitions) before writing.
🏢 Analogy: The "Submit for Approval" button on a form. Technically the submit endpoint accepts your request, but only the official Submit button wires it up correctly — it validates everything first. You can't POST directly to bypass the checks because the button handler does the real work.
get_record_history(p_entity_type, p_entity_id)
SECURITY DEFINER RPC — audit history without full audit log access
A controlled window into employee_audit_log. You call this function with a specific entity type and ID (e.g. an employee's UUID), and it returns only the change history for that record — nothing else. The function itself (not you) reads the audit log using elevated permissions, then filters to only what you're entitled to see based on your *.history permission.
🏢 Analogy: You ask the archivist: "Can I see the change history for employee #4521?" The archivist goes into the secure vault (which you can't enter), finds just that employee's file, and hands you only those pages. You never touch the vault.
get_workflow_participants(p_module_code text) → jsonb[]
SECURITY DEFINER RPC — approver routing preview for ESS users (mig 166)
Resolves the active workflow template steps for a given module code into a display-safe participant list for the WorkflowSubmitModal. The function reads workflow_steps, profiles, and employees using elevated permissions — bypassing the wf_templates.view RLS that normally gates those tables — and returns only the information needed to render the "Who will approve this?" preview.
Returns: jsonb array of { step_order, step_name, approver_type, approver_label } — no raw IDs exposed.
Grant: REVOKE ALL … GRANT EXECUTE TO authenticated — any logged-in user can call it; no extra permission required.
Root cause fixed: mig 153 correctly tightened workflow_steps SELECT to user_can('wf_templates','view'), which broke the ESS pre-submit preview ("No approvers" shown to employees). get_workflow_participants() is the read-only, display-safe bypass.
🏢 Analogy: Before submitting a form, you want to see the list of people who will review it. You can't read the approval rulebook yourself (wf_templates.view), but you can ask reception "Who needs to sign off on a leave request?" — reception checks the rulebook and reads you the names without handing you the book.

Part 6 — Business Rules & Validations

Hard constraints enforced at the database layer (CHECK constraints, UNIQUE indexes, trigger guards) plus soft business rules enforced by RLS policies and RPC functions.

💳 Expense Module

CategoryRuleSeverityEnforcement TypeActual ImplementationFailure BehaviorBypass Risk
Data IntegritySoft-delete only in draftCriticalCHECK ConstraintCHECK (deleted_at IS NULL OR status = 'draft') — prevents setting deleted_at on non-draft recordsPostgreSQL raises check_violation — transaction rolls backNone — enforced at storage layer
WorkflowStatus lifecycle is one-wayHighSECURITY DEFINER RPCdraft → submitted → approved|rejected. RPCs validate transition before writing. No reversion to draft after submitRAISE EXCEPTION in RPC — no status change appliedservice_role can UPDATE status directly, bypassing RPC guards
Access ControlEdit: owner pathHighRLS Policyuser_can('expense_reports','edit',employee_id) AND employee_id = get_my_employee_id() — employee can edit their own reportSilent — 0 rows updated; no error raisedservice_role bypasses RLS
Access ControlEdit: approver path (dual-control)HighRLS Policyuser_can('expense_reports','edit',employee_id) AND assigned workflow task is pending AND workflow_steps.allow_edit = true — approver can edit only when the step explicitly permits itSilent — 0 rows updated; no error raisedservice_role bypasses RLS; approver cannot edit if step has allow_edit = false
Data IntegrityLine items require parent reportHighFK Constraintline_items.report_id NOT NULL FK → expense_reportsPostgreSQL raises foreign_key_violationNone — FK enforced at storage layer
Data IntegrityAttachments require parent reportMediumFK Constraintattachments.report_id NOT NULL FK → expense_reportsPostgreSQL raises foreign_key_violationNone — FK enforced at storage layer
Data IntegrityCurrency must existMediumFK Constraintline_items.currency_id → currencies.id (NOT NULL)PostgreSQL raises foreign_key_violationNone — FK enforced at storage layer

⚙️ Workflow Module

CategoryRuleSeverityEnforcement TypeActual ImplementationFailure BehaviorBypass Risk
ConcurrencyOne active workflow per recordCriticalUNIQUE IndexUNIQUE(module_code, record_id) WHERE status = 'in_progress' — partial index prevents duplicate active instancesPostgreSQL raises unique_violationNone — enforced at storage layer
Data IntegritySteps must be ordered 1-basedMediumUNIQUE IndexUNIQUE(template_id, step_order)PostgreSQL raises unique_violationNone — enforced at storage layer
ConfigurationOnly one active template per codeHighUNIQUE IndexUNIQUE(code) WHERE is_active = truePostgreSQL raises unique_violationNone — enforced at storage layer
VersioningVersion control on templatesMediumUNIQUE IndexUNIQUE(code, version) — enables snapshot versioning across template changesPostgreSQL raises unique_violationNone — enforced at storage layer
Access ControlMutations via RPCs onlyCriticalRLS Policyworkflow_instances/tasks INSERT blocked for regular users; wf_submit(), wf_approve(), wf_reject() are SECURITY DEFINER and bypass RLS. wf_pending_changes INSERT gated on user_can('wf_manage','edit',NULL) (mig 160)Silent — 0 rows inserted/updated for non-adminsWorkflow admins with wf_manage.edit can insert directly
Workflow LogicApprover type routingHighApplication LogicMANAGER→manager_id, ROLE→any with approver_role, DEPT_HEAD→dept head, SPECIFIC_USER→fixed profile, RULE_BASED→condition eval — resolved in wf_resolve_approver()RAISE EXCEPTION in wf_submit if approver cannot be resolvedNo DB enforcement — logic entirely in wf_submit/wf_resolve_approver
Data IntegrityDelegation overlap guardMediumTrigger RLS PolicyMig 042 trigger raises EXCEPTION on overlapping delegator/period. Mig 132 added RLS INSERT/UPDATE: delegator_id = auth.uid() OR user_can('wf_delegations','edit',NULL) — users can only create/edit their own delegationsRAISE EXCEPTION if overlapping delegation detectedservice_role can INSERT delegations directly
Workflow LogicInitiator-as-approver removalHighApplication LogicAlways active (no flag needed). After wf_resolve_approver(), if resolved approver = workflow_instances.submitted_by, the step is silently removed: NO workflow_tasks row created, INSERT workflow_action_log action='step_removed' task_id=NULL, UPDATE current_step, recurse. Completely invisible to end users. Applies in both wf_advance_instance() and wf_submit() (mig 163)Silently removed — no task row. Workflow advances automatically. No error raisedNo DB enforcement — logic in wf_advance_instance/wf_submit only; direct SQL can create self-approval tasks
Workflow LogicConsecutive duplicate approver removal (silent)MediumApplication LogicTemplate flag: workflow_templates.remove_duplicate_approver = true. Look-ahead, keep-last: step N is silently removed if the NEXT step (N+1) resolves to the same approver. No workflow_tasks row created. Logs workflow_action_log action='step_removed' task_id=NULL. Last step never removed. Applies in both wf_advance_instance() and wf_submit() (mig 163)Silently removed — no task row. Invisible to end usersFlag defaults false; templates must opt-in. No DB enforcement — logic in wf_advance_instance only
Workflow LogicConsecutive duplicate approver skip (visible)MediumApplication LogicTemplate flag: workflow_templates.skip_duplicate_approver = true. Same look-ahead, keep-last logic as removal, but CREATES a workflow_tasks row with status='skipped' and acted_at=now(). Visible in Activity feed. Logs workflow_action_log action='skipped'. No notification. Processing order: removal (mig 163) evaluated first, then skip (mig 164). Applies in both wf_advance_instance() and wf_submit() (mig 164)Task created with status='skipped' — visible in Activity. Workflow advances automaticallyFlag defaults false; templates must opt-in. No DB enforcement — logic in wf_advance_instance only
Access ControlESS approver preview via SECURITY DEFINER RPCHighSECURITY DEFINER RPCMig 153 tightened workflow_steps SELECT to user_can('wf_templates','view'), blocking ESS users from reading step data needed for the WorkflowSubmitModal approver preview. Mig 166 adds get_workflow_participants(p_module_code) SECURITY DEFINER RPC — reads workflow_steps, profiles, employees with elevated access, returns only display-safe { step_order, step_name, approver_type, approver_label } jsonb. GRANT EXECUTE TO authenticated. Hook: useWorkflowParticipants replaced the 3-step direct table chain with a single .rpc('get_workflow_participants') call.Without mig 166: WorkflowSubmitModal shows "No approvers" for ESS users. With it: preview renders correctly for all usersRead-only RPC — returns display labels only, no raw UUIDs or sensitive data. service_role can query workflow_steps directly
Workflow LogicROLE type fan-out (Option A)HighApplication LogicMig 204 (Option A): when a workflow step has approver_type = 'ROLE', wf_advance_instance() queries user_roles JOIN roles ON r.code = approver_role WHERE ur.is_active = true AND (expires_at IS NULL OR expires_at > now()) and creates one pending workflow_tasks row per active role holder. Delegation chains applied per holder. When approval_mode IS NULL: first holder to approve cancels all sibling tasks and advances the instance. When approval_mode = 'ALL_OF': advance only when the last sibling is approved. Adding/removing role members takes effect at the next submission — no template change needed.If a role has no active members: zero tasks created → instance auto-advances (step silently skipped). Admins should ensure roles are populated before go-liveNo DB enforcement — logic in wf_advance_instance only; direct SQL can create tasks without fan-out
Workflow Logicapproval_mode ALL_OF gateHighApplication LogicMig 204: workflow_steps.approval_mode = 'ALL_OF' requires every role holder to approve. wf_approve() checks EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM workflow_tasks WHERE instance_id = v_instance.id AND step_order = v_step.step_order AND status = 'pending' AND id != p_task_id). If any sibling is still pending, the individual approval is recorded (task status → approved) but wf_advance_instance() is NOT called — the instance stays at the current step. Only when the last sibling approves does the instance advance. CHECK constraint on workflow_steps: CHECK (approval_mode IN ('ALL_OF') OR approval_mode IS NULL) — ANY_OF removed in mig 204.Partial approvals are visible in workflow_action_log but the instance remains in_progress. Approver Inbox continues to show pending tasks until all have approvedNo DB enforcement — logic in wf_approve only; direct SQL can advance instance without all-of check
Data IntegrityWorkflow notes 1 000-char capMediumCHECK Constraint Application LogicMig 194: NOT VALID CHECK constraints on workflow_tasks.notes, workflow_action_log.notes, and line_items.note enforce char_length(notes) <= 1000 for new/updated rows. RPCs wf_approve, wf_reject, wf_return_to_initiator, wf_return_to_previous_step also enforce the cap via explicit RAISE EXCEPTION before writing. Frontend textarea has matching 1 000-char counter.RPC raises EXCEPTION 'Notes must not exceed 1000 characters'. CHECK constraint raises check_violation for direct SQLNOT VALID skips full table scan — old rows are unconstrained. service_role can bypass CHECK via direct UPDATE of old rows

👤 Employee Module

CategoryRuleSeverityEnforcement TypeActual ImplementationFailure BehaviorBypass Risk
Data IntegrityEmail uniquenessCriticalUNIQUE IndexUNIQUE(business_email) — prevents duplicate accountsPostgreSQL raises unique_violationNone — enforced at storage layer
Data IntegrityEmployee number uniquenessHighUNIQUE IndexUNIQUE(employee_number)PostgreSQL raises unique_violationNone — enforced at storage layer
LifecycleStatus lifecycle + RLS routingHighApp + TriggerDraft → Incomplete → Active → Inactive. UPDATE RLS routes by status: inactive_employees.create = deactivate, inactive_employees.edit = reactivate, hire_employee.edit = pipeline edits, employee_details.edit = active employee edits. trigger (mig 148) syncs profile/roles on transition.No hard DB constraint on transitions — application layer only; trigger raises RAISE WARNING not EXCEPTION on errorDirect SQL UPDATE on employees can skip lifecycle validation
Access ControlESS auto-grant on invite acceptHighSECURITY DEFINER Triggerhandle_new_user(): email match → profile linked → ESS role granted with assignment_source='auto'If trigger fails, user has no permissions; must be granted manually by adminCannot bypass — trigger fires for all auth.users inserts
LifecycleDeactivation syncHighSECURITY DEFINER TriggerAFTER UPDATE on employees WHERE status → 'Inactive': sets profiles.is_active = false + deletes ALL user_roles for that profile (mig 148)Silently skipped if trigger errors (RAISE WARNING, not EXCEPTION) — profile may stay activeDirect SQL update bypassing trigger context would skip sync
LifecycleReactivation syncHighSECURITY DEFINER TriggerAFTER UPDATE WHERE Inactive → 'Active': sets profiles.is_active = true + re-grants ESS only. Elevated roles (manager, HR etc.) must be manually re-assigned (mig 148)Same as deactivation — silently skipped on trigger errorSame as deactivation
Data IntegrityOne dept head per dateMediumUNIQUE IndexUNIQUE(department_id, from_date)PostgreSQL raises unique_violationNone — enforced at storage layer
StructuralManager must be employeeMediumFK Constraintemployees.manager_id → employees.id (nullable self-reference)PostgreSQL raises foreign_key_violationNone — FK enforced at storage layer
Data IntegritySatellite records 1:1HighPK Constraintemployee_personal, employee_contact, employee_employment use employee_id as PK — enforces exactly one row per employeePostgreSQL raises unique_violation on second insertNone — PK enforced at storage layer

🔄 Employee Lifecycle & Profile Sync

Every status transition on the employees table cascades to profiles and user_roles via the sync_profile_on_employee_status() trigger (mig 148). This ensures login access, permission state, and workflow pool membership stay in sync automatically.

Status Flow
Draft
→
Incomplete
→
Active
⇄
Inactive
Draft/Incomplete = hire pipeline only. Active ⇄ Inactive is the live deactivation/reactivation path.
TransitionTrigger / MechanismWhat happens
→ Draft Admin creates hire record Employee row created with status=Draft. No profile, no auth account yet.
Draft → Incomplete Application logic Required fields partially filled. Still no auth account. Visible only to users with hire_employee.view.
Incomplete → Active Hire complete Admin action + handle_new_user() trigger on invite accept Invite email sent → employee accepts → Supabase creates auth.users row → handle_new_user() inserts into profiles (is_active=true) and links employee_id → ESS role granted automatically (assignment_source='auto').
Active → Inactive Deactivation sync_profile_on_employee_status() trigger (mig 148) 1. profiles.is_active = false — blocks the user from the system and removes them from all workflow pools
2. All user_roles deleted — every permission is revoked immediately
3. Employee row still exists; historical data preserved. Visible to users with inactive_employees.view.
Inactive → Active Reactivation sync_profile_on_employee_status() trigger (mig 148) 1. profiles.is_active = true — restores login access
2. ESS role re-granted (assignment_source='system_reactivation')
3. Elevated roles are NOT auto-restored — manager, HR, finance roles must be re-assigned manually by an admin. This is intentional: a reactivated employee starts with minimal access.
⚠️ Key design decisions
profiles has no Permission Matrix module — access is self-only or super admin (mig 127). Never grant direct table access via the UI.
All roles deleted on deactivation (not just ESS) — prevents a deactivated employee with an admin role from passing user_can() checks.
Only ESS re-granted on reactivation — clean-slate security posture. Elevated access requires deliberate re-assignment.
Employee data is never deleted — soft-delete via status only. Historical records, audit trails, and payroll data remain intact.

🔐 Permissions Module

CategoryRuleSeverityEnforcement TypeActual ImplementationFailure BehaviorBypass Risk
Data IntegrityOne role per user per setMediumUNIQUE IndexUNIQUE(permission_set_id, role_id, target_group_id) on permission_set_assignmentsPostgreSQL raises unique_violationNone — enforced at storage layer
Data IntegritySystem groups undeletableHighRLS PolicyDELETE policy: user_can('sec_target_groups','edit',NULL) AND NOT is_system — admins CAN delete non-system groups; only system-seeded groups (is_system=true) are protected by the AND NOT guardSilent — 0 rows deleted; no error raisedservice_role bypasses RLS
Access ControlScope types determine resolutionCriticalApplication Logicself/everyone/direct_l1/direct_l2/same_department/same_country/custom — each resolved differently in user_can(). NULL scope = permission applies to all recordsReturns false if scope resolution fails — no exception raisedNo DB enforcement — entirely in user_can() logic; direct SQL reads bypass scope entirely
Access ControlSuper admin writes blockedCriticalRLS PolicySELECT open to all authenticated (USING (true)) — any user can read the super_admins table. No INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policies for authenticated users — only service_role can mutate. is_super_admin() is the recommended read path.Permission denied — transaction aborted for authenticated usersMust use service_role — intentionally restricted to ops team
Data IntegrityPermission codes unique per moduleHighUNIQUE IndexUNIQUE(code) on permissions table (code is module.action format)PostgreSQL raises unique_violationNone — enforced at storage layer
AuditAudit all permission changesHighSECURITY DEFINER TriggerMig 143: trg_audit_user_roles, trg_audit_psa, trg_audit_psi write to audit_log on every DML on user_roles, permission_set_assignments, permission_set_itemsIf trigger errors, DML still succeeds but audit row is silently lostTriggers fire even for service_role — cannot be bypassed without dropping trigger

📚 Reference Data

CategoryRuleSeverityEnforcement TypeActual ImplementationFailure BehaviorBypass Risk
Data IntegrityNo self-referencing exchange rateMediumCHECK ConstraintCHECK (from_currency_id <> to_currency_id)PostgreSQL raises check_violationNone — DB constraint enforced at storage layer
Data IntegrityOne rate per currency pair per dayMediumUNIQUE IndexUNIQUE(from_currency_id, to_currency_id, effective_date)PostgreSQL raises unique_violationNone — enforced at storage layer
StructuralPicklist values can be nestedLowFK Constraintpicklist_values.parent_id → picklist_values.id (nullable self-reference)PostgreSQL raises foreign_key_violation if parent doesn't existNone — FK enforced at storage layer
Data IntegrityProject codes uniqueMediumUNIQUE IndexUNIQUE(code) on projectsPostgreSQL raises unique_violationNone — enforced at storage layer

📋 Audit Rules

CategoryRuleSeverityEnforcement TypeActual ImplementationFailure BehaviorBypass Risk
Audit IntegrityAudit tables are append-onlyCriticalRLS PolicyNo authenticated user can UPDATE or DELETE from audit_log or employee_audit_log — RLS UPDATE/DELETE policies are falsePermission denied — transaction abortedservice_role can UPDATE/DELETE — intentional for emergency data ops only
Audit IntegrityINSERT via triggers onlyCriticalRLS PolicyRLS INSERT = false for authenticated users on audit tables; only SECURITY DEFINER trigger functions can writePermission denied for direct inserts by authenticated usersservice_role bypasses RLS — triggers ensure correct data shape
Audit Integrityauth.uid() captured correctlyHighSECURITY DEFINER TriggerTriggers run as function owner (definer) but capture auth.uid() via request.jwt.claims at trigger time to record the real actorIf JWT missing (service_role direct op), actor_id may be NULL in audit rowExpected: all writes via authenticated sessions; NULL actor_id signals service_role ops
Audit CoverageEmployee audit covers all satellitesHighSECURITY DEFINER Triggertrg_write_audit_log attached to: employees, employee_personal, employee_contact, employee_employment, employee_addresses, emergency_contacts, identity_records, passports (8 tables)If trigger errors silently, audit row is missed but DML still succeedsTriggers fire even for service_role — cannot be silently bypassed
Audit CoveragePermission audit covers PSA chainHighSECURITY DEFINER Triggertrg_audit_user_roles (user_roles) + trg_audit_psa (permission_set_assignments) + trg_audit_psi (permission_set_items) — mig 143Same as employee audit — DML succeeds, audit row may be lost on trigger errorTriggers fire even for service_role
Audit CoverageExpense audit — status transitions namedHighSECURITY DEFINER TriggerMig 145: trg_audit_expense_reports writes distinct verbs: expense_report.submitted / .approved / .rejected / .deleted / .updated. Soft-delete and hard-delete distinguishedIf trigger errors, expense DML still succeeds but audit row is lostTriggers fire even for service_role
Audit CoverageExpense audit — line itemsMediumSECURITY DEFINER TriggerMig 145: trg_audit_line_items. metadata includes report_id — reconstruct full report trail by filtering entity_type='line_items' AND metadata->>'report_id'If trigger errors, DML still succeeds but audit row is lostTriggers fire even for service_role
Audit CoverageExpense audit — attachments immutableMediumSECURITY DEFINER TriggerMig 145: trg_audit_attachments — INSERT (attachment.uploaded) and DELETE only. No UPDATE trigger — attachments cannot be edited once uploadedIf trigger errors, DML still succeeds but audit row is lostTriggers fire even for service_role

🔤 Enum & Status Reference

expense_status
draft ↓ submit submitted ↓ send back needs_update ↓ resubmit | withdraw ↓ approve | reject approved rejected
needs_update added mig 180
employee_status
Draft ↓ Incomplete ↓ Active ↓ Inactive
⚠ Title-case strings (unusual)
workflow_instance status
in_progress ↓ send back awaiting_clarification ↓ resubmit | withdraw → final states: approved rejected withdrawn cancelled

🏦 Bank Account Effective-Date Rules

Formalized in migration 299. These rules govern when an ESS user may submit a bank change and when an approver may approve it, preventing mid-month banking switches that could disrupt payroll runs.

CategoryRuleSeverityEnforcement TypeActual ImplementationFailure BehaviorBypass / Exemption
Submission Window ESS submit: 1st – 15th only Critical SECURITY DEFINER RPC upsert_bank_account: if EXTRACT(day FROM now()) > 15 AND caller is not bank_exceptions/admin/hr role AND not a new hire → RAISE EXCEPTION 'Bank changes may only be submitted between the 1st and 15th of the month' RAISE EXCEPTION — transaction aborted; frontend shows inline error toast Roles bank_exceptions, admin, hr bypass. New hires bypass all date rules.
Effective Date effective_from must be 1st of current month Critical SECURITY DEFINER RPC upsert_bank_account: validates p_effective_from = date_trunc('month', now())::date for standard ESS users. Rejects any other date (past months, future months, mid-month dates). RAISE EXCEPTION — transaction aborted Exempt roles may pass any valid date. New hires bypass.
Approval Window Approver blocked after 20th High SECURITY DEFINER RPC apply_profile_pending_change (AFTER UPDATE trigger on workflow_pending_changes): when module_code = 'profile_bank' and status transitions to 'approved', if EXTRACT(day FROM now()) > 20 and approver is not bank_exceptions/admin/hr → RAISE EXCEPTION 'Bank changes cannot be approved after the 20th' RAISE EXCEPTION — approval blocked; workflow task stays pending; approver sees error Roles bank_exceptions, admin, hr bypass the 20th gate.
New Hire Bypass New hires exempt from all date rules High SECURITY DEFINER RPC If the employee's hire_date falls within the current calendar month, all date-window checks are skipped entirely in upsert_bank_account. This allows onboarding bank setup regardless of submission date. N/A — no failure; rule is bypassed Condition: hire_date >= date_trunc('month', now())
Frontend Signal is_bank_exception flag in gates RPC Medium SECURITY DEFINER RPC get_profile_workflow_gates() returns is_bank_exception: boolean (true if current user has bank_exceptions/admin/hr role). BankAccountsPortlet uses this to hide/show the submission-window warning banner without an extra RPC call. If missing: portlet always shows warning banner even for admins Read-only signal — no security enforcement at this layer
Bank change calendar summary
Days 1 – 15
ESS users may submit a bank change. effective_from locked to 1st of current month.
Days 1 – 20
Approvers may approve pending bank change requests.
Days 16 – 31
ESS submission blocked. After day 20, approvals also blocked. Exempt roles unaffected.

Part 7 — Design Observations

An objective review of the schema's architectural strengths, technical risks, and suggested improvements. Written for engineering leadership and future maintainers.

✅ What's Well Designed

Three-Layer Security Architecture
The system enforces authorization at three independent layers: DB RLS (user_can() on every table), UI gate (get_my_permissions() cached in React), and data scoping (get_target_population()). Compromise of any single layer doesn't bypass the others. This is enterprise-grade defense-in-depth.
Permission Set Abstraction
The PSA chain (role → permission_set → permissions) decouples role assignment from permission grants. Adding a new permission to a set immediately applies it to all roles holding that set — no per-role migration needed. This is exactly how enterprise RBAC systems (SAP, Workday) handle it.
Target Group Scoping
The target_groups + scope_type system elegantly handles "a manager can see only their direct reports" vs "finance can see everyone" within the same permission framework — without hardcoded role names in RLS policies. New scope types can be added without schema changes.
Immutable Audit Tables
RLS UPDATE/DELETE = false on audit tables is a strong compliance guarantee. SECURITY DEFINER trigger functions capture auth.uid() correctly even when called from service_role context. The dual-table approach (audit_log for permissions, employee_audit_log for PII changes) keeps concerns separated.
Employee Satellite Table Design
Splitting employees into 7 satellite tables (personal, contact, employment, addresses, emergency_contacts, identity_records, passports) enables per-module RLS at the field level. An employee can edit their own contact info without seeing another's identity documents. Rare in small-team HRIS systems.
Workflow Engine Decoupling
The workflow engine is generic (module_code + record_id) and not tied to expense reports. Any future module (leave requests, procurement) can reuse the same template/instance/task machinery. Template versioning (UNIQUE code+version) means in-flight approvals are not broken by template updates.

⚠️ Technical Risks

RLS Performance at Scale
user_can() is called on every row evaluated by RLS. For tables with 100k+ rows, this can result in thousands of function invocations per query. The function is STABLE and Supabase caches it per transaction, but complex queries (JOINs across 3+ tables) may still run user_can() many times. Benchmark at 50k+ employee rows before go-live.
target_group_members Sync Lag
The 'everyone' and 'custom' scope types rely on pre-computed target_group_members rows (synced by a pg_cron job). If the sync job fails or lags, newly onboarded employees won't appear in 'everyone' groups until the next run. This could silently lock out finance approvers from new employee data.
employee_status Title-Case Strings
'Draft', 'Incomplete', 'Active', 'Inactive' are title-case text values, not a PostgreSQL ENUM. This is inconsistent with expense_status (lowercase) and means a typo like 'active' will silently insert and bypass status checks. Should be migrated to a proper ENUM type.
No Row Count Guard on Satellite 1:1
employee_personal, employee_contact, employee_employment use employee_id as PK — correct. But employee_addresses, emergency_contacts, identity_records, passports use their own UUIDs, allowing multiple rows per employee. Application code must enforce "one address at a time" logic — there's no DB constraint preventing duplicates.
✓ Legacy role_permissions Removed (resolved in mig 146)
role_permissions table, sync triggers (trg_psi_sync, trg_psa_sync), and sync functions dropped. user_can() now reads permission_set_assignments exclusively. Verified by pre-flight DO block in mig 146.
ip_address in audit_log is App-Populated
audit_log.ip_address is a nullable text field populated by the application layer. This means IP logging depends on the API layer correctly passing the IP — it's not enforced at DB level. A misconfigured proxy could log incorrect IPs or leave the field NULL.
✓ workflow_pending_changes INSERT Hardened (resolved in mig 160 + 161)
What was the gap: Only workflow_pending_changes INSERT had an open gate — auth.uid() IS NOT NULL — meaning any authenticated user could directly INSERT a pending change row, bypassing wf_submit() business-rule enforcement. workflow_instances and workflow_tasks were already gated on admin permissions.

Mig 160: Changed wpc_insert to user_can('wf_manage','edit',NULL) — same pattern as workflow_tasks. wf_submit() is SECURITY DEFINER and bypasses RLS, so it is unaffected.

Mig 161: Wired profile_* modules into wf_sync_module_status(). Previously only expense_reports was handled — profile changes were approved in the workflow engine but workflow_pending_changes.status was never updated, so trg_apply_profile_pending_change never fired and the data never landed in satellite tables.

💡 Suggested Improvements

PriorityImprovementImpactEffort
High Convert employee_status to a PostgreSQL ENUM Eliminates silent typo bugs; type-safe in generated TypeScript 1 migration + UPDATE existing rows
High Remove legacy Path D from user_can() ✓ Done — mig 146 role_permissions table and sync triggers dropped Completed
High Add sync job health check alert Prevents silent target_group_members staleness; catches pg_cron failures Supabase log alert on job_run_log.status='failed'
Medium Add CHECK constraints for status transitions Prevents application bugs from creating invalid lifecycle states Trigger or CHECK per table
Medium Add DB-level IP capture via request header Makes IP logging reliable regardless of app-layer bugs Use current_setting('request.headers') in audit trigger
Medium Add LIMIT guards to user_can() internal queries Prevents runaway queries on large permission_set_assignments tables Add LIMIT 1 to EXISTS subqueries
Low Add created_by to expense_reports Enables audit of "who submitted on behalf of" for delegated submissions 1 column + migration
Low Materialise user permissions in a view Enables admin to see "what can user X do?" in one SELECT without joining 5 tables CREATE VIEW get_user_effective_permissions AS ...
Low Add partial index on active user_roles Already exists (idx_user_roles_profile_active) — verify it's used by EXPLAIN ANALYZE 0 — validate with EXPLAIN

📊 Migration Milestone Summary

Migration RangeWhat Changed
001–090Initial schema: currencies, departments, employees, expense_reports, profiles, profile_roles (legacy RBAC)
091–110Permission codes, modules table, sec_admin_access.view introduction, picklists, projects, exchange_rates
111–112super_admins table, is_super_admin() function, SECURITY DEFINER pattern established
113user_can() RPC — the central authorization function, all 4 paths, replaces has_role() in RLS
114–122Workflow engine: templates, steps, instances, tasks, SLA events, delegations, notifications + workflow_step_conditions, workflow_action_log, workflow_notification_templates, workflow_notification_queue, workflow_assignment_audit, workflow_pending_changes + 5 reporting views (vw_wf_pending_tasks, vw_wf_my_requests, vw_wf_operations, vw_notification_monitor, pending_invite_reminders)
123–142RLS migration: 20 SQL migrations replacing legacy has_role()/has_permission() with user_can() across all tables
143Audit triggers: user_roles, permission_set_assignments, permission_set_items → audit_log
144Super admin frontend bypass: get_my_permissions() + get_target_population() return full access for super_admins
145Expense audit triggers: status-aware audit on expense_reports (submitted/approved/rejected/deleted), line_items, and attachments → audit_log
146Drop role_permissions table, trg_psi_sync / trg_psa_sync triggers, and sync functions. user_can() now reads permission_set_assignments exclusively — clean two-path structure (Path A: super admin, Path B: admin module, Path D: scope-aware).
147Lookup permission architecture (Phase 2): ALTER permissions_action_check constraint to include 'lookup' action. Seed projects + currencies modules. Seed 4 permissions: projects.lookup, currencies.lookup, picklists.lookup, departments.lookup. Add additive SELECT lookup RLS policies on projects, currencies, picklists, picklist_values, departments. Create 4 SECURITY INVOKER lookup views (vw_projects_lookup, vw_currencies_lookup, vw_picklist_values_lookup, vw_departments_lookup). Assign all 4 lookup permissions to ESS permission set so every employee can populate transactional dropdowns.
148Sync profiles.is_active with employees.status: add sync_profile_on_employee_status() trigger — fires AFTER UPDATE on employees. Inactive: sets profiles.is_active = false + deletes all user_roles. Reactivation: sets profiles.is_active = true + re-grants ESS role. Elevated roles must be re-assigned by an admin.
149Introduce sec_permission_matrix module + view + edit permissions. Re-point write RLS on permission_sets, permission_set_items, permission_set_assignments from sec_permission_catalog.edit → sec_permission_matrix.edit. Permission Catalog is now read-only; Permission Matrix tab owns all writes.
150Align SELECT RLS with view-permission split: user_roles_select changed from sec_role_assignments.edit → sec_role_assignments.view so view-only admins can read member lists and counts. audit_log_select gains OR user_can('sec_role_assignments','view',NULL) so Role Assignments History tab works for view-only users without requiring sys_audit_log.view.
151Remove all SQL-seeded permission_set_items: deletes the rows hardcoded into the ESS permission set by migrations 110 (employee/expense permissions) and 147 (lookup permissions). Permission Matrix UI is now the single source of truth for all permission_set_items — no future migration will INSERT into this table.
152Introduce wf_notification_config module (sort_order 402). Seeds wf_notification_config.view + wf_notification_config.edit permissions. Re-points workflow_notification_templates write RLS (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) from wf_notifications.edit → wf_notification_config.edit. Manages Notifications is now a distinct permission concern, independent of Notification Monitor.
153Fix SELECT RLS on four workflow tables that were left as auth.uid() IS NOT NULL after migration 128/152 updated the write side. workflow_templates, workflow_steps, workflow_step_conditions SELECT → user_can('wf_templates','view'); workflow_notification_templates SELECT → user_can('wf_notification_config','view'). Engine functions are SECURITY DEFINER and unaffected.
154Fix workflow_tasks SELECT policy — replace user_can('wf_assignments','view') with user_can('wf_manage','view'). wf_assignments is the template-to-module routing module (admin config); the correct audience for seeing all tasks is Workflow Managers who monitor running workflows. Submitter visibility clause retained.
155Fix wf_sla_events SELECT policy — replace user_can('wf_manage','view') with user_can('wf_performance','view'). SLA breach events feed the Performance dashboard (cycle times, SLA metrics, bottleneck analysis); wf_performance.view is the dedicated permission for that screen. Assignee visibility clause retained.
156Tighten workflow_delegations RLS — self-service delegation operations now require wf_inbox.view in addition to delegator_id = auth.uid(). Previously any authenticated user could create/edit/delete their own delegations; only approvers (wf_inbox.view holders) should be able to delegate rights they actually have. Delegate SELECT visibility is unchanged (delegates always see rows where they cover). Path B, NULL target throughout.
157Fix SELECT RLS on workflow_pending_changes — add approver visibility via EXISTS subquery through workflow_tasks. Migration 129 set wpc_select to submitted_by OR wf_manage.view, missing the assigned approver who needs to read the change payload to action it. Also corrects INSERT policy docs: the actual gate is auth.uid() IS NOT NULL (open to authenticated); wf_submit() SECURITY DEFINER RPC enforces business rules.
158Add get_record_history(p_entity_type, p_entity_id) SECURITY DEFINER RPC. Provides contextual audit history for individual records without requiring sys_audit_log.view. Access enforced internally by domain: expense_reports (own OR expense view), profiles/employees (own OR hr_employees.view), workflow_instances/pending_changes (submitter OR assigned approver OR wf_manage.view), security tables (sec_role_assignments.view OR sec_permission_matrix.view). sys_audit_log.view bypasses all domain checks. REVOKE/GRANT ensures only authenticated users can call it.
159Security fix: scope audit_log SELECT for sec_role_assignments.view. Migration 150 added sec_role_assignments.view as an unscoped OR clause, inadvertently granting full audit_log read to role admins. Fixed by adding AND entity_type IN ('user_roles','permission_set_assignments','permission_set_items') so role admins can only see role/permission history entries — not expense, login, or HR events.
160Harden workflow_pending_changes INSERT RLS. Migration 046 set wpc_insert to auth.uid() IS NOT NULL — any authenticated user could directly INSERT, bypassing wf_submit() business-rule enforcement. Changed to user_can('wf_manage','edit',NULL) — consistent with workflow_tasks pattern. wf_submit() is SECURITY DEFINER so it bypasses RLS and is unaffected.
161Wire profile_* modules into wf_sync_module_status(). Before this migration, only expense_reports was handled — all other module_codes fell into a RAISE NOTICE no-op, meaning workflow_pending_changes status was never updated on approval/rejection and trg_apply_profile_pending_change (mig 117) never fired. Added ELSIF module_code LIKE 'profile_%' branch that UPDATEs workflow_pending_changes status/resolved_at, which triggers the existing apply function to write proposed_data to the correct satellite table (employee_personal, employee_contact, employee_addresses, passports, emergency_contacts).
162Fix wf_sync_module_status() profile_* branch: map p_status='draft' to 'withdrawn' when updating workflow_pending_changes. wf_withdraw() always passes 'draft' (correct for expense_reports to reset for editing), but workflow_pending_changes.status CHECK constraint only allows pending/approved/rejected/withdrawn. Without this fix, withdrawal of a profile_* workflow caused a constraint violation leaving the WPC row stuck at 'pending'. After this fix, withdrawn profile change requests are correctly marked 'withdrawn' with resolved_at set.
163Approver step removal rules (silent — no workflow_tasks row created). Rule 1 (always on): if wf_resolve_approver() returns the same profile_id as workflow_instances.submitted_by, the step is silently removed — an employee cannot approve their own request. Rule 2 (template flag): if workflow_templates.remove_duplicate_approver=true, a step is removed when the NEXT step resolves to the same approver (look-ahead, keep-last: the approver acts on the final consecutive occurrence). Removal writes workflow_action_log with action='step_removed', task_id=NULL for admin audit only — completely invisible to end users. Schema: workflow_templates.remove_duplicate_approver boolean DEFAULT false. Both wf_advance_instance and wf_submit updated.
164Consecutive duplicate approver visible skip. Template flag: workflow_templates.skip_duplicate_approver=true. Uses same look-ahead, keep-last logic as mig 163: step N is skipped when the NEXT step (N+1) resolves to the same approver. Unlike removal (mig 163), skip CREATES a workflow_tasks row with status='skipped' and acted_at=now(), and logs workflow_action_log with action='skipped'. Visible in the Activity feed; no notification sent to the bypassed approver. workflow_tasks.status CHECK constraint extended to include 'skipped' (via dynamic DO block to safely drop/re-add the existing constraint). Processing order in wf_advance_instance: Removal (mig 163) evaluated first, then Skip (mig 164), then CC auto-complete, then normal task creation. Both wf_advance_instance and wf_submit updated. UI: skip_duplicate_approver toggle added to template detail card and New Template modal in WorkflowTemplates.tsx.
165Workflow step conditions (RULE_BASED approver type): wf_step_conditions table, condition evaluation in wf_resolve_approver(). Enables dynamic routing based on record field values.
166SECURITY DEFINER RPC get_workflow_participants(p_module_code): resolves active template steps into a display-safe participant list for WorkflowSubmitModal. Bypasses RLS on workflow_steps, profiles, employees — ESS users can see approver routing preview without wf_templates.view. Returns jsonb array of {step_order, step_name, approver_type, approver_label}. REVOKE ALL / GRANT EXECUTE TO authenticated. Root cause: mig 153 correctly tightened workflow_steps SELECT to wf_templates.view, but broke the pre-submit "Who will approve this?" preview for ESS users. get_workflow_participants() is the safe read-only bypass.
167Profile change workflow — submit path (wf_submit_profile_change RPC). SECURITY DEFINER RPC that orchestrates submitting a profile field change for approval: validates field, inserts workflow_pending_changes row, calls wf_submit() to create the workflow instance. Returns instance_id. Called by WorkflowSubmitModal when the module_code is profile_*.
168Profile workflow gates — useProfileWorkflowGates() hook (initial version). Client-side hook that queried workflow_assignments + workflow_pending_changes directly for gate detection and pending counts. Superseded by mig 175 which replaces the direct queries with a user-aware SECURITY DEFINER RPC.
169workflow_steps approver assignee SELECT policy (wf_steps_assignee_read). Migration 153 tightened workflow_steps SELECT to wf_templates.view, which also blocked approvers from reading the step they are actively assigned to review. Approvers have wf_inbox.view but not wf_templates.view, causing WorkflowReview.tsx's nested join (.select('workflow_steps(allow_edit)')) to silently return null for step columns — collapsing the canEditMidFlight gate to always-false. Fix: add a second SELECT policy (PostgreSQL ORs multiple SELECT policies) scoped to steps the caller is actually assigned to via workflow_tasks. No UI changes needed — the existing nested join resolves automatically.
170WorkflowReview.tsx — mid-flight line item editing UI. Renders the allow_edit flag from workflow_steps (now readable by approvers via mig 169) to conditionally enable inline editing of line items while a workflow is in progress. Controlled by canEditMidFlight = stepAllowEdit && canEditOnApproval.
171MyProfile pending banner — SectionHeader component update. Each profile section header now shows a pending-approval banner (amber, with hourglass icon) when useProfileWorkflowGates() reports a non-zero pending count for that section. Powered by pendingCounts already fetched by the hook — no extra DB call. Visible only to the submitter viewing their own profile.
172Workflow submission error toast (MyProfile). Adds a fixed-position error toast (emp-toast--error pattern, 7-second auto-dismiss) to WorkflowSubmitModal in MyProfile for any uncaught exception during executeGatedSubmit(). Existing setSaveError is retained; the toast ensures the error is always visible regardless of modal scroll position.
173Fix wf_sync_module_status() profile_* branch — complete status mapping. Migration 162 fixed 'draft'→'withdrawn' but left 'submitted' and 'in_progress' unmapped, causing a CHECK constraint violation (workflow_pending_changes.status only accepts pending/approved/rejected/withdrawn) when wf_submit() first calls wf_sync_module_status with p_status='submitted'. Full CASE mapping: submitted→pending, in_progress→pending, draft→withdrawn, cancelled→withdrawn, approved→approved, rejected→rejected, withdrawn→withdrawn, unknown→no-op. Also fixes resolved_at: only set for terminal statuses (approved/rejected/withdrawn); previously always set to now() even on 'submitted', which incorrectly stamped a resolution time on open records.
174Fix infinite recursion between workflow_tasks and workflow_instances SELECT RLS policies. wf_tasks_select (mig 154) contained an inline EXISTS on workflow_instances; wf_instances_select (mig 129) contained an inline EXISTS on workflow_tasks — creating a circular cross-reference. PostgreSQL error 42P17 (infinite recursion detected in policy for relation workflow_tasks) surfaced when WorkflowTemplates.tsx's step-list query triggered the RLS stack. Fix: create is_wf_task_assignee(p_instance_id uuid) — a SECURITY DEFINER function that reads workflow_tasks as the function owner, bypassing its RLS and breaking the loop. wf_instances_select rebuilt using this helper. The approver clause on wf_instances_select must remain (not removed) because vw_wf_pending_tasks — the approver inbox view — is a plain CREATE VIEW that JOINs workflow_instances and depends on approvers being able to see their assigned instances.
