175Fix profile workflow gate detection — replace direct workflow_assignments query with user-aware SECURITY DEFINER RPC get_profile_workflow_gates(). Root cause (two bugs): (1) The hook queried workflow_assignments directly ("does ANY assignment exist for this module?") which is not user-aware — a ROLE-based assignment for profile_address would gate all users in the UI even if their role didn't match, causing submit_change_request to fail with "No active workflow assignment found". (2) The hook ran once on mount — if admin assigned/removed a workflow while My Profile was open, gates were stale. Fix: get_profile_workflow_gates() calls resolve_workflow_for_submission(module_code, auth.uid()) for each of the 7 profile module codes — same resolver used by submit_change_request — guaranteeing UI gate and submission layer are always consistent. Also returns pending counts scoped to submitted_by = auth.uid(). Hook updated to call this RPC; exposes refetch() which MyProfile calls on edit-mode entry to ensure gates are always fresh. No schema changes — purely a new SECURITY DEFINER function + hook rewrite.
176Approver Inbox before/after diff — add current_data snapshot to workflow_pending_changes. Three-part change: (1) ADD COLUMN current_data jsonb (nullable) to workflow_pending_changes — NULL for all existing rows, backward compatible. (2) Rewrite submit_change_request() to snapshot current satellite-table values before inserting the pending change row. Uses safe approach: reads entire row via to_jsonb() then filters to only the keys present in proposed_data — no hard-coded column names. Table map: profile_personal→employee_personal, profile_contact→employee_contact, profile_address→employee_addresses, profile_passport→passports, profile_identification→identity_records, profile_emergency_contact→emergency_contacts. Snapshot skipped for create/delete actions and non-employee callers (current_data stays NULL). (3) Recreate vw_wf_pending_tasks with LEFT JOIN workflow_pending_changes to expose current_data; LEFT JOIN ensures expense_reports tasks (wi.record_id = expense_reports.id, not a wpc.id) continue to return NULL for current_data. UI: ProfileEnrichment in ApproverInbox.tsx updated to SF-style diff — keeps existing grid layout, adds struck-through old value below the new value for any field where current_data[key] ≠ proposed_data[key]. Fields with no change show nothing extra. useWorkflowTasks hook maps r.current_data with (r as any) cast until type regen is run.
177Employee submission comment threading — wire comment text from WorkflowSubmitModal through to workflow_action_log.notes. Root cause: executeGatedSubmit() received the comment but dropped it (parameter was named _comment). Fix: (1) wf_submit() gains p_comment text DEFAULT NULL — stored as NULLIF(trim(p_comment),'') in the 'submitted' action_log row. (2) submit_change_request() gains p_comment text DEFAULT NULL — passes through to wf_submit. Both use DEFAULT NULL so all existing callers are unaffected. (3) MyProfile/index.tsx: submitViaWorkflow() gains comment? 4th param, passes p_comment to RPC; executeGatedSubmit renamed from _comment to comment. UI: Approver Inbox Prior Notes callout already reads workflow_action_log.notes — employee's submission comment now appears automatically with no UI changes. No schema changes — workflow_action_log.notes already existed.
178Broaden profiles_select RLS — add third path: employee_details.view. Root cause: Approver Inbox Reassign typeahead searched employees (step 1, worked) then looked up profile IDs via profiles.employee_id (step 2, returned empty). profiles_select was self-only + admin, so non-admin approvers couldn't read other users' profiles. Fix: DROP + recreate profiles_select with OR (employee_id IS NOT NULL AND user_can('employee_details','view',employee_id)). Any user who can see an employee via the Permission Matrix can also read their profiles row (only contains id, employee_id, is_active — no sensitive data). Mirrors employees_select pattern exactly. Admin and self-only paths unchanged. Reassign typeahead in ApproverInbox now works for all non-admin approvers. Future features that need profile_id from employee searches also benefit automatically.
179Sent-Back Inbox support. Two fixes: (1) Rebuild vw_wf_my_requests to expose wi.metadata — the Sent Back tab's ExpenseEnrichment / ProfileEnrichment components need metadata (name, total_amount, proposed field values) but the view previously omitted it. (2) Broaden wf_withdraw() to accept 'awaiting_clarification' status in addition to 'in_progress', so a submitter can withdraw a sent-back request rather than being forced to resubmit.
180Update flow for sent-back requests. Three-part change: (1) Schema — add 'needs_update' to expense_status enum and workflow_pending_changes.status CHECK; update wf_sync_module_status to map 'needs_update' for profile_* modules. (2) New RPC wf_prepare_update(p_instance_id) — sets module record to 'needs_update' so the edit form can unlock fields; returns module_code + record_id for frontend navigation. (3) Patch wf_resubmit to re-lock the module record back to 'submitted'/'pending' after resuming the instance, closing both the dangling-draft gap and the re-lock gap.
181Allow wf_resubmit to update workflow_pending_changes.proposed_data. Adds optional p_proposed_data parameter: when provided, overwrites wpc.proposed_data, resets wpc.status to 'pending', then proceeds with normal resubmit (resume instance, new task, notify). Completes the Update & Resubmit path for profile_* modules. Expense reports (no wpc row) pass NULL — fully backward-compatible.
182Distinguish update vs. respond in the workflow action log. wf_resubmit now logs action = 'updated_and_resubmitted' when p_proposed_data is provided (employee changed field values) vs. 'resubmitted' when only a comment was added. Lets the approver see in the timeline exactly what happened.
183Fix "column reference module_code is ambiguous" in wf_prepare_update. PostgreSQL 42702 error: the RETURNS TABLE() output columns named module_code and record_id clashed with the unqualified column names in the SELECT INTO query. Fix: add table alias wi to the SELECT INTO query so all references are unambiguously qualified.
184Make wf_prepare_update module-agnostic. Previously called wf_sync_module_status(module_code, record_id, 'needs_update'), requiring an explicit branch per module. New approach: wf_prepare_update simply logs 'update_form_opened' and returns routing info. The frontend's existing awaiting_clarification check gates re-entry — no module-level status flip needed. Audit action label corrected from 'update_started' to 'update_form_opened'.
185Add updated_at to workflow_pending_changes. Migration 181 patched wf_resubmit to SET updated_at = now() when p_proposed_data is provided, but the column had never been added to the table. Fix: ADD COLUMN updated_at timestamptz; back-fill existing rows with created_at.
186Keep workflow_instances.metadata in sync on resubmit with proposed data. wf_resubmit previously only updated wpc.proposed_data, leaving wi.metadata pointing at original submitted values — the approver's Proposed Changes panel showed stale data after a resubmit. Fix: merge p_proposed_data into wi.metadata using the JSONB || operator, preserving non-field keys (e.g. "name") while overwriting changed field values.
187Drop the old 4-parameter overload of wf_submit. Migration 177 added p_comment DEFAULT NULL to wf_submit, but PostgreSQL CREATE OR REPLACE creates a second overload when the parameter list changes. Both the old 4-param and new 5-param versions coexisted, causing "function wf_submit is not unique" for calls without p_comment. Fix: DROP FUNCTION wf_submit(text, text, uuid, jsonb) — leaves only the canonical 5-parameter version.
188wf_resubmit — restart workflow from Step 1. Previous behaviour: resubmit re-assigned to the same step that requested clarification (e.g. Finance at Step 2), bypassing earlier approvers. New behaviour: reset current_step = 1 and create a new pending task for the Step 1 approver, so the full approval chain runs again — mirrors original submission behaviour.
189Add expense report name to workflow metadata snapshot. submit_expense was building the metadata JSONB without the report's name, so the Approver Inbox showed the workflow template name ("Expense Report Approval") instead of the actual report name. Fix: include 'name' in the jsonb snapshot. Backfill existing workflow_instances so already-submitted reports also show their name.
190line_items_update — replace broken expense.edit_approval gate with dynamic expense.edit + is_workflow_assignee check. The original 'expense.edit_approval' permission code was seeded into the old role_permissions table (dropped in mig 146) and was never migrated to the RBP system — so has_permission() always returned false for approvers, silently blocking note saves in WorkflowReview. New approver path: user_can('expense_reports','edit') AND is_workflow_assignee(). ESS and admin paths unchanged.
191Drop non-rbp line_items_update policy (the mig 190 artefact). The rebuilt policy still used has_permission() which reads the dropped role_permissions table — always errors or returns false. rbp_line_items_update (mig 084) already covers every case correctly via user_can(). Dropping line_items_update removes dead code and leaves rbp_line_items_update as the sole UPDATE policy. Frontend: WorkflowReview.tsx canEditMidFlight gate updated from can('expense.edit') → can('expense_reports.edit').
192Consolidate module_registry into module_codes + add edit_route column. Problem: two separate tables (module_codes = all modules; module_registry = attachment rules) made it impossible to add navigation config to profile_* modules (status_column NOT NULL in module_registry, but profile tables have no status column). Fix: add all module_registry columns (nullable) + new edit_route text to module_codes; copy expense_reports and time_off data; rewrite can_view_module_record() / can_write_module_record() to read from module_codes; seed edit_route for expense_reports → '/expense/report/:id'; DROP module_registry. Profile modules leave attachment columns NULL and edit_route = NULL (Pattern B inline edit — intentional). Note: mig 223 later overwrote these NULLs with ESS self-service paths, causing a regression (fixed in mig 333).
193wf_approver_update_pending_changes RPC. Allows an active-step approver to overwrite workflow_pending_changes.proposed_data when the step has allow_edit = true. SECURITY DEFINER function enforces three guards: (1) caller is the pending task assignee for the instance, (2) step.allow_edit = true, (3) instance is in_progress. Required because wpc_update RLS gates UPDATE on wf_manage.edit, which regular approvers don't hold.
194Enforce 1 000-character limit on workflow notes. (1) ADD CHECK constraints (NOT VALID) on workflow_tasks.notes, workflow_action_log.notes, and line_items.note. (2) Add char_length guard at the top of wf_approve, wf_reject, wf_return_to_initiator, and wf_return_to_previous_step RPCs. NOT VALID skips a full table scan — constraint applies to new/updated rows only. wf_bulk_approve inherits the guard via wf_approve delegation.
195Auto-migrate workflow_assignments on template publish. When admin publishes a new template version (v2), wf_publish_template now UPDATEs every active workflow_assignments row pointing at any older version of the same template code to reference the new template id. Fixes broken Manage Assignments UI (dropdown showed "— select workflow —" for assignments referencing deactivated old versions). In-flight workflow_instances are unaffected (they store template_id as a snapshot). Audit trail preserved via wa_audit_trigger.
196get_workflow_participants() — resolve SELF type to submitter name. SELF-type steps (typically CC/notification steps that copy the submitter) were falling through to ELSE in the resolvedName CASE, showing the raw step name ("Employee") instead of the submitter's actual name. Fix: add self_emp LATERAL join (profiles → employees WHERE sp.id = p_profile_id); add WHEN 'SELF' branches to resolvedName, resolvedDesignation, and hasResolvedPerson. Degrades gracefully to 'You' when p_profile_id is NULL.
197Add step_allow_edit to vw_wf_pending_tasks. ApproverInbox.tsx was fetching workflow_steps.allow_edit with a separate client query that required wf_templates.view — approvers without that permission got null → Update button never rendered. Fix: recreate vw_wf_pending_tasks adding ws.allow_edit AS step_allow_edit (the view already JOINs workflow_steps, so this is free). ApproverInbox can now read step_allow_edit directly from the hook with no extra RLS risk.
198vw_wf_pending_tasks — use live proposed_data as metadata. wf_approver_update_pending_changes (mig 193) writes updated proposed_data to workflow_pending_changes, but the view exposed wi.metadata (snapshotted at submission time). Approvers re-loading a task after saving edits saw the original values. Fix: replace wi.metadata with COALESCE(wpc.proposed_data, wi.metadata) via LEFT JOIN on wpc.id = wi.record_id. Expense reports (record_id → expense_report row, not wpc) produce no match and fall back to wi.metadata — unchanged.
199vw_wf_my_requests — use live proposed_data as metadata. Same fix as mig 198 applied to the submitter-facing view. When an approver edited proposed_data then returned the item to the initiator, the Sent Back tab's ProfileEnrichment showed stale original values (vw_wf_my_requests.metadata = wi.metadata). Fix: COALESCE(wpc.proposed_data, wi.metadata) with LEFT JOIN on wpc.id = wi.record_id. Expense reports unaffected.
200Multi-approver step support — schema. (1) ADD workflow_steps.approval_mode text column (NULL = single approver default; 'ALL_OF' = all co-approvers must approve; 'ANY_OF' = first to approve completes). (2) CREATE workflow_step_approvers junction table: one row per co-approver in a multi-approver step, with approver_type / approver_role / approver_profile_id / sort_order columns mirroring workflow_steps. Not consulted when approval_mode IS NULL — all existing steps unaffected.
201Multi-approver engine. (1) New internal helper wf_resolve_approver_ex — resolves approver from explicit type/role/profile params (not a step row); used by the multi-approver fan-out loop. (2) wf_advance_instance — when approval_mode IS NOT NULL loops over workflow_step_approvers, resolves each co-approver, creates one pending task per resolved approver (skips submitter and null). (3) wf_approve — ANY_OF: first approver cancels all siblings then advances; ALL_OF: advance only after last sibling approves. (4) wf_return_to_initiator — cancels all sibling tasks on send-back. (5) wf_resubmit — delegates step-1 task creation to wf_advance_instance so both single and multi-approver step-1s are handled correctly.
202get_workflow_participants() — multi-approver support. Adds two optional fields: approvalMode ('ANY_OF'|'ALL_OF'|null) and coApprovers JSON array [{approverType, resolvedName, resolvedDesignation}] populated for multi-approver steps from a correlated sub-select over workflow_step_approvers. Single-approver steps return null for both fields — fully backward-compatible. Frontend WorkflowSubmitModal uses coApprovers to render the multi-approver routing chain.
203ROLE fan-out in multi-approver mode. In mig 201 wf_advance_instance called wf_resolve_approver_ex (LIMIT 1) for ROLE-type co-approver entries, returning only one role holder even when multiple exist. Fix: detect v_co.approver_type = 'ROLE' in the multi-approver loop and iterate over ALL active user_roles holders, creating one task per holder with delegation applied individually. Non-ROLE co-approver types (SPECIFIC_USER, MANAGER etc.) unchanged.
204Simplify approval_mode — Option A. After UX review, the three-state model (null / ANY_OF / ALL_OF) is simplified to two: null (default, ROLE fan-out with first-to-approve wins for ROLE type; single task for all other types) and 'ALL_OF' (all role holders must approve). Changes: (1) Convert existing 'ANY_OF' rows to NULL; tighten CHECK to only allow 'ALL_OF' or NULL. (2) Delete all workflow_step_approvers rows (table kept for rollback compatibility). (3) Rewrite wf_advance_instance — ROLE type always fans out to all holders regardless of approval_mode; workflow_step_approvers loop removed. (4) Rewrite wf_approve — NULL mode: cancel siblings then advance; ALL_OF: advance only when last sibling approved; ANY_OF branch removed. (5) get_workflow_participants coApprovers subquery removed (always returns null).
205get_workflow_participants() — roleMembers array for ROLE steps. Adds roleMembers JSON array [{name, jobTitle}] to each ROLE-type step in the RPC output. Populated by a correlated sub-select over user_roles → roles → profiles → employees filtered to active, non-expired members of the approver_role code. Non-ROLE steps return null. UI: ≤4 members render as stacked initials avatars; 5+ members show a generic role icon with member count badge; both modes show a fixed-position hover tooltip listing all members and the approval mode footer. STACKED_LIMIT = 4 in WorkflowSubmitModal's RoleGroupBubble component.
206–288Bank Accounts module (phases 1–4), hire review enhancements, additional satellite table audit coverage, profile change history permissions, and miscellaneous RLS + RPC hardening. See migs 273–288 in the Bank Accounts section (Part 11).
289Employee Dependents module — core schema. Creates employee_dependents (effective-dated with partial UNIQUE on dependent_code WHERE effective_to='9999-12-31', BEFORE INSERT trigger fn_generate_dependent_code() generates {emp_code}_DEP_{NN} codes) and employee_dependent_attachments tables. Seeds RPCs: get_employee_dependents, upsert_dependent, remove_dependent (direct-write paths). Seeds permissions: dependents.view (140), dependents.create (141), dependents.edit (142), dependents.delete (143). Seeds module code profile_dependents = 'Profile – Dependents'. Seeds picklists DEPENDENT_RELATIONSHIP_TYPE (DR001–DR008) and DEPENDENT_DOCUMENT_TYPE (DD001–DD007). RLS Path D on both tables.
290Dependents workflow staging. Upgrades upsert_dependent and remove_dependent to the dual-path pattern: PATH A — no workflow template → direct write to employee_dependents; PATH B — workflow template found → stage in workflow_pending_changes, call wf_submit(). Adds profile_dependents ELSIF branch to apply_profile_pending_change (the AFTER UPDATE trigger on workflow_pending_changes) to handle add/amend/remove operations when a pending change is approved.
291get_profile_workflow_gates() — add profile_bank and profile_dependents. Adds 'profile_bank' and 'profile_dependents' to the v_module_codes array in all three query blocks (gate check loop, pending counts, instance_ids). The function now covers 9 profile modules. Fixes: MyProfile "Workflow Pending Approval" banner and pendingCounts never appearing for bank/dependents changes.
292Fix dependent attachment FK. Corrects the employee_dependent_attachments.dependent_id foreign key to reference employee_dependents.id ON DELETE SET NULL (nullable, allows attachments without a resolved dependent row during workflow staging).
293Dependents permission action column alignment. Ensures the dependents.view/create/edit/delete permission rows have the correct action column values matching the CHECK constraint pattern on the permissions table.
294Bank and dependents change history permission. Seeds bank_dependents_history module + view/edit permissions, enabling a dedicated history tab for bank account and dependent change audit logs.
295Hire review — dependents section. Adds a Dependents section to the AddEmployee hire-review workflow so HR can enter initial dependent information during the hire pipeline before the employee goes Active.
296Bank/dependents RLS personal_info_pattern alignment. Re-aligns the RLS policies on employee_dependents and employee_bank_accounts to use the personal_info_pattern (self-access OR user_can path D), consistent with passports and identity_records.
297Fix hire review — dependents section rendering. Patches a frontend-data-shape bug in the AddEmployee hire-review dependents section introduced in mig 295 (field name mismatch between the RPC response shape and the form field accessors).
298Fix dependent relationship label hide code. Corrects a picklist value display bug where the DEPENDENT_RELATIONSHIP_TYPE code prefix was rendered alongside the label in the DependentsPortlet relationship column.
299Bank account effective-date rules. Three-part change: (1) upsert_bank_account — standard ESS users may only submit on days 1–15; effective_from locked to 1st of current month; new hires bypass date rules. (2) apply_profile_pending_change (profile_bank branch) — approvers blocked after day 20 unless bank_exceptions/admin/hr. (3) get_profile_workflow_gates — adds is_bank_exception boolean for frontend banner.
300–319Miscellaneous RLS/RPC fixes — bank amend close-out guard, ordinal day messaging, no-overlap constraint, account number uniqueness, personal info sync RPCs.
320Set-snapshot schema — dependents. Creates employee_dependent_set (parent, effective-dated) and employee_dependent_item (child, stable dependent_code) tables with partial-unique index enforcing one active set per employee and one item per code per set. RLS mirrors the personal_info_pattern on both tables.
321apply_profile_pending_change — dependents set branch. Replaces the old per-row add/amend/remove branch with a call to fn_apply_dependent_set_transition, which closes the current set and inserts the new one atomically.
322Dependent set RPCs. Introduces submit_dependent_set (dual-path: PATH A direct write via fn_apply_dependent_set_transition, PATH B workflow staging), get_employee_dependent_set (active set + items + attachments as of a date), get_employee_dependent_set_history (all sets newest-first), and fn_apply_dependent_set_transition (idempotent set transition, assigns dependent_code to new items).
323Dependents backfill. Migrates existing active rows from employee_dependents into the new set-snapshot tables. One active set per employee; one item per active dependent. Validation: every legacy active row has a matching item. Aborts in transaction if any check fails. NOTICEs: 2 employees → 2 sets → 2 items.
324–327Personal info gap fixes and sync RPC corrections (unrelated to set-snapshot).
328Set-snapshot schema — bank accounts. Creates employee_bank_account_set and employee_bank_account_item tables, mirroring the dependent set pattern. Partial-unique index enforces one active set per employee; secondary unique index enforces exactly one is_primary item per set. Country-specific CHECK constraints (IND→IFSC, PAK/SAU→IBAN, LKA→branch_code) preserved. RLS: same two-path pattern (Path A target-group, Path D hire-pipeline).
329Bank account set RPCs. Introduces submit_bank_account_set (with 15th-submission and bank_exceptions/admin/hr exempt logic), get_employee_bank_account_set, get_employee_bank_account_set_history, and fn_apply_bank_account_set_transition.
330apply_profile_pending_change — bank set branch. Replaces the old per-account branch with fn_apply_bank_account_set_transition; 20th-of-month approver cutoff enforced at this point.
331Bank accounts backfill. Active-set only (no historical reconstruction). One set per employee; one item per active legacy account. Validation: every active legacy account has a matching set item; active set count ≥ legacy active employee count.
332Phase 6 cleanup. Renames employee_dependents → employee_dependents_legacy and employee_bank_accounts → employee_bank_accounts_legacy. Drops legacy RPCs: get_employee_dependents, upsert_dependent, remove_dependent, get_employee_bank_accounts, upsert_bank_account. Drops the now-dead FK on employee_dependent_attachments (joins on dependent_code henceforth). _legacy tables kept ≥ 2 weeks for rollback; scheduled for DROP ≥ 2026-06-14.
333Fix profile_* module edit_route — approver Update button was navigating to the approver's own My Profile instead of opening inline edit. Root cause: mig 223 bulk-registered profile modules with ESS self-service paths (/profile/personal etc.) as their edit_route, overwriting the deliberate NULLs set by mig 192. Pattern A (navigate to edit form) is wrong for profile change approvals — approvers must use Pattern B (inline edit in the inbox panel). Fix: SET edit_route = NULL for all 9 profile_* module codes. Frontend guard also added (PROFILE_INLINE_MODULES set in ApproverInbox.tsx) so the inbox always uses Pattern B for profile modules regardless of DB value.

Overall Assessment

Prowess is built on a security-first foundation that is unusually sophisticated for a startup-stage HRIS. The three-layer enforcement model, audit immutability, and generic workflow engine would not look out of place in a Series B enterprise product. Legacy dead code (role_permissions table, sync triggers, hardcoded ESS seeds) has been removed — user_can() has a clean two-path structure backed solely by permission_set_assignments, and the Permission Matrix UI is the single source of truth for all permission configuration. Every expense_reports policy routes through user_can() Path D; access scope is fully controlled by target groups rather than hardcoded role checks.

Migrations 167–175 completed the profile-change workflow path end-to-end: ESS users can submit personal data changes for approval, approvers see their inbox correctly, and mid-flight editing is gated per step config. Two latent RLS circular dependency bugs were discovered and fixed (mig 174): workflow_tasks ↔ workflow_instances cross-table EXISTS subqueries resolved by SECURITY DEFINER helper functions. Migration 176 added current_data snapshots to workflow_pending_changes for SF-style before/after diffs. Migration 177 threaded employee submission comments through to workflow_action_log.notes. Migration 178 broadened profiles_select RLS to fix the Approver Inbox Reassign typeahead for non-admin approvers.

Migrations 179–199 completed the Sent-Back / Update & Resubmit flow. Migration 179 exposed metadata in vw_wf_my_requests and broadened wf_withdraw to accept awaiting_clarification status. Migrations 180–188 built the full update-after-sendback cycle: wf_prepare_update (module-agnostic form unlock), wf_resubmit with p_proposed_data (update proposed values and restart from Step 1), wf_sync_module_status status mapping corrections, and PostgreSQL overload cleanup (mig 187 dropped the stale 4-param wf_submit). Migration 189 added the expense report name to workflow metadata snapshots. Migrations 190–191 fixed the mid-flight line-item edit gate (dropped the broken expense.edit_approval permission path; rbp_line_items_update is now the sole UPDATE policy). Migration 192 consolidated module_registry into module_codes and added the edit_route column, enabling all modules to carry navigation config. Migration 193 added wf_approver_update_pending_changes — a SECURITY DEFINER RPC allowing approvers to edit proposed_data mid-flight when allow_edit = true. Migration 194 enforced 1 000-char limits on all workflow note columns with NOT VALID CHECK constraints and RPC guards. Migration 195 auto-migrates workflow_assignments when a template is republished, fixing the broken assignment selector after version bumps. Migration 196 resolved SELF-type steps to the submitter's real name in get_workflow_participants. Migrations 197–199 eliminated stale-metadata issues: vw_wf_pending_tasks now exposes step_allow_edit directly (no RLS-gated nested query) and both pending-tasks and my-requests views use COALESCE(wpc.proposed_data, wi.metadata) so approver edits are immediately visible.

Migrations 200–205 delivered the multi-approver engine and then simplified it to a clean two-state model (Option A). Migration 200 added the approval_mode column to workflow_steps (NULL / ANY_OF / ALL_OF) and the workflow_step_approvers junction table. Migration 201 wired the full multi-approver fan-out into wf_advance_instance (one task per resolved co-approver), ANY_OF/ALL_OF completion logic into wf_approve, and sibling-task cancellation into wf_return_to_initiator. Migration 202 added a coApprovers JSON array to get_workflow_participants for the submit-modal routing preview. Migration 203 extended ROLE fan-out inside the multi-approver branch to all active role holders rather than just the first one. Migration 204 (Option A simplification) collapsed the model to two states — null (ROLE fans out, first to approve wins; all other types single-task) and ALL_OF (all role holders must approve). ANY_OF rows converted to null, CHECK constraint tightened, workflow_step_approvers cleared (table kept for rollback), engine rewritten without the co-approver loop. Migration 205 added a roleMembers JSONB array for ROLE-type steps in get_workflow_participants, enabling the WorkflowSubmitModal to render stacked initials avatars (≤4 members) or a generic role icon with count badge (5+ members), both with a fixed-position hover tooltip showing all members and approval mode. The workflow engine is now production-ready for multi-step approvals with role-based routing and configurable fan-out semantics.

Migrations 320–332 (2026-05-29) replaced the per-row effective-dating model for both Dependents and Bank Accounts with a parent-child set-snapshot pattern (modelled after SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central). The key motivation: adding 4 dependents previously required 4 separate workflow submissions; under the new model the employee edits their entire dependent configuration in one session, submits once, and the approver sees a single diff card (NEW / AMENDED / REMOVED / UNCHANGED per item). The same architecture applies to bank accounts. On the DB side: employee_dependent_set + employee_dependent_item (mig 320) and employee_bank_account_set + employee_bank_account_item (mig 328) hold the new data; fn_apply_*_set_transition handles the atomic set-close + new-set-insert; partial-unique indexes enforce one active set per employee and one primary account per set. Backfill migrations 323 and 331 migrated all existing active rows into the new tables. Frontend rewrites: DependentsPortlet and BankAccountsPortlet now use view / draft / pending / history modes; DependentsEnrichment and BankEnrichment in ApproverInbox are read-only set-diff viewers; WorkflowReview profile_dependents and profile_bank sections show per-item diff cards. Migration 332 cleaned up: renamed legacy tables to _legacy, dropped all five legacy RPCs, dropped the dead employee_dependent_attachments FK. Legacy tables scheduled for DROP ≥ 2026-06-14.

Migrations 289–299 delivered the Employee Dependents module and hardened the Bank Accounts effective-date rules. Migration 289 created the employee_dependents and employee_dependent_attachments tables with a BEFORE INSERT trigger that auto-generates dependent codes ({emp_code}_DEP_{NN}), seeded picklists (DEPENDENT_RELATIONSHIP_TYPE, DEPENDENT_DOCUMENT_TYPE), and introduced the profile_dependents module code. Migration 290 upgraded the dependent RPCs to the dual-path workflow pattern (direct write or workflow_pending_changes staging) and wired profile_dependents into apply_profile_pending_change. Migration 291 added both profile_bank and profile_dependents to get_profile_workflow_gates(), completing the 9-module gate coverage. Migrations 292–298 applied incremental fixes: attachment FK correction, permission action column alignment, bank/dependents history permission, hire-review dependents section, personal_info_pattern RLS alignment, and picklist label rendering fixes. Migration 299 formalized the bank effective-date rules: ESS submit window (days 1–15), effective_from locked to 1st of current month, approver gate after day 20, new-hire bypass, and the is_bank_exception flag added to get_profile_workflow_gates() for frontend banner logic.

Part 8 — Permission Matrix Reference

What each permission code controls — which screen it gates, which DB tables it's enforced on, and what each action does. Derived from PermissionMatrix.tsx and all migrations through 159. Last overwriting migration noted for each code.

Legend: Path D — scoped to target group (employee_id) Path B — module-level, no target group (NULL) Frontend-only gate — no DB RLS (route guard only) Lookup — read-only reference data access
👁 Employee View — Path D (target-group scoped)
Permission Code Actions Screen / Route DB Tables Enforced On What each action does
expense_reports
Path D		 view
create
edit
delete
history		 My Expenses / Expense list — /admin/expenses expense_reports, expense_line_items view — SELECT own + target-group expense reports
create — INSERT new expense report for target employee
edit — UPDATE expense report (submit, update details)
delete — DELETE expense report
history — see audit trail via get_record_history() RPC
Mig 084 initial; mig 123 dropped legacy line policies; mig 145 added audit triggers
employee_details
Path D		 view
edit		 Employee list, Employee profile — /admin/employees and all employee detail sub-tabs employees (Active status rows) view — SELECT active employee rows within target group; sets the scope for all info portlets below
edit — UPDATE active employee core fields (name, status, department, etc.)
Note: admin-only delete and history are separate items in the Admin section. Mig 095, 098 (final RLS).
personal_info
Path D		 view
edit		 Personal Info tab on employee profile employee_personal view — SELECT personal details (legal name, gender, DOB) for target employees
edit — INSERT / UPDATE personal info record
Mig 095 initial; history gated to personal_info.history on employee_audit_log
contact_info
Path D		 view
edit		 Contact tab on employee profile employee_contact view — SELECT contact details (work/personal email, phone) for target employees
edit — INSERT / UPDATE contact record
Mig 095 initial
employment
Path D		 view
edit		 Employment tab on employee profile employee_employment view — SELECT job title, department, employment type, contract type, start date
edit — INSERT / UPDATE employment record
Mig 095 initial
address
Path D		 view
edit		 Address tab on employee profile employee_addresses view — SELECT home, postal and work addresses for target employees
edit — INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE address records
Mig 095 initial
passport
Path D		 view
edit		 Passport tab on employee profile passports view — SELECT passport number, issuing country, expiry, visa info
edit — INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE passport records
Mig 095 initial
identity_documents
Path D		 view
edit		 Identity Docs tab on employee profile identity_records view — SELECT national ID, driving licence and other government IDs
edit — INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE identity records
Mig 095 initial
emergency_contacts
Path D		 view
edit		 Emergency Contact tab on employee profile emergency_contacts view — SELECT emergency contact names, relationships, phone numbers
edit — INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE emergency contact records
Mig 095 initial
🔀 Employee View — Feature Toggles (Frontend-only, no target group)
Permission Code Action Screen / Route DB Enforcement Notes
org_chart
Frontend gate only		 view Org Chart — /admin/emp-org-chart and /admin/org-chart Route guard only — no RLS on org chart tables. Reads from employees + department_heads which have their own policies. Feature on/off toggle. No target group. Mig 082 + 097 (seeded permission).
wf_my_requests
Frontend gate only		 view My Requests — /workflow/my-requests Route guard only. Shows vw_wf_my_requests view which filters by submitted_by = auth.uid() at the underlying table level. Shows the user's own submitted workflow requests and their status. No target group. Mig 108.
wf_inbox
Path B (module-level)		 view Approver Inbox — /workflow/inbox
Workflow Review — /workflow/review/:id
My Delegations — /workflow/delegations		 Route guard on all three routes. Also gates INSERT/UPDATE on wf_delegations (self-service delegation — only approvers can delegate rights they hold). No RLS on inbox itself — task visibility is via assigned_to = auth.uid(). Required to be an approver. Also controls self-service delegation (mig 156 — delegators must hold wf_inbox.view). No target group. Mig 108 seeded; mig 150 + 156 tightened.
🔍 Reference Lookups — Path B (read-only dropdown data)
Permission Code What it unlocks DB Tables / Views
projects.lookupPopulates the Project dropdown in expense formsprojects, vw_projects_lookup
currencies.lookupPopulates the Currency dropdown in expense formscurrencies, vw_currencies_lookup
picklists.lookupPopulates all picklist dropdowns across forms (employment type, contract type, etc.)picklists, picklist_values, vw_picklist_values_lookup
departments.lookupPopulates the Department dropdown in employee and expense formsdepartments, vw_departments_lookup

All lookup permissions use action = 'lookup', Path B (NULL target). Separate from management permissions — a user can have departments.lookup without departments.view (manage). Mig 147.

⚙️ Admin — Employee Management (Path D for hire/inactive, Path B for departments/ref)
Permission Code Actions Screen / Route DB Tables What each action does
hire_employee
Path D		 view
create
edit
delete
history		 New hire pipeline screen — /admin/employees/hire employees (Draft / Incomplete status rows) view — SELECT Draft/Incomplete employee records
create — INSERT new Draft employee (submit hire)
edit — UPDATE pending hire record before activation
delete — DELETE Draft/Incomplete record (cancel hire)
history — audit trail for hire pipeline via employee_audit_log
Mig 082 seeded; mig 097 added history action; mig 098 final RLS
employee_details
(Admin section)
Path D		 delete
history		 Employee list admin actions — hard delete and change history tab employees (Active rows) + employee_audit_log delete — hard DELETE active employee record
history — SELECT from employee_audit_log for active employee rows
Same module code as EV employee_details. Admin section exposes only delete + history actions. Mig 097 added history permission; mig 098 + 100 final RLS.
inactive_employees
Path D		 view
create
edit
delete
history		 Inactive employees list — /admin/employees/inactive employees (Inactive status rows) + employee_audit_log view — SELECT inactive employee rows within target group
create — deactivate: UPDATE active → Inactive
edit — reactivate: UPDATE inactive → Active
delete — hard DELETE inactive employee record
history — audit trail for inactive employee rows
Mig 097 seeded module + permissions; mig 098 final RLS
departments
Path B (NULL)		 view
create
edit
delete		 Department management — /admin/departments departments view — SELECT all departments
create / edit — INSERT / UPDATE department records
delete — DELETE department
Note: departments.lookup is separate (read-only dropdown). view covers management screen; lookup covers form dropdowns. Mig 134 (final RLS).
picklists
Path B (NULL)		 view
create
edit
delete		 Picklist management — /admin/picklists picklists, picklist_values view — SELECT all picklists and their values (management screen)
create / edit — INSERT / UPDATE picklists and values
delete — DELETE picklists and values
picklists.lookup is separate — read-only for form dropdowns. Mig 137 (final RLS).
projects_mgmt
Path B (NULL)		 view
create
edit
delete		 Project management — /admin/projects projects view — SELECT all projects (management screen)
create / edit — INSERT / UPDATE projects
delete — DELETE projects
projects.lookup is separate — read-only for expense form dropdown. Mig 138 (final RLS).
exchange_rates_mgmt
Path B (NULL)		 view
create
edit
delete		 Exchange rate management — /admin/exchange-rates exchange_rates view — SELECT all exchange rates
create / edit — INSERT / UPDATE currency rates
delete — DELETE rate entries
Mig 136 (final RLS).
🔐 Admin Access & Security — Path B (module-level)
Permission Code Actions Screen / Route DB Tables Enforced On Notes
sec_admin_access
Frontend gate only		 view Entire /admin/* area — top-level gate Route guard only — no DB RLS. Without this, all other admin permissions are unreachable via the UI. The single on/off switch to the admin area. All other admin permissions assume this is also granted. Mig 091.
sec_role_assignments
Path B (NULL)		 view
edit		 Role Assignments — /admin/permissions/assignments user_roles, roles (non-system only for delete/create), audit_log (scoped to user_roles + permission_set_* entity types only — mig 159) view — see all role members + role-assignment history from audit_log
edit — add/remove users from roles; create/delete custom (non-system) roles
Mig 125 (initial RLS); mig 149 split sec_permission_matrix out; mig 159 scoped audit_log access.
sec_permission_matrix
Path B (NULL)		 view
edit		 Permission Matrix — /admin/permissions/matrix permission_sets, permission_set_items, permission_set_assignments view — open and read the Permission Matrix tab
edit — save changes to permission sets, items and assignments
Introduced mig 149 (split from sec_permission_catalog). Previously permission_catalog.edit incorrectly gated matrix writes.
sec_target_groups
Path B (NULL)		 view
edit		 Target Groups — /admin/permissions/target-groups target_groups (non-system rows only for edit/delete) view — see all target groups and their criteria
edit — create, modify and delete custom (non-system) groups. System groups (everyone, self, direct_l1, etc.) cannot be modified.
Mig 124 (final RLS).
sec_permission_catalog
Frontend gate only		 view Permission Catalog — /admin/permissions/catalog Route guard only. Read-only reference view of all permissions — reads from permissions and modules tables which have their own super-admin / authenticated policies. View-only catalog of all permission codes, grouped by module. No write access. Mig 091 seeded; mig 126 RLS; mig 149 separated from sec_permission_matrix.
sec_rbp_troubleshoot
Frontend gate only		 view RBP Troubleshooting — /admin/permissions/rbp Route guard only. Calls get_target_population() and get_my_permissions() SECURITY DEFINER RPCs — no direct table access needed. Inspect what permissions any user currently has and trace why. Calls SECURITY DEFINER functions so no extra RLS grants needed. Mig 071, 091.
🔄 Workflow Admin — Path B (module-level, NULL target)
Permission Code Actions Screen / Route DB Tables Enforced On What each action does
wf_manage
Path B (NULL)		 view
edit		 Workflow Operations — /admin/workflow/operations workflow_instances (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE), workflow_pending_changes (UPDATE/DELETE), workflow_tasks (SELECT — see all tasks) view — SELECT all workflow instances + pending changes + tasks
edit — trigger / cancel / configure running workflows (UPDATE/DELETE instances and pending changes)
Mig 129 initial; mig 153 fixed SELECT RLS on templates/steps/step_conditions; mig 157 fixed pending changes approver visibility.
wf_templates
Path B (NULL)		 view
edit		 Manage Workflow Templates — /admin/workflow/templates workflow_templates, workflow_steps, workflow_step_conditions view — SELECT all templates, steps and conditions
edit — INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE templates, steps, conditions
Mig 128 initial; mig 153 fixed SELECT policy — previously templates were readable by any authenticated user.
wf_notification_config
Path B (NULL)		 view
edit		 Manage Notifications — /admin/workflow/notification-config workflow_notification_templates (write operations only — SELECT is open to authenticated) view — open the Manage Notifications tab and read template list
edit — INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE notification templates
Introduced mig 152 — split from wf_notifications.edit which previously controlled template writes. wf_notifications now only covers the notification queue monitor.
wf_delegations
Path B (NULL)		 view
edit		 Manage Delegations (Admin) — /admin/workflow/delegations wf_delegations (admin: all rows; delegates always see their own rows regardless) view — SELECT all delegation rules (admin oversight)
edit — INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE any delegation entry
Distinct from wf_inbox which controls self-service delegation by approvers. Mig 132 initial; mig 156 tightened self-service to require wf_inbox.view.
wf_assignments
Path B (NULL)		 view
edit		 Manage Assignments — /admin/workflow/assignments workflow_assignments, workflow_tasks (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE), workflow_assignment_audit (SELECT) view — SELECT workflow role assignments and assignment audit log
edit — INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE assignments and tasks
Mig 131 (final RLS).
wf_performance
Frontend gate only		 view Performance dashboard — /admin/workflow/performance Route guard only. Dashboard reads from workflow_instances + workflow_tasks via views. No dedicated RLS. View-only: cycle times, SLA metrics, bottleneck analysis. Mig 091.
wf_analytics
Frontend gate only		 view Analytics — /admin/workflow/analytics Route guard only. No dedicated DB RLS. View-only: volume trends, approval rates, turnaround distributions. Mig 091.
wf_notifications
Path B (NULL)		 view Notification Monitor — /admin/workflow/notifications workflow_notification_queue (SELECT only). Note: write policies also reference wf_notifications.edit but this is now dead — all writes go via SECURITY DEFINER functions. view — SELECT from notification queue monitor (sent, pending, failed events)
Template management moved to wf_notification_config.edit (mig 152). wf_notifications.view is now purely the monitor queue. Mig 133 initial; mig 152 split.
⏱ Jobs & Reports
Permission Code Actions Screen / Route DB Tables Notes
jobs_manage
Path B (NULL)		 view Scheduled Jobs monitor — /admin/jobs job_run_log (SELECT only) Monitor pg_cron job execution health, success/failure rates. Writes go via SECURITY DEFINER job functions only. Mig 141 upgraded from legacy admin role check.
reports_admin
Frontend gate only		 view
(generate)		 Reports — /admin/reports and /admin/reports/expense-analytics Route guard only. Reads from expense_reports and other tables via existing RLS — no separate reports RLS. view (reports_admin.view) — access the Reports screen
generate (reports_admin.generate) — export / download reports
Seeded early; no dedicated DB RLS.

⚠️ Important Distinctions & Common Gotchas

wf_notifications vs wf_notification_config
wf_notifications.view = notification queue monitor (read-only operational view). wf_notification_config.view/edit = template management screen. Template write RLS was moved from wf_notifications.edit → wf_notification_config.edit in mig 152.
employee_details appears twice
Same permission code in both EV and Admin sections. EV exposes view + edit (active employees). Admin exposes delete + history only. Both hit the employees table (Active rows). The actions available per section are different subsets.
departments.view vs departments.lookup
departments.view gates the management screen (CRUD). departments.lookup gates read-only access to department dropdown in forms. A user can have lookup without view. Same pattern for picklists, projects, currencies.
wf_inbox vs wf_delegations
wf_inbox.view = approver permission; gates self-service delegation + review routes (non-admin). wf_delegations.view/edit = admin delegation management screen. After mig 156, self-service delegation INSERT/UPDATE requires wf_inbox.view (not just auth.uid()).
sec_permission_catalog vs sec_permission_matrix
sec_permission_catalog.view = read-only reference list of all permission codes. sec_permission_matrix.view/edit = the actual permission set builder. Before mig 149, permission_matrix writes were incorrectly gated on permission_catalog.edit — now cleanly separated.
sec_role_assignments.view + audit_log scope
After mig 159, sec_role_assignments.view only grants access to audit_log rows where entity_type IN ('user_roles', 'permission_set_assignments', 'permission_set_items'). Before mig 159, this permission gave unintended full audit_log read access.

Part 9 — Workflow Engine

End-to-end lifecycle of a workflow request — submit, approve, sendback, update & resubmit, ROLE fan-out, delegation, escalation, rejection, and withdrawal. Every RPC is SECURITY DEFINER. Notification codes shown at each trigger point. Reflects Option A approval model (mig 204): approval_mode is NULL (fan-out, first-to-approve wins) or 'ALL_OF' (every role holder must approve).

Instance Status State Machine
in_progress
wf_return_to_initiator() ↓          ↑ wf_resubmit()
awaiting_clarification
approved
wf_advance_instance()
all steps done
rejected
wf_reject()
withdrawn
wf_withdraw()
① Submit — wf_submit() / submit_expense() / submit_change_request()
Employee clicks Submit
  ↓
submit_expense(record_id)  — expense_reports module
submit_change_request(module, fields, comment?)  — profile_* modules
  Both call → wf_submit(template_code, module_code, record_id, metadata, comment?)  [SECURITY DEFINER]
  │
  ├── INSERT workflow_instances       status = 'in_progress' · metadata = {name, fields...}
  ├── INSERT workflow_action_log     action = 'submitted' · notes = comment (mig 177)
  ├── INSERT workflow_pending_changes  status = 'pending' · current_data snapshot (mig 176)
  └── → wf_advance_instance()  ← handles ROLE fan-out and all step logic (see ③ below)
Uniqueness guard: UNIQUE(module_code, record_id) WHERE status = 'in_progress' — prevents double-submission. A second submit while in_progress raises a constraint error.
Template snapshot: template_id is captured on the instance row at submit time — template changes after submission don't affect in-flight workflows.
current_data snapshot (mig 176): workflow_pending_changes.current_data is populated with to_jsonb() of the satellite row filtered to proposed_data keys — shown as before/after diff in the Approver Inbox.
Template Configuration Flags — workflow_templates
is_active boolean
Only one active version per code allowed (UNIQUE partial index). Controlled via Publish / Deactivate buttons — not a direct toggle. Draft templates have is_active = false and published_at = NULL.
skip_duplicate_approver boolean · default false · mig 164
Visible skip (Activity feed). Look-ahead, keep-last: step N is skipped when step N+1 resolves to the same approver — the approver acts on the last consecutive occurrence. Creates a workflow_tasks row with status='skipped' and acted_at=now() — the skipped step is visible in the Activity feed. Logs workflow_action_log action='skipped'. No notification sent. In a chain [user1, user1, user1, user2], steps 1 and 2 are skipped; step 3 is kept. The final step is never skipped (look-ahead finds nothing). Applies in both wf_advance_instance() and wf_submit().
UI: toggle (blue) on the template detail card · also in New Template modal
remove_duplicate_approver boolean · default false · mig 163
Silent step removal (invisible to end users). Same look-ahead, keep-last logic as skip: step N is removed when step N+1 resolves to the same approver. Does not create a workflow_tasks row — the step simply never appears in the workflow. Writes workflow_action_log with action='step_removed' and task_id=NULL for admin-level audit only. No notification sent. Also always removes any step where the resolved approver equals the submitter (Rule 1 — always active regardless of this flag). Applies in both wf_advance_instance() and wf_submit().
UI: toggle (purple) on the template detail card · also in New Template modal
version integer · auto-incremented
Each Clone creates version N+1 as a draft. Active instances snapshot the template_version at submit time — mid-flight workflows are unaffected by template changes.
effective_from date · nullable
Informational. Records the date from which this version is intended to apply. Does not automatically activate the template — activation is always manual via Publish.
Step Configuration — workflow_steps (per-step fields)
approval_mode — NULL (default)
ROLE type: fan-out — one task per active role holder; first to approve wins, cancels all sibling tasks. All other types: single task, advances immediately on approval.
approval_mode — 'ALL_OF'
ROLE type: every active role holder must approve before step advances. Each approval recorded individually; instance stays in_progress until last sibling approved. Single-person types behave identically to default (only 1 person).
allow_edit — boolean (default false)
When true, the active task assignee can edit proposed_data mid-flight via wf_approver_update_pending_changes(). The Update button is shown in WorkflowReview only when this flag is true and the approver holds the module's edit permission.
is_cc — boolean (default false)
CC / notification-only step. Task is created, notification sent, task auto-approved, instance advances immediately — no human action required.
Approver Resolution — wf_resolve_approver() / wf_advance_instance()
MANAGER
Submitter's direct manager via employees.manager_id. Single task always.
ROLE — Option A fan-out (mig 204)
All active, non-expired user_roles holders for the role code get a task. approval_mode = NULL: first to approve wins (sibling tasks cancelled). approval_mode = 'ALL_OF': every holder must approve. Role membership is resolved at task-creation time — adding a member takes effect on next submission. Delegation applied per-holder.
DEPT_HEAD
Head of the submitter's department. Single task always.
SPECIFIC_USER
Fixed profile_id set on the step definition. Single task always.
SELF
Submitter themselves. Delegation NEVER applied — always routes to submitter regardless of active delegations (e.g. acknowledgement steps). CC steps only in practice.
RULE_BASED
Evaluates workflow_step_conditions against record field values → resolves to a role code → fallback to MANAGER if no condition matches. Single task.
Delegation Chain (applied after all types except SELF, when step.allow_delegation = true)
Resolved approver = A
A has active delegation to B? → follow → B
B has active delegation to C? → follow → C
C has no delegation → final assignee = C

Max 5 hops (hard cap — prevents infinite loops)
template_id-specific delegations take priority over all-template
Re-evaluated fresh on every task creation (submit, resubmit, advance, return)
② Task Actions (Approver's choices)
✓ APPROVE — wf_approve(task_id, notes?)
Guards: task pending · instance in_progress · you're assigned (or wf_manage.edit)
notes max 1 000 chars (mig 194)
UPDATE workflow_tasks (this)  status = 'approved'
INSERT workflow_action_log  action = 'approved'
│
├── approval_mode = 'ALL_OF' && sibling tasks still pending?
│   → STOP — wait for remaining siblings
│
└── approval_mode = NULL or last ALL_OF sibling
    → UPDATE siblings status = 'cancelled' (fan-out cleanup)
    → wf_advance_instance()
↩ SENDBACK — wf_return_to_initiator(task_id, message)
Guards: task pending · instance in_progress · you're assigned (or admin)
message max 1 000 chars
UPDATE workflow_tasks (this)  status = 'returned'
UPDATE workflow_tasks (all siblings pending)  status = 'cancelled'
UPDATE workflow_instances  status = 'awaiting_clarification'
INSERT workflow_action_log  action = 'returned_to_initiator'
📧 wf.clarification_requested → Submitter
Submitter has two options in Sent Back tab:

Path A — Respond & Resume: add a clarification comment → wf_resubmit(instance_id, response)
Path B — Update & Edit: wf_prepare_update(instance_id) opens the original form → submitter edits fields → wf_resubmit(instance_id, response?, proposed_data)

Both paths: instance resets current_step = 1, creates new task for Step 1 approver (restart from top)
Action log: 'resubmitted' (Path A) or 'updated_and_resubmitted' (Path B)
📧 wf.clarification_submitted → Step 1 approver
✎ MID-FLIGHT EDIT — wf_approver_update_pending_changes(instance_id, proposed_data)
Guards: caller is pending task assignee · step.allow_edit = true · instance in_progress
UPDATE workflow_pending_changes  proposed_data = new_data · updated_at = now()
UPDATE workflow_instances  metadata = metadata || new_data (mig 186)
No instance status change — approver then Approves or Sends Back as normal
Available only when workflow_steps.allow_edit = true.
vw_wf_pending_tasks and vw_wf_my_requests use COALESCE(wpc.proposed_data, wi.metadata) so edited values appear immediately on reload.
↪ RETURN TO PREV STEP — wf_return_to_previous_step(task_id, reason)
Guards: task pending · step_order > 1 · you're assigned (or admin)
Finds prev step via action_log (handles skipped steps correctly)
UPDATE workflow_tasks  status = 'returned'
UPDATE workflow_instances  current_step = prev_step
wf_resolve_approver() → delegation re-applied
INSERT workflow_tasks  (new task for prev approver, fresh SLA)
INSERT workflow_action_log  action = 'returned_to_previous_step'
📧 wf.returned_to_previous_step → Previous approver
✗ REJECT — wf_reject(task_id, reason)
Guards: task pending · instance active · you're assigned (or wf_manage.edit)
reason must not be blank
UPDATE workflow_tasks (this)  status = 'rejected'
UPDATE workflow_tasks (all other pending)  status = 'cancelled'
UPDATE workflow_instances  status = 'rejected'
wf_sync_module_status() → source record status = 'rejected'
📧 wf.rejected → Submitter (includes reason + step)
⇄ REASSIGN — wf_reassign(task_id, new_profile_id, reason)
Guards: task pending · you're assigned OR admin/wf_manage.edit
If step.allow_delegation: follow chain on new_profile_id (up to 5 hops)
UPDATE workflow_tasks (old)  status = 'reassigned'
INSERT workflow_tasks (new)  assigned_to = final_assignee
INSERT workflow_action_log  action = 'reassigned'
   metadata: from/to/final_assignee/delegation_applied
📧 wf.task_assigned → New assignee (reassigned=true)
📧 wf.task_removed → Old assignee (only if admin reassigned)
⊘ WITHDRAW — wf_withdraw() / wf_withdraw_by_record()
Guards: you're the submitter (or admin)
        instance must be in_progress or awaiting_clarification
UPDATE workflow_tasks (all pending)  status = 'cancelled'
UPDATE workflow_instances  status = 'withdrawn'
wf_sync_module_status() → source record status = 'withdrawn'
INSERT workflow_action_log  action = 'withdrawn'
③ wf_advance_instance() — called after every approval or on submit
Find next active step where step_order > current AND is_active = true
  │
  ├── MORE STEPS REMAIN
  │    ├── CC step? (is_cc = true)
  │    │    → INSERT task · 📧 wf.task_assigned → CC recipient
  │    │    → Auto-mark approved · INSERT action_log ('cc_notified')
  │    │    → RECURSE → wf_advance_instance() again
  │    │
  │    └── Regular step — approver_type matters
  │        │
  │        ├── ROLE TYPE — Option A fan-out (mig 204)
  │        │    Fetch all active user_roles holders WHERE role.code = approver_role
  │        │    For each holder: apply delegation chain → resolve final_assignee
  │        │    Skip if final_assignee = submitted_by (initiator-as-approver rule)
  │        │    INSERT workflow_tasks (one per holder · due_at = now() + sla_hours)
  │        │    📧 wf.task_assigned → each holder
  │        │    If 0 tasks created → step silently skipped → RECURSE
  │        │    → STOP — wait for approvals (see wf_approve ② for completion logic)
  │        │
  │        └── All other types (MANAGER, DEPT_HEAD, SPECIFIC_USER, SELF, RULE_BASED)
  │            → wf_resolve_approver() (delegation re-applied fresh)
  │            │
  │            ├── TIER 1 — Initiator-as-approver removal (always active)
  │            │    resolved = submitted_by?
  │            │    → NO task · INSERT action_log ('step_removed', task_id=NULL) · RECURSE
  │            │
  │            ├── TIER 2A — remove_duplicate_approver flag (template opt-in)
  │            │    next step resolves to same approver?
  │            │    → NO task · INSERT action_log ('step_removed', task_id=NULL) · RECURSE
  │            │
  │            ├── TIER 2B — skip_duplicate_approver flag (template opt-in)
  │            │    next step resolves to same approver?
  │            │    → INSERT task status='skipped' acted_at=now() · INSERT action_log ('skipped') · RECURSE
  │            │
  │            └── Normal — INSERT workflow_tasks (due_at = now() + sla_hours)
  │                UPDATE workflow_instances current_step = next
  │                📧 wf.task_assigned → Next approver · STOP
  │
  └── LAST STEP — all steps approved
      → UPDATE workflow_instances  status = 'approved'
      → INSERT workflow_action_log  action = 'completed'
      → 📧 wf.completed → Submitter
      → wf_sync_module_status()
          expense_reports → UPDATE expense_reports  status/approved_at/approved_by
          profile_*       → UPDATE workflow_pending_changes  status=approved / resolved_at
                          └── fires trg_apply_profile_pending_change
                                → upserts satellite table row ✓
④ wf_resubmit() — after Sent Back, restart from Step 1
Guards: instance status = 'awaiting_clarification' · caller = submitted_by (or admin)
Double-submit guard: if already in_progress → no-op return
│
├── p_proposed_data provided? (Path B — Update & Edit)
│   UPDATE workflow_pending_changes  proposed_data = p_proposed_data · updated_at = now()
│   UPDATE workflow_instances        metadata = metadata || p_proposed_data
│   action = 'updated_and_resubmitted'
│
├── response only (Path A — Respond & Resume)
│   action = 'resubmitted'
│
├── INSERT workflow_action_log  action (above) · notes = response comment
├── UPDATE workflow_instances  status = 'in_progress' · current_step = 0 (← reset)
└── → wf_advance_instance()  ← picks up from step 1, fan-out applies as normal
⑤ SLA Escalation — automatic, every 30 minutes (pg_cron)
wf_escalate_overdue_tasks() runs every 30 min via pg_cron
  Finds: status = 'pending' AND due_at < now() AND escalated_at IS NULL
  (SKIP LOCKED — safe for concurrent runs)
  │
  For each overdue task:
  ├── 📧 wf.sla_reminder         → Approver  "You have an overdue task"
  ├── Resolve approver's manager via employees.manager_id
  ├── 📧 wf.sla_escalation        → Approver's manager  "X has not actioned Y"
  │    (silently skipped if no manager found)
  └── UPDATE workflow_tasks  escalated_at = now()  ← one-shot flag, never fires again for this task
⑥ Submit Modal Routing Preview — get_workflow_participants()
SECURITY DEFINER RPC called by useWorkflowParticipants hook before the employee submits. Bypasses RLS on workflow_steps, profiles, and employees — ESS users (no wf_templates.view) can still see the routing chain. Signature: get_workflow_participants(p_module_code text, p_profile_id uuid DEFAULT NULL).
Resolved fields per step
stepOrder · stepName · approverType · approverRole · isCC · approvalMode · resolvedName · resolvedDesignation · hasResolvedPerson
roleMembers (ROLE steps · mig 205)
Array of {name, jobTitle} for all active role holders. UI: ≤4 → stacked initials avatars; 5+ → generic icon + count badge. Hover tooltip always shows full list + approvalMode footer.
MANAGER / DEPT_HEAD resolution
When p_profile_id supplied (= auth.uid() from AuthContext), the RPC resolves the submitter's actual manager name + title. Without it: falls back to 'Direct Manager' / 'Resolved at submission time'.
SELF resolution (mig 196)
SELF-type steps (e.g. CC acknowledgement) resolve to the submitter's actual name when p_profile_id is provided. Degrades to 'You' without it.
🛠 Admin Setup — Multi-Step Template Configuration
Admins build workflow templates in the Workflows → Manage Templates UI. A template is a named, versioned sequence of ordered approval steps. Once published, templates are linked to modules via workflow_assignments. The engine resolves the correct template at submit time using resolve_workflow_for_submission().
① Create Template
Admin creates a new wf_templates row (name, description). Status is draft until published.
→ Template has version = 1, is_active = false, effective_from = NULL.
② Add & Order Steps
Each step is a row in workflow_steps with a 1-based step_order. The engine advances through steps in ascending step_order. Steps can be added in any order in the UI then dragged to re-sequence.
⚠ Constraint: UNIQUE(template_id, step_order) — no two steps on the same template can share an order number. Re-ordering in the UI must UPDATE all affected rows atomically.
③ Configure Each Step
approver_type
ROLE · MANAGER · DEPT_HEAD · SPECIFIC · SELF
Determines how the engine resolves the actual approver at run-time.
approver_role / approver_profile_id
Required when type = ROLE (role name) or SPECIFIC (fixed profile UUID). Leave NULL for dynamic types (MANAGER, DEPT_HEAD, SELF).
approval_mode
NULL (default) — ROLE fans out to all holders, first to approve wins. 'ALL_OF' — every active role holder must approve before step advances. Ignored for non-ROLE types.
sla_hours
Number of hours before pg_cron sends reminder + escalation to approver's manager. NULL = no SLA. Sets due_at on each task row at creation time.
allow_edit
When true, the approver at this step can edit workflow_pending_changes.proposed_data mid-flight via wf_approver_update_pending_changes(). Changes are immediately visible in both pending-tasks and my-requests views.
is_cc
CC (copy) steps receive a notification only — no action required. wf_advance_instance creates the task and immediately auto-completes it, advancing to the next step without waiting.
step_name
Human-readable label shown in the timeline (e.g. "Line Manager Approval", "Finance Review"). Stored in action log for audit trail.
skip_if_same_as_submitter
When true, if the resolved approver equals the submitter's profile_id, the engine silently skips the step and advances. Prevents self-approval loops (e.g. employee IS their own manager).
④ Publish — wf_publish_template(p_template_id)
Calling wf_publish_template() does three things atomically:
1. Sets is_active = true, effective_from = now() on the new version.
2. Sets is_active = false on all older versions of the same template code (deactivates prior versions).
3. (mig 195) UPDATEs every active workflow_assignments row that pointed at any older version of this template to reference the new template_id. Fixes the "— select workflow —" broken-assignment bug in Manage Assignments UI after version bumps. In-flight workflow_instances are unaffected — they stored a snapshot template_id at submit time.
Template versioning: Editing a published template creates a new draft clone at version + 1. The original stays active. In-flight instances always run against the template version that was active at submit time — mid-flight template changes never affect running approvals.
⑤ Assign to Module — workflow_assignments
After publishing, the template must be linked to a module via workflow_assignments so the engine knows which template to use when an employee submits a request.
Field Description
module_code Which module this assignment applies to (e.g. 'expense_reports', 'profile_address').
wf_template_id The published template to use.
assignment_type GLOBAL — applies to all employees. ROLE — applies to employees holding a specific role. EMPLOYEE — applies to one specific employee (overrides ROLE and GLOBAL).
role_id / profile_id Required for ROLE and EMPLOYEE types respectively. NULL for GLOBAL.
effective_from / effective_to Optional date range bounding when the assignment is active. Engine only matches assignments where now() falls within the range.
resolve_workflow_for_submission(module_code, profile_id) — priority chain
EMPLOYEE match
→ wins if found, else
ROLE match
→ wins if found, else
GLOBAL
→ fallback
Same resolver is used by submit_expense / submit_change_request (RPC layer) AND by get_profile_workflow_gates() (UI gate detection) — guaranteeing the gate and the submission always agree on whether a workflow exists for the current user.
End-to-End Admin Checklist
✅ Create template → name + description → save as draft
✅ Add steps → at least 1 step with step_order = 1; add more for multi-step chains
✅ Configure each step → approver_type, approver_role/profile_id, approval_mode, sla_hours, allow_edit, is_cc
✅ Publish → calls wf_publish_template() → deactivates old version, auto-migrates assignments
✅ Assign to module → create workflow_assignments row(s) with correct assignment_type + date range
✅ Test with employee account → submit → verify routing preview matches assignment, tasks created correctly
⚠ In-flight instances are always isolated from template changes — safe to republish while approvals are in progress.
📧 Notification Reference
Code Sent To Triggered By
wf.task_assigned Approver Submit · advance to next step · reassign · resubmit after clarification
wf.clarification_requested Submitter wf_return_to_initiator()
wf.clarification_submitted Approver wf_resubmit()
wf.returned_to_previous_step Previous step approver wf_return_to_previous_step()
wf.completed Submitter wf_advance_instance() — last step approved
wf.rejected Submitter wf_reject() — includes reason + step_order
wf.sla_reminder Approver pg_cron every 30 min — task past due_at (one-shot per task)
wf.sla_escalation Approver's manager pg_cron every 30 min — same run as sla_reminder (one-shot per task)
wf.task_removed Old approver wf_reassign() — only when an admin did the reassign, not self-reassign

Part 10 — New Hire Process

📋 Overview

When a workflow is attached to the New Hire module, creating a new employee no longer saves and activates immediately. Instead, the form is submitted for approval. The record is locked while under review so data cannot change mid-approval.

If no workflow is configured, the original behaviour applies: the administrator fills in the form and activates the employee directly. The system detects this automatically — no configuration change needed.

👥 Roles Involved
Role Responsibility
HR Analyst Fills in the Add Employee form and submits it for approval.
HR Head (Approver) Reviews the submission, can request changes, reject, or approve. Optionally edits the record in-flight if the workflow step allows it.
System Administrator Configures which workflow is attached to the New Hire module and manages role permissions.
🔄 Step-by-Step Flow
Step Who What Happens
1 — Fill Form HR Analyst Opens Add New Employee and completes all required sections (Personal Info, Employment Details, etc.).
2 — Submit HR Analyst Clicks Submit for Approval instead of Save. Record is saved as Pending and locked. A workflow task is created in the approver's inbox.
3 — Review HR Head Opens the workflow task. All employee sections are rendered one below another via get_employee_hire_review() RPC. Record is read-only by default.
4 — Edit in Flight (optional) HR Head If the workflow step has Allow Edit in Flight enabled AND the approver has employee_hire.edit permission, an Update button appears. Approver opens the form, makes corrections, and returns to the review screen.
5 — Decision HR Head Approves, Returns for Clarification, or Rejects the request.
6 — Auto-Activate System On approval, wf_activate_employee() RPC fires automatically. Employee status → Active, welcome email sent with magic link.
7 — First Login New Employee Clicks the magic link in the welcome email, sets a password, and accesses the employee portal.
🏷 Hiring Record Statuses
Draft
Form started but not yet submitted. Only the creating analyst can see and edit it.
Pending Approval
Submitted and awaiting decision. Record is locked — no edits unless the approver uses Edit in Flight.
Changes Requested
Returned to the analyst with comments. Analyst must update and resubmit. Record is unlocked for edits.
Rejected
Approver rejected the hire. No further action taken. Analyst can view the rejection reason.
Active
Approved and activated. Employee has received the welcome email and can log in to the system.
The employees table carries two fields to track this: status (draft / pending / rejected / active) and locked (boolean). The locked flag is set to true on submit and false again if returned for changes or rejected.
👁 Who Sees What — Pending Hires List
User Default With view_all_pending
HR Analyst Only their own submitted records. No change — analysts always see only their own records.
HR Head / Approver Only records assigned to them as a workflow task. Can see ALL pending hire records across all analysts, even if not assigned as approver.
System Administrator Full access to all records via admin panel. Full access regardless of permission setting.
🔑 New Hire Permissions

Two new permissions live in the NEW HIRE row of the Permission Matrix (admin → Roles & Permissions):

Badge Permission Code What it Allows
V employee_hire.view_all_pending See every pending hire record, not just those assigned as a workflow task.
E employee_hire.edit_all_pending Open and edit any pending hire record for correction — even without an active workflow task assigned.
The standard employee_hire.edit permission (existing) gates Edit in Flight on the workflow review screen. The two new permissions above are for the standalone Pending Hires list view.
✏️ Edit in Flight — How It Works

Edit in Flight lets the approver correct the employee record directly from the review screen without rejecting and restarting the process. Two navigation patterns exist depending on the module:

Pattern A — Navigate to edit form
Used by employee_hire and expense_reports. module_codes.edit_route is non-null (e.g. /employees/add?mode=edit&id=:id). Inbox navigates to that route with returnTo so the approver can make changes in the full form before returning to approve.
Pattern B — Inline edit in panel
Used by all profile_* modules (personal info, contact, address, passport, etc.). module_codes.edit_route is NULL. The inbox opens an inline edit mode showing the proposed values with editable fields — the approver adjusts and clicks Confirm. profile_* modules must always use Pattern B — their ESS routes (/profile/personal etc.) are self-referential and would show the approver's own profile, not the subject employee's. Mig 333 + frontend PROFILE_INLINE_MODULES guard enforce this.

All conditions required for the Update button to appear:

1 The workflow step has Allow Edit in Flight enabled — configured in the workflow template step.
2 The approver holds the module's edit permission — e.g. personal_info.edit for profile_personal, employee_hire.edit for hire. Checked via can(editPermCode) against get_my_permissions().
3 The approver has an active workflow task assigned to them for that specific record.
⚡ What Happens on Approval — Automatic Activation

When the approver clicks Approve, the system calls wf_activate_employee(p_employee_id) as an atomic server-side RPC. No manual "Activate" button — the 6 actions below happen in sequence automatically:

Action Detail
#1 Activate Record Employee status → Active, locked → false.
#2 Save Extended Data All extended profile sections (custom fields) are persisted to the database.
#3 Create Auth User A Supabase auth user is created via signInWithOtp using the employee's business email. No password set at this stage.
#4 Link Profile Auth user is linked to the employee record via link_profile_to_employee() RPC.
#5 Record Invite An entry is written to the employee_invites table to track the invitation event.
#6 Send Welcome Email Employee receives a welcome email with a one-time magic link to set up their account. invite_sent_at is stamped on the employee row.
🔍 Review RPC — get_employee_hire_review()

Called on the WorkflowReview screen when module_code = 'employee_hire'. Returns the employee's full profile as structured JSON sections — all sections added in future are automatically included in the review screen without code changes.

-- Response shape
[
{ section: "Personal Info", fields: [{ label, value }] },
{ section: "Employment Details", fields: [{ label, value }] },
{ section: "...", fields: [...] }
]
The WorkflowReview component detects the employee_hire module and switches from the standard line-items view to a stacked-sections view, rendering each section as a card.
🆔 Employee ID Generation

IDs are generated at the database level using a Postgres sequence — not on the frontend. This guarantees uniqueness even when two analysts submit simultaneously.

Format
EMP-0042
EMP prefix + zero-padded 4-digit sequence
Mechanism
CREATE SEQUENCE emp_id_seq
DEFAULT value on employees.employee_id column
Collision handling
Postgres sequence is atomic — no two rows can receive the same value, even under concurrent inserts.
📧 Notification Events
Template Code Sent To Trigger
hire_submitted Approver (HR Head) Analyst submits a new hire record for approval.
hire_approved HR Analyst (submitter) Approver approves the request and the employee is activated.
hire_returned HR Analyst (submitter) Approver returns the request with comments requesting changes.
hire_rejected HR Analyst (submitter) Approver rejects the hire request with a reason.
employee_welcome New Employee Auto-sent on activation — contains magic link for first login and account setup.
⚙️ Module Registration — module_codes

The New Hire module is registered in module_codes so the workflow engine can attach templates and the review screen can resolve the edit route:

code = 'employee_hire'
label = 'New Hire'
edit_route = '/employees/add?mode=edit&id='
Admins can attach a workflow to the New Hire module the same way they do for any other module — via Workflow Assignments in the admin panel.
📦 Pending Implementation — Migration Checklist
□ Register employee_hire in module_codes with edit_route.
□ Add locked boolean DEFAULT false column on employees table.
□ Create Postgres sequence emp_id_seq and wire to employees.employee_id DEFAULT.
□ Create wf_activate_employee(p_employee_id) RPC — atomic server-side activation.
□ Create get_employee_hire_review(p_employee_id) RPC — structured JSON sections for review screen.
□ Add employee_hire.view_all_pending and employee_hire.edit_all_pending permissions; add NEW HIRE row to PermissionMatrix.tsx.
□ AddEmployee.tsx — Submit for Approval button, lock-on-submit, Pending Hires section, edit-mode support.
□ WorkflowReview.tsx — detect employee_hire module, render stacked sections from get_employee_hire_review().
□ Register 5 notification templates: hire_submitted, hire_approved, hire_returned, hire_rejected, employee_welcome.

Part 11 — Employee Bank Details Module

Set-snapshot model (Phase 4–5, mig 324–332). One atomic workflow submission per change session; approver sees a per-account diff. Multi-country, effective-dated, workflow-gated.

✓ Status: Live — Set-Snapshot model (mig 324–332, 2026-05-29)
⚑ Set-Snapshot Architecture (Phase 4+)

The original per-row effective-dating model (employee_bank_accounts) has been superseded by a parent-child set-snapshot pattern. All changes to a bank account set are now submitted as one atomic workflow request.

New tables (live)
employee_bank_account_set
employee_bank_account_item
employee_bank_attachments (unchanged)
New RPCs (live)
submit_bank_account_set(p_employee_id, p_effective_from, p_items)
get_employee_bank_account_set(p_employee_id, p_as_of?)
get_employee_bank_account_set_history(p_employee_id)
fn_apply_bank_account_set_transition(…)
Legacy table employee_bank_accounts renamed to employee_bank_accounts_legacy (mig 332). Legacy RPCs upsert_bank_account and get_employee_bank_accounts dropped (mig 332). The _legacy table will be dropped ≥ 2026-06-14.

1. Overview

Countries
IN · LK · PK · SA
Extensible via Reference Data
Tables
2 new tables
employee_bank_accounts · employee_bank_attachments
Multi-record
Yes — like Identity Docs
Each account has independent history chain
Effective dating
Date-driven (no is_active)
Active = effective_to = '9999-12-31'
Workflow gate
Optional — admin-controlled
module code: profile_bank
Storage
hr-attachments bucket
Path: bank-accounts/{emp_id}/{group_id}/{file}

2. Database Tables

employee_bank_accounts

Column Type Nullable Notes
idUUIDNOT NULLPK, default gen_random_uuid()
employee_idUUIDNOT NULLFK → employees.id ON DELETE CASCADE
