6. Workflow Gate & UI Placement

Module code: profile_bank Admin assigns workflow template via Workflow Assignments screen. When assigned → approval flow triggered on save. When not assigned → direct save.
AddEmployee (New Hire)
Section 9 — after Emergency Contact. Mandatory during hire. Effective dating rules do not apply. effective_from = hire date, read-only.
EmployeeDetails (Admin)
New section alongside Address and Emergency Contact. Multi-record table. Add Account + Update + History toggle.
WorkflowReview (Approver)
New section in hire review and profile change review. Approver blocked after 20th cutoff (except bank_exceptions role).
MyProfile (Employee ESS)
New portlet embedded in profile. Multi-record, history visible. Submission blocked after 15th (except bank_exceptions role).

7. Permissions

Permission Key Description
employee.view_bank_accountsView bank accounts portlet
employee.edit_bank_accountsAdd / edit bank accounts
Scope via Target Groups — own vs all employees is not encoded in the permission key. It is controlled entirely by the Target Group assigned to the permission set (Self / Direct Reports / All Employees). Permission Matrix UI row added after Emergency contact in Employee info group.
Bank Exceptions role bank_exceptions — bypasses the 15th submission cutoff and 20th approval cutoff. Does NOT bypass any other validation (duplicates, mandatory fields, attachment). Existing role — no new role creation needed.

8. Implementation Plan

□Migration A — Create employee_bank_accounts + employee_bank_attachments tables, indexes, CHECK constraints, RLS policies
□Migration B — Seed Bank picklist (top 10 banks × 4 countries). Seed permissions employee.view_bank_accounts + employee.edit_bank_accounts. Add profile_bank to module_codes
□Migration C — RPCs: upsert_bank_account (insert + close previous version in group), get_employee_bank_accounts (all records inc. history), get_bank_picklist (filtered by country)
□Frontend — BankAccounts portlet component (multi-record table, Add/Update/History). Shared country-rules config. Effective date picker with cutoff validation modal
□AddEmployee.tsx — Section 9 Bank Details. Mandatory. effective_from = hire date, read-only. Date rules bypassed
□EmployeeDetails / EmployeeEditPanel — Bank section alongside Address
□MyProfile — Bank portlet with workflow gate awareness
□WorkflowReview — Bank section in hire and profile change review. Approver date cutoff check
□PermissionMatrix.tsx — Add Bank accounts row after Emergency contact in Employee info group
□Supabase Storage — Create hr-attachments bucket if not exists. RLS policy: employee owns own path, admin/finance can access all
□Type regen: npx supabase gen types typescript --project-id okpnubnswpgybpzgwgtr > src/types/database.types.ts

Part 12 — Dependents Module

Set-snapshot model (Phase 2–3, mig 320–323). One atomic workflow submission per change session; approver sees a per-dependent diff. Introduced in migrations 289–298; rewritten to set-snapshot in 320–323.

⚑ Set-Snapshot Architecture (Phase 2+)

The original per-row effective-dating model (employee_dependents) has been superseded by a parent-child set-snapshot pattern. All changes to a dependent set are now submitted as one atomic workflow request.

New tables (live)
employee_dependent_set
employee_dependent_item
employee_dependent_attachments (unchanged — joins on dependent_code)
New RPCs (live)
submit_dependent_set(p_employee_id, p_effective_from, p_items)
get_employee_dependent_set(p_employee_id, p_as_of?)
get_employee_dependent_set_history(p_employee_id)
fn_apply_dependent_set_transition(…)
Legacy table employee_dependents renamed to employee_dependents_legacy (mig 332). Legacy RPCs upsert_dependent, remove_dependent, get_employee_dependents dropped (mig 332). The _legacy table will be dropped ≥ 2026-06-14.

1. Overview

The Dependents module lets employees record and maintain information about their family members and other dependents (spouse, children, parents, etc.) for insurance eligibility, payroll deductions, and HR record-keeping. A "dependent set" represents the employee's full dependent configuration as of a given effective date. Adding, amending, or removing dependents creates a new set version, preserving the full history of dependent relationships over time.

Core tables
employee_dependent_set
employee_dependent_item
employee_dependent_attachments
Workflow module code
profile_dependents
Label: "Profile – Dependents"
Migrations
289 — Core schema + direct RPCs
290 — Workflow dual-path
291 — Gate function update
292–298 — Fixes & polish

2. Table Schema

Key columns only — audit timestamps (created_at, updated_at) and created_by/updated_by columns exist on both tables but are omitted for brevity.

employee_dependents

ColumnTypeNullableDefault / ConstraintDescription
idUUIDNOT NULLgen_random_uuid()Primary key
dependent_codetextNOT NULLUNIQUE WHERE effective_to='9999-12-31' (partial)Auto-generated by fn_generate_dependent_code() BEFORE INSERT trigger. Format: {emp_code}_DEP_{NN}. All versions of the same dependent share this code — it's the stable identity key across edits and history.
employee_idUUIDNOT NULLFK → employees.id ON DELETE CASCADEOwner employee
relationship_typetextNOT NULLFK → picklist_values (DEPENDENT_RELATIONSHIP_TYPE)DR001–DR008 (see picklist section)
dependent_nametextNOT NULL—Full name of the dependent
date_of_birthdatenullable—Optional date of birth
gendertextnullable—Optional gender field
insurance_eligiblebooleanNOT NULLfalseWhether this dependent qualifies for group insurance coverage
effective_fromdateNOT NULL—Start of this version's validity window
effective_todateNOT NULL'9999-12-31'End of validity. Sentinel '9999-12-31' = current/active. Historical rows have effective_to < '9999-12-31'.
is_activebooleanNOT NULLtruefalse = removed/terminal record. Active record: is_active=true AND effective_to='9999-12-31'. Removal record: is_active=false AND effective_to='9999-12-31'.
inactive_attimestamptznullable—Timestamp when is_active was set to false (removal)
inactive_byUUIDnullableFK → profiles.idWho performed the removal
UNIQUE partial index: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ON employee_dependents (dependent_code) WHERE effective_to = '9999-12-31'
Ensures only one "current" row per dependent exists at any time. Historical rows (effective_to < '9999-12-31') are excluded so the same dependent_code can appear multiple times in history without conflict.

employee_dependent_attachments

ColumnTypeNullableDescription
idUUIDNOT NULLPrimary key
dependent_codetextNOT NULLLogical link to employee_dependents via dependent_code (not a FK). Allows attachments to outlive individual version rows.
employee_idUUIDNOT NULLFK → employees.id ON DELETE CASCADE. Enables RLS scope check without joining through dependent_code.
dependent_idUUIDnullableFK → employee_dependents.id ON DELETE SET NULL. Points at the specific version row that was current when the file was uploaded. Set NULL if that version row is later closed or deleted.
document_typetextNOT NULLDD001–DD007 (see DEPENDENT_DOCUMENT_TYPE picklist)
file_nametextNOT NULLStored/sanitised file name in the bucket
original_file_nametextNOT NULLOriginal file name as uploaded by the user
file_pathtextNOT NULLStorage path: dependents/{employee_id}/{dependent_code}/{file_name} in hr-attachments bucket
mime_typetextnullableMIME type of the uploaded file
file_sizebigintnullableFile size in bytes
is_activebooleanNOT NULLfalse = soft-deleted attachment
uploaded_attimestamptzNOT NULLTimestamp of upload
uploaded_byUUIDnullableFK → profiles.id — who uploaded the file

3. Effective-Dating Pattern

The Dependents module uses the same bi-temporal effective-dating approach as employee_bank_accounts. The partial UNIQUE index and the is_active flag together define the state of each dependent at any point in time.

Active record
is_active = true
effective_to = '9999-12-31'
The current, live dependent record. At most one per dependent_code (enforced by partial UNIQUE index).
Removal / terminal record
is_active = false
effective_to = '9999-12-31'
The dependent has been removed. Still covered by the partial UNIQUE index — no new active row should exist for this code after removal.
Historical record
effective_to < '9999-12-31'
A previous version (closed when amended). Multiple historical rows can coexist for the same dependent_code — the partial index excludes them.
How upsert_dependent handles amendments
1. Close the current row: UPDATE … SET effective_to = p_effective_from - 1
2. Insert new row with same dependent_code, updated field values, and effective_from = p_effective_from, effective_to = '9999-12-31'
3. The BEFORE INSERT trigger does NOT generate a new code when a dependent_code is supplied — it only generates one on first insert.

4. Workflow — Dual-Path Pattern

The same dual-path pattern used by all profile_* modules. The path is resolved at runtime by resolve_workflow_for_submission('profile_dependents', auth.uid()).

PATH A — Direct write
No active workflow template for profile_dependents found via workflow_templates.

upsert_dependent / remove_dependent writes directly to employee_dependents in the same transaction. No staging, no approval needed.
PATH B — Workflow staging
Active template found. The RPC:
1. Inserts a row into workflow_pending_changes with module_code='profile_dependents' and the proposed dependent data as proposed_data JSONB.
2. Calls wf_submit() to create the workflow instance and first task.
3. On approval: the AFTER UPDATE trigger apply_profile_pending_change fires and writes the approved data to employee_dependents.
apply_profile_pending_change — profile_dependents branch
Handles three operations (from proposed_data->>'action'):
add — inserts a new dependent row (generates code via trigger)
amend — closes the current row and inserts new version with updated fields
remove — sets is_active=false on the current row

5. Key RPCs

RPCSecurityWhat it does
get_employee_dependents(p_employee_id) SEC DEFINER Returns all dependent rows for the given employee, newest-first. Includes is_active, effective_from/to so the frontend can render current + historical dependents. RLS bypassed to allow managers/HR to view dependents they own in scope.
upsert_dependent(…) SEC DEFINER Add or amend a dependent. Dual-path: PATH A writes directly to employee_dependents; PATH B stages in workflow_pending_changes and submits to workflow engine. Takes all dependent fields plus p_effective_from, p_dependent_code (null for new).
remove_dependent(p_dependent_code, p_employee_id) SEC DEFINER Remove a dependent. PATH A: sets is_active=false directly. PATH B: stages a remove action in workflow_pending_changes for approval.
get_profile_workflow_gates() SEC DEFINER Covers profile_dependents in all three return fields: gated_modules (is profile_dependents workflow-gated for this user?), pending_counts (how many pending dependent changes?), instance_ids (active workflow instance for the dependent module, if any).
Auto-code generation trigger: fn_generate_dependent_code() BEFORE INSERT on employee_dependents. Reads employee.employee_number, counts existing dependents for that employee, produces {emp_code}_DEP_{NN} (zero-padded to 2 digits). Skipped if NEW.dependent_code is already set (amendment re-inserts supply the existing code).

6. Frontend Placement

LocationComponent / SectionBehaviour
MyProfile DependentsPortlet ESS self-service view. Employee can add, amend, and remove their own dependents. Workflow gate awareness via get_profile_workflow_gates() — shows pending-approval banner when a change is in flight. "View approval progress" link opens WorkflowParticipantsModal.
EmployeeEditPanel DependentsPortlet HR/manager view of an employee's dependents. Gated by dependents.view permission on the target employee. Edit/add/remove gated by dependents.edit + dependents.create + dependents.delete.
AddEmployee (Hire Flow) Dependents section (hire review, mig 295) HR can enter initial dependent information as part of the hire pipeline before the employee status goes Active. Effective_from = hire date. No workflow gate in the hire flow — direct write.
WorkflowReview profile_dependents section Approver sees the proposed dependent change (add/amend/remove) alongside the current dependent data in the before/after diff panel, powered by workflow_pending_changes.proposed_data vs current_data.

7. Permissions

SortPermission CodeActionWhat it controls
140dependents.viewviewRead employee_dependents rows for employees in scope. Required to see the Dependents section in EmployeeEditPanel / EmployeeDetails. ESS users access their own dependents via the self-path in RLS (no explicit permission needed for self-view).
141dependents.createcreateINSERT new dependent rows (or submit a new-dependent workflow change). Required for the Add Dependent button in DependentsPortlet and the hire-flow dependents section.
142dependents.editeditUPDATE / amend existing dependent rows (or submit an amend workflow change). Also gates the Edit button per dependent row in DependentsPortlet.
143dependents.deletedeleteRemove a dependent (set is_active=false, or submit a remove workflow change). Gates the Remove action in DependentsPortlet.
—bank_dependents_history.viewviewView the bank/dependents change history tab (mig 294). Shows past effective-dated versions and approved/rejected workflow_pending_changes for both modules.
RLS pattern: Both employee_dependents and employee_dependent_attachments use Path D (personal_info_pattern): self-access OR user_can(module, action, employee_id). Self-path: employee_id = get_my_employee_id() — no explicit permission needed. Scope-aware: managers, HR, etc. access dependents of employees in their assigned target group.

8. Picklists

DEPENDENT_RELATIONSHIP_TYPE

CodeLabel
DR001Spouse
DR002Child
DR003Parent
DR004Sibling
DR005Step-Child
DR006Domestic Partner
DR007Ward
DR008Other

DEPENDENT_DOCUMENT_TYPE

CodeLabel
DD001Birth Certificate
DD002Marriage Certificate
DD003Passport Copy
DD004National ID Copy
DD005Insurance Proof
DD006Adoption Certificate
DD007Other

9. File Storage

Bucket
hr-attachments
Same bucket as bank attachments. Private bucket — no public access.
Path pattern
dependents/{employee_id}/{dependent_code}/{file_name}
All attachment versions for a dependent are grouped under the same dependent_code folder regardless of which version row they were uploaded against.

Part 13 — Personal Information Module

Complete reference for the Personal Information feature: effective-dated schema, name split, draft pipeline, RPCs, workflow integration, nightly activation job, RLS, permissions, and frontend placement. Introduced in mig 020 (flat row); rewritten to effective-dating in mig 315–319; name split in mig 334.

1. Overview

The Personal Information module stores an employee's identity fields — name (split into first / middle / last), date of birth, gender, marital status, nationality, and preferred name. It is the only module where a change also propagates to employees.name, the system-wide denormalized display name used in workflow tasks, notifications, and audit logs.

Core table
employee_personal
Effective-dated (mig 315)
Staging table
employee_personal_draft
Hire pipeline only (mig 316)
Workflow module
profile_personal
Label: "Profile – Personal Info"
Key migrations
020 · 315 · 316 · 317 · 318 · 319 · 324 · 325 · 334

2. Table Schema

employee_personal

Effective-dated satellite table. One open-ended active row per employee (effective_to = '9999-12-31' AND is_active = true). Historical rows are preserved when amended — the prior row's effective_to is closed to p_effective_from − 1 day. A partial unique index enforces the single-active-row constraint.

Column Type Nullable Description
iduuidNOT NULL (PK)Surrogate PK. One row per effective slice.
employee_iduuidNOT NULLFK → employees.id ON DELETE CASCADE.
first_nametextNOT NULLRequired. Added mig 334 (split from name).
middle_namenullablenullableOptional. Net-new (not on employees table).
last_nametextnullableOptional (single-name employees). Added mig 334.
nametext (generated)NOT NULLComputed: first_name [middle_name] [last_name]. Kept in sync with employees.name via RPC.
preferred_nametextnullableDisplay/preferred name. Not synced to employees.name.
date_of_birthdatenullableUsed for age calculation and target group scoping.
gendertextnullableFrom GENDER picklist. Used in target group rules.
marital_statustextnullableFrom MARITAL_STATUS picklist. Used in target group rules.
nationalitytextnullableISO country code. From ID_COUNTRY picklist. Used in target group rules.
effective_fromdateNOT NULLDate from which this version is true. May be future-dated (nightly job activates).
effective_todateNOT NULL'9999-12-31' = open-ended (active row). Closed to effective_from_next − 1 on amendment.
is_activebooleanNOT NULL, default truefalse = soft-deleted (rare). Active row = is_active=true AND effective_to='9999-12-31'.
created_byuuidnullableFK → profiles.id. Who inserted this row.
created_at / updated_attimestamptzNOT NULLRow timestamps. updated_at maintained by trigger.
Indexes & Constraints
UNIQUE PARTIAL idx_ep_one_active_row ON employee_personal (employee_id) WHERE effective_to = '9999-12-31' AND is_active = true — enforces exactly one open-ended active row per employee
INDEX ON employee_personal (employee_id, effective_from, effective_to) — point-in-time slice queries
CHECK chk_ep_effective_order effective_to >= effective_from

employee_personal_draft

Staging table used exclusively by the hire pipeline. When an HR user fills in personal info during the Add Employee wizard, the data is written here (not to employee_personal) because the employee record is still in Draft/Incomplete status. On wf_activate_employee(), the draft row is consumed to seed the first employee_personal slice with effective_from = hire_date, then deleted.

Column Type Notes
employee_iduuid (PK)FK → employees.id ON DELETE CASCADE. One draft row per employee.
first_name / middle_name / last_nametextAdded mig 334. Mirrors employee_personal columns.
date_of_birth / gender / marital_status / nationality / preferred_nametext / dateSame field set as employee_personal. All nullable.
created_at / updated_attimestamptzupdated_at maintained by trigger.

3. Name Concatenation Rule (mig 334)

The name column is a generated/maintained concatenation of the three name parts. The rule is applied server-side inside upsert_personal_info() and on activation.

Concatenation Logic
first + middle + last → "Vijey Aananth SR"
first + last only → "Vijey SR"
first + middle only → "Vijey Aananth"
first only → "Vijey"
Backfill Rule (mig 334)
Existing name was split by finding the last space: everything before → first_name, last word → last_name. If no space: entire name → first_name, last_name = NULL. middle_name left unchanged (was already net-new).
employees.name sync guard (mig 316)
A BEFORE UPDATE trigger trg_guard_employee_name_sync on the employees table blocks any direct write to employees.name from application code. The only allowed path to update it is through upsert_personal_info() (which is SECURITY DEFINER and bypasses the guard) or the nightly activation job. This prevents drift between employee_personal.name and employees.name.

4. RPC Contracts

WRITE upsert_personal_info(p_employee_id uuid, p_proposed_data jsonb, p_effective_from date)

Single write entry point. Dual-path: PATH A (no workflow assigned) writes directly to employee_personal; PATH B (workflow assigned) stages in workflow_pending_changes and calls wf_submit().

PATH A return (direct write)
{"ok": true, "workflow": false, "id": "<row_uuid>"}
PATH B return (workflow staged)
{"ok": true, "workflow": true, "instance_id": "<uuid>"}
p_proposed_data shape
{ "first_name": "Vijey", "middle_name": "Aananth", "last_name": "SR",
  "date_of_birth": "1990-03-01", "gender": "Male",
  "marital_status": "Married", "nationality": "IND", "preferred_name": null }
• p_effective_from required — rejected if NULL or sentinel date
• Access guard: self (own employee_id) OR user_can('personal_info','edit', p_employee_id) OR active approver task on the instance
• Overlap guard: rejects if p_effective_from falls inside an existing historical slice's window
• Syncs employees.name immediately when p_effective_from <= CURRENT_DATE (SECURITY DEFINER bypasses the name guard trigger)
• Future-dated slices do NOT sync employees.name at write time — deferred to the nightly activation job
READ get_current_personal_info(p_employee_id uuid)

Returns the single active slice (effective_to = '9999-12-31' AND is_active = true) as a JSONB object. Returns null if no active row exists (employee has no personal info yet).

Access: self OR user_can('personal_info','view', p_employee_id)
READ get_personal_info_history(p_employee_id uuid)

Returns all effective-dated slices for an employee, ordered by effective_from DESC (newest first), as a JSONB array. Includes both the current active row and all historical closed rows. Used by the history accordion in EmployeeEditPanel.tsx.

Access: user_can('personal_info','view', p_employee_id) — self-access not sufficient for history.

5. Effective Dating Rules

Standard amendment (effective_from > current.effective_from)
Current active row is closed: effective_to = p_effective_from − 1 day, is_active = false. New row inserted with effective_from = p_effective_from, effective_to = '9999-12-31'. If p_effective_from <= today: employees.name synced immediately.
Future-dated amendment (effective_from > today)
New row inserted but employees.name is NOT updated immediately. The nightly activation job (activate_personal_info_records() at 00:05) picks up the row when effective_from = CURRENT_DATE and syncs the name. A drift-detection view flags any employees where employees.name has diverged.
Back-dated amendment / Mig-288 pattern (effective_from <= current.effective_from)
When the new effective_from is earlier than or equal to the current row's effective_from, the current row is replaced in-place (same effective_from kept, fields updated). This avoids creating a zero-day historical slice.
Overlap guard
The RPC checks if any existing historical row (closed, effective_to < '9999-12-31') has its window overlapping the new p_effective_from. If so, the RPC returns an error: "The chosen effective date overlaps with an existing historical record."

6. Nightly Activation Job (mig 318)

The function activate_personal_info_records() runs nightly at 00:05 UTC via pg_cron. It finds any employee_personal rows where effective_from = CURRENT_DATE and syncs employees.name for those employees. Each run is logged to job_run_log with job_code 'activate_personal_info_records'.
Self-healing admin job
A separate admin-triggered job (Jobs Admin screen) finds all Active employees where employees.name has drifted from their current employee_personal.name and re-syncs them. Used to recover from any missed activations (e.g. pg_cron downtime).
Flow: future-dated change lifecycle
1Employee submits name change with effective_from = future date → workflow staged in workflow_pending_changes
2Approver approves → apply_profile_pending_change fires → upsert_personal_info() writes future row to employee_personal. employees.name NOT synced yet.
3On effective_from date at 00:05 UTC → activate_personal_info_records() syncs employees.name
✓employees.name now reflects the new name across all downstream systems

7. Workflow Integration

Module code
profile_personal
Label: "Profile – Personal Info"
record_id strategy
employee_id (UUID)
Unique constraint on (module_code, record_id) → one in-flight change per employee
apply_profile_pending_change
When a pending change is approved, the AFTER UPDATE trigger fires and calls upsert_personal_info(employee_id, proposed_data, proposed_data.effective_from) (PATH A forced — no second workflow staging).
Approver Update button
Always Pattern B (inline edit). module_codes.edit_route = NULL (set by mig 333). Never navigates to /profile/personal (ESS self-service path). The PROFILE_INLINE_MODULES frontend guard enforces this as belt-and-suspenders.
proposed_data shape (in workflow_pending_changes)
{
  "employee_id": "<uuid>",
  "effective_from": "2026-05-29",
  "first_name": "Vijey", "middle_name": "Aananth", "last_name": "SR",
  "date_of_birth": "1990-03-01", "gender": "Male",
  "marital_status": "Married", "nationality": "IND",
  "preferred_name": null
}

8. Row-Level Security

Both employee_personal and employee_personal_draft use the personal_info_pattern: self-access OR user_can() Path D (target-group scoped).

Policy Operation Condition
ep_selectSELECTuser_can('personal_info','view', employee_id) — self included via Path D self-scope
ep_insertINSERTuser_can('personal_info','edit', employee_id) OR hire-pipeline path (edit NULL + hire_employee.edit + status in Draft/Incomplete/Pending)
ep_updateUPDATESame as INSERT — user_can('personal_info','edit', employee_id)
ep_deleteDELETEuser_can('personal_info','edit', employee_id) — only used by the nightly job for cleanup
Note: upsert_personal_info() is SECURITY DEFINER — it bypasses RLS for the actual write but enforces its own access guard inside the function body (self OR user_can OR active approver task). This is required to allow the name-sync to employees table which has its own guard trigger.

9. Permissions

Code Action What it gates
personal_info.viewviewRead employee_personal via RLS SELECT / get_current_personal_info / get_personal_info_history. Also controls Personal tab visibility in EmployeeEditPanel and MyProfile.
personal_info.editeditWrite via upsert_personal_info (INSERT/UPDATE RLS). Shows Edit button in MyProfile Personal section. Shows Update button in Workflow Inbox (when step_allow_edit=true). Included in ESS permission set — employees can edit their own personal info.
⚠ Update button warning: If an approver's permission set includes personal_info.edit AND the workflow step has allow_edit = true, the Update button will appear in their inbox. This is by design — it means the approver can adjust the proposed changes inline before approving. If the button should not appear for a given approver role, remove personal_info.edit from that role's permission set.

10. Frontend Placement

MyProfile/index.tsx — Personal section
ESS self-service
Scrollable section #mps-personal. Shows Edit button when can('personal_info.edit'). Edit button hidden (replaced by amber pending banner) when pendingCounts['profile_personal'] > 0. On save: calls upsert_personal_info() from App.tsx. Gated field indicator shown when a workflow is active.
EmployeeEditPanel.tsx — Personal tab
Admin / HR
Loads get_personal_info_history() to populate the history accordion. Admin saves via upsert_personal_info(). Shown only when can('personal_info.edit').
AddEmployee.tsx — Personal Info tab
Hire pipeline
During hire, writes to employee_personal_draft (not employee_personal). On wf_activate_employee(), the draft seeds the first employee_personal slice with effective_from = hire_date.
ApproverInbox.tsx — profile_personal task
Workflow approver
Renders proposed vs current values in a diff grid (changed fields highlighted in amber). Update button uses Pattern B (inline edit) — never navigates away. Required: step_allow_edit = true AND can('personal_info.edit').
WorkflowReview.tsx — full-page review
Workflow approver
Full-page diff view: effective_from highlighted when changed; all fields shown with prev/next comparison. Approval history timeline below.

11. Picklists Used

Picklist ID Field Notes
GENDERgenderAlso used in Target Group rules on employee_personal.gender.
MARITAL_STATUSmarital_statusAlso used in Target Group rules on employee_personal.marital_status.
ID_COUNTRYnationalityStores ISO alpha-3 code. Also used in Target Group rules on employee_personal.nationality.

12. Migration History

Mig What changed
020Original employee_personal as a flat single-row satellite. No effective dating. Fields: name, date_of_birth, gender, marital_status, nationality.
315Effective-dating rewrite. Added id (UUID PK), effective_from, effective_to ('9999-12-31' sentinel), is_active, created_by. Partial unique index enforcing one active row per employee. CHECK constraint on date order. Backfilled existing rows.
316employee_personal_draft table for hire pipeline. fn_guard_employee_name_sync() trigger on employees table blocking direct employees.name writes.
317RPCs: upsert_personal_info() (dual-path, overlap guard, name sync), get_current_personal_info(), get_personal_info_history().
318Nightly activation job: activate_personal_info_records() pg_cron at 00:05. Drift-detection view. job_run_log integration.
319apply_profile_pending_change — profile_personal branch. Wires workflow approval to upsert_personal_info() PATH A.
324Gap fixes: apply_profile_pending_change now deletes the employee_personal_draft row after activation so the hire wizard's draft doesn't persist as a stale ghost.
325Null-clear fix in upsert_personal_info — explicit NULL values in proposed_data now correctly clear optional fields (previously ignored).
326Activation job fix — wf_activate_employee now calls upsert_personal_info (with PATH A forced) to seed employee_personal from draft, replacing direct INSERT that bypassed the guard trigger.
333Null out profile_personal edit_route in module_codes (was /profile/personal — the ESS path — causing approver Update button to navigate to the approver's own profile). Pattern B (inline edit) is now always used for profile_personal.
334Name split: added first_name (NOT NULL) and last_name (nullable) to employee_personal and employee_personal_draft. name column now generated from concatenation. Backfill: split existing name by last space. upsert_personal_info / get_current_personal_info / get_personal_info_history all updated. wf_activate_employee updated to seed first_name / last_name from draft.

Part 14 — Employment Module (Effective-Dated)

⚠
Design Phase — Not Yet Implemented
This documents the planned employment effective-dating refactor. None of the schema or RPCs described below are deployed yet. All design decisions are locked (see docs/employment-effective-dating-design.md). Implementation sequence: migrations 324 → 328 backend, then 6 frontend phases. ~14 working days.

Complete reference for the planned Employment module: 10 fields moved from employees master to an effective-dated employee_employment satellite. Shares the nightly activation job with Personal Info. No draft table (matches actual practice — employee_personal_draft was created but never used). Adds dedicated workflow approver UI and forward-propagation logic for manager / currency / role-revoke cascades.

1. Overview

Today, ten employment fields (designation, job_title, dept_id, manager_id, hire_date, end_date, work_country, work_location, base_currency_id, status) live directly on the employees master table as flat columns with no history. The refactor moves them into employee_employment as an effective-dated timeline (same shape as employee_personal), while keeping the master columns as a denormalized mirror cache so existing JOINs across RLS, target_groups, and reports continue to work unchanged.

Core table
employee_employment
Expanded in place (mig 324)
Staging table
— none —
Hire writes directly to satellite
Workflow module
profile_employment
Promote from stub to full
Planned migrations
324 · 325 · 326 · 327 · 328

2. Fields Moving

All ten fields below move to the satellite as effective-dated columns. The same ten columns remain on employees as a denormalized mirror cache, written by the RPC and the nightly job.

Field Type Source Notes
designationtextDESIGNATION picklistPicklist UUID; not FK-enforced
job_titletextFree-formAuto-populated from resolved designation label when blank; user override allowed. Kept (not dropped) because 30+ workflow UI surfaces read it as the display subtitle under person names.
dept_iduuidFK departments(id)ON DELETE SET NULL
manager_iduuidFK employees(id)Self-FK. Cycle check up to 10 hops in RPC.
hire_datedateUser inputSeeds first slice's effective_from; corrections via in-place update.
end_datedateUser input / nullSentinel '9999-12-31' = open-ended. Drives end-date→Inactive auto-flip.
work_countrytextID_COUNTRY picklistDrives auto-derivation of base_currency_id.
work_locationtextLOCATION picklistParent-filtered by work_country.
base_currency_iduuidFK currencies(id)Auto-derived from work_country via picklist meta.currencyId.
statusemployee_status ENUMSystemSame-day lifecycle transitions (Draft→Pending→Active) update slice in place; different-day transitions (Active→Inactive on future end_date) create new slice.

3. Table Schema (post-mig 324)

employee_employment

One open-ended active row per employee (effective_to = '9999-12-31' AND is_active = true). Historical rows preserved when amended.

Column Type Nullable Description
iduuidNOT NULL (PK)Surrogate PK (NEW — was employee_id pre-324).
employee_iduuidNOT NULLFK → employees.id ON DELETE CASCADE. No longer PK.
designationtextnullableDESIGNATION picklist UUID.
job_titletextnullableFree-form. Auto-fill from designation label.
dept_iduuidnullableFK → departments.id ON DELETE SET NULL.
manager_iduuidnullableSelf-FK → employees.id ON DELETE SET NULL.
hire_datedatenullableInitial value seeds effective_from.
end_datedatenullableOpen-ended sentinel: '9999-12-31'.
work_countrytextnullableID_COUNTRY picklist UUID.
work_locationtextnullableLOCATION picklist UUID. parent_value_id = work_country.
base_currency_iduuidnullableFK → currencies.id. Auto-derived from work_country.
statusemployee_statusnullableDraft | Incomplete | Pending | Active | Inactive.
probation_end_datedatenullablePre-existing column (kept; effective-dated with the rest).
effective_fromdateNOT NULLSlice activation date. May be future.
effective_todateNOT NULL'9999-12-31' = open. Closed to next.effective_from − 1 on amendment.
is_activebooleanNOT NULL, default truefalse = soft-deleted (rare).
created_by / updated_by / inactive_at / inactive_by / created_at / updated_atuuid / timestamptzmixedStandard audit columns.
Indexes & Constraints
UNIQUE PARTIAL idx_ee_one_active_row ON (employee_id) WHERE effective_to = '9999-12-31' AND is_active = true
INDEX idx_ee_employee_timeline ON (employee_id, effective_from, effective_to)
INDEX PARTIAL idx_ee_manager_active ON (manager_id) WHERE effective_to = '9999-12-31' AND is_active = true — enables fast manager cycle walks
CHECK chk_ee_effective_order effective_to >= effective_from

4. Status & Hire-Date Semantics

Status — same-day transitions
During onboarding (Draft → Pending → Active) the same calendar day's slice's status updates in place. No new slice is created — the lifecycle state machine isn't a date-driven history. Mirror sync updates employees.status atomically, firing the existing role-grant and ESS-resync chain (sync_profile_on_employee_status, after_employee_role_sync).
Status — different-day transitions
Future-dated Active → Inactive (e.g. set end_date = 2026-12-31) creates a NEW slice with effective_from = 2026-12-31 and status = 'Inactive'. Sync job picks it up on day 2026-12-31, mirrors to master, fires role-revoke.
Hire_date — effective-dated as correction history
The first slice's effective_from is seeded from hire_date. Corrections to hire_date update the current slice in place. No retroactive history rewrite; the audit log captures the correction.
end_date → Inactive auto-flip
Part B (synchronous): if RPC writes end_date ≤ today AND status = Active, the same slice flips to Inactive.
Part C (nightly): _scan_end_date_inactive() catches future-dated end_dates that have come due.

5. RPC Contracts

WRITE upsert_employment_info(p_employee_id uuid, p_proposed_data jsonb, p_effective_from date)

Single write entry point. Dual-path (PATH A direct vs PATH B workflow staged). Mirrors upsert_personal_info verbatim with employment-specific cascades.

p_proposed_data shape
{ "designation": "<DESIGNATION uuid>", "job_title": "Senior Engineer",
  "dept_id": "<uuid>", "manager_id": "<uuid>",
  "hire_date": "2024-04-01", "end_date": "9999-12-31",
  "work_country": "<ID_COUNTRY uuid>", "work_location": "<LOCATION uuid>",
  "status": "Active" }
• base_currency_id is ignored in input — derived server-side from work_country's picklist meta.currencyId
• Manager cycle check — walks chain up to 10 hops, returns {ok: false, error: 'CYCLE_DETECTED', chain: [...]} (UI renders modal)
• Currency derivation failure returns {ok: false, error: 'CURRENCY_DERIVATION_FAILED', country: '<iso>'}
• Overlap guard rejects backdates that fall inside a closed historical slice
• Mirror sync to employees fires immediately when effective_from ≤ CURRENT_DATE; otherwise deferred to nightly job
READ get_current_employment_info(p_employee_id uuid)

Returns the current open slice as JSONB. Same access guard pattern as the personal_info read (view/edit + ESS self + active approver + sent-back + hire-pipeline view). Returns NULL on access denied.

READ get_employment_info_history(p_employee_id uuid)

Returns jsonb_agg(... ORDER BY effective_from DESC) of all slices. Gated on employment.history OR employment.edit (new permission, mig 324 seed).

6. Mirror Sync & Guard Trigger

All 10 mirror columns on employees are protected by a BEFORE UPDATE guard trigger fn_guard_employee_employment_sync that blocks ad-hoc writes unless the bypass flag prowess.allow_employment_sync = 'true' is set.

Allowed write paths
upsert_employment_info() (sets flag) · _sync_employment_today() · _scan_end_date_inactive() · Hire pipeline direct writes when status IN ('Draft','Incomplete','Pending') (guard exempts)
Blocked write paths
Any direct UPDATE on Active employees touching the 10 columns from application code outside the RPC raises: employees.employment columns must be updated via upsert_employment_info().

7. Shared Nightly Job (mig 326)

The existing activate_personal_info_records() is renamed to activate_effective_dated_records() and broadened to run three sequential passes. Single pg_cron entry, single job_run_log row covering all three.

_sync_personal_info_today()
Personal info slices whose effective_from = today → mirror employees.name. Existing logic from mig 318.
_sync_employment_today()
Employment slices whose effective_from = today → mirror 10 cols on employees. On manager change, calls sync_system_roles for new manager.
_scan_end_date_inactive()
Scans Active employees with end_date ≤ today; flips status to Inactive in slice + mirror. sync_profile_on_employee_status fires on mirror UPDATE → roles revoked.
pg_cron schedule
'5 0 * * *' (00:05 daily) — jobname: activate_effective_dated_records

8. Forward Propagation Rules

All cascades are server-side inside upsert_employment_info so they fire on every write path (workflow apply, admin edit, hire wizard, mid-flight edits).

Field Changed Cascade
work_countryAuto-derive base_currency_id from ID_COUNTRY picklist meta.currencyId. Reject save if derivation fails.
manager_idCycle check (10 hops max) → modal error if cycle. On success: sync_system_roles(new_manager_profile_id) to grant manager role.
dept_idsync_single_target_group('everyone') + invalidate per-dept target_groups via sync_target_group_members().
designation / work_locationKick sync_target_group_members() for custom target_groups whose filter_rules match these fields.
end_date ≤ today AND status = ActivePart B: same slice flips status = Inactive. Mirror sync fires existing role-revoke chain.
end_date future-datedPart C: nightly _scan_end_date_inactive() flips status when the day arrives.
hire_date correctionMirror sync updates employees.hire_date. No retroactive slice rewrite. Audit log captures.
status (Draft→Pending→Active)Same-slice in-place update. Mirror sync triggers existing role-grant and ESS-resync logic.
Any of the 10 fieldsaudit_employees trigger fires on the mirror UPDATE — audit log row written automatically.

9. Workflow Integration

submit_change_request snapshot
New WHEN 'profile_employment' THEN branch reads the current open slice and stores it in workflow_pending_changes.current_data filtered to proposed_data keys.
apply_profile_pending_change branch
On status → 'approved': read effective_from from proposed_data (default today), call upsert_employment_info(emp_id, data, eff_from). Warns and skips if RPC returns ok:false.
EmploymentEnrichment (NEW)
New component in ApproverInbox.tsx replacing the generic key-value fallback. Resolves picklist UUIDs to labels (designation/country/location), dept name lookups, manager search, currency display, date picker. Per-field amend diff with was: strikethrough.
WorkflowReview full-page support
profile_employment added to FULL_REVIEW_MODULES. Same EmploymentEnrichment component renders in the full-page Open View ↗.
Required dictionary additions in ApproverInbox.tsx
PROFILE_FIELD_LABELS['employment'] = {
  designation:'Designation', job_title:'Job Title', dept_id:'Department',
  manager_id:'Reports To',   hire_date:'Hire Date',  end_date:'End Date',
  work_country:'Work Country', work_location:'Work Location',
  base_currency_id:'Base Currency', status:'Status'
};
PROFILE_DATE_FIELDS.add('hire_date'); PROFILE_DATE_FIELDS.add('end_date');
PROFILE_PICKLIST_FIELDS['profile_employment'] = {
  designation:'DESIGNATION', work_country:'ID_COUNTRY', work_location:'LOCATION'
};

10. Frontend Touchpoints

File Change
src/components/employee/MyProfile/index.tsxPromote Employment section to editable. New edit form with designation/dept/manager/etc. inputs. Save → submit_change_request('profile_employment', ...).
src/components/admin/AddEmployee.tsxHire wizard writes directly to employee_employment with effective_from = hire_date. Guard trigger exempts Draft/Incomplete/Pending.
src/components/admin/EmployeeEditPanel.tsxAdmin Employment section save routes to upsert_employment_info (direct write, bypasses workflow).
src/components/admin/EmployeeDetails.tsxAdd "Employment History" link → opens timeline panel showing get_employment_info_history.
src/workflow/screens/ApproverInbox.tsxNew EmploymentEnrichment component + dictionary entries (PROFILE_FIELD_LABELS, PROFILE_DATE_FIELDS, PROFILE_PICKLIST_FIELDS).
src/workflow/screens/WorkflowReview.tsxAdd 'profile_employment' to FULL_REVIEW_MODULES. Renders EmploymentEnrichment.
src/components/employee/hooks/useEmploymentSection.tsNEW — wraps get_current_employment_info + get_employment_info_history for MyProfile/EmployeeEditPanel reuse.

11. Manager Cycle Detection & Modal UX

The RPC walks the proposed manager_id's manager chain up to 10 hops. If p_employee_id appears upstream, the RPC returns a structured error which the frontend renders as a modal dialog.

Error envelope
{
  "ok": false,
  "error": "CYCLE_DETECTED",
  "message": "Assigning {newManager} as manager would create a reporting cycle.",
  "chain": ["EMP-0042", "EMP-0017", "EMP-0042"]
}

Frontend modal: title "Reporting cycle detected", body shows the chain visualisation, single "Got it" button. Same modal renders at every call site (MyProfile, EmployeeEditPanel, AddEmployee, approver mid-flight edit).

12. Backfill (mig 328)

Per employee (all, not just Active): skip if already has an active slice; otherwise insert one slice with effective_from = hire_date (fallback chain: created_at::date → '2000-01-01'), effective_to = '9999-12-31', all 10 fields copied from employees master. Validation: row counts match, no duplicate active slices, every Active employee's slice status = 'Active'. On validation failure, the entire migration aborts in transaction (DB rolls back to pre-state). Legacy employees.* columns are NOT touched during backfill — they ARE the source.

13. Locked Decisions

All 10 fields move — including hire_date and status; both still mirrored on employees as cache.
No draft table — hire writes directly to satellite. Matches actual personal_info practice (mig 316 draft was never used).
job_title kept — 30+ workflow UI surfaces consume it as display title. Auto-populate from designation label when blank.
Mirror policy: Option A — 10 cols on employees as denormalized cache. Pure-satellite refactor rejected (touches every JOIN in the codebase).
end_date → Inactive: both B + C — synchronous when end_date ≤ today; nightly scanner for future-dated.
profile_employment in FULL_REVIEW_MODULES — full-page approver support.
Admin direct-write via EmployeeEditPanel routes to RPC. RPC enforces access guard.
Manager cycle check — 10-hop walk, modal error UX.
employment.history permission — added to permissions seed.
Sync job sharing — single pg_cron entry runs personal + employment + end_date scanner.

14. Planned Migrations

Mig What
324employee_employment schema expansion: add 10 effective-dated columns + indexes + chk_ee_effective_order. Drop old PK on employee_id, add surrogate id. fn_guard_employee_employment_sync trigger.
325RPCs: upsert_employment_info (dual-path, overlap guard, mirror sync, currency derivation, manager cycle check, job_title auto-fill, status-in-slice semantics), get_current_employment_info, get_employment_info_history. employment.history permission seed.
326Shared sync job: DROP activate_personal_info_records, CREATE activate_effective_dated_records orchestrator calling _sync_personal_info_today + _sync_employment_today + _scan_end_date_inactive. Reschedule pg_cron under new jobname.
327apply_profile_pending_change — new profile_employment branch calling upsert_employment_info. submit_change_request snapshot branch for profile_employment.
328Backfill: per employee, create first slice with effective_from = hire_date (fallback chain), copy 10 fields from employees. Validation checks then commit.

Note: migration numbers may shift if other parallel work (e.g. set-snapshot rewrite at 320–323) takes additional slots first.

Part 15 — Job Relationships Module (Matrix Managers)

⚠
Design Phase — Not Yet Implemented
This documents the planned Job Relationships module — SuccessFactors-style matrix managers with effective-dating. None of the schema, RPCs, or UI described below are deployed yet. All design decisions are locked (see docs/job-relationships-design.md). Implementation sequence: migrations 357 → 362 backend, then 5 frontend phases. ~16 working days total.

Introduces up to six matrix-manager assignments per employee (codes PM01, PM02, PM03, OM01, OM02, OM03), effective-dated using the set-snapshot pattern shared with dependents and bank. The six master columns mirror onto employees and become a new workflow approver type JOB_RELATIONSHIP, so any workflow step can route to (for example) PM01 just as today it routes to MANAGER or DEPT_HEAD.

1. Overview

Matrix organisations need multiple reporting lines beyond the single direct manager employees.manager_id. SuccessFactors Employee Central solves this with a Job Relationships entity — effective-dated, multi-typed, queryable as a workflow approver target. This module imports that pattern: six fixed codes (admin labels them), one effective-dated set per employee with up to six per-code items inside, mirror columns on the master, and a new workflow approver type.

Parent table
employee_job_relationship_set
Item table
employee_job_relationship_item
Workflow approver type
JOB_RELATIONSHIP
+ relationship_code on workflow_steps
Planned migrations
357 · 358 · 359 · 360 · 361 · 362

2. Codes & Picklist

Six codes are seeded in JOB_RELATIONSHIP_TYPE picklist. Codes are immutable; admin can edit only the display labels and the active flag via standard picklist management.

Code (ref_id) Default Label Master column
PM01Project Manageremployees.pm01_manager_id
PM02Programme Manageremployees.pm02_manager_id
PM03Practice Manageremployees.pm03_manager_id
OM01Operations Manageremployees.om01_manager_id
OM02Operations Leademployees.om02_manager_id
OM03Operations Coordinatoremployees.om03_manager_id

3. Table Schema

employee_job_relationship_set

Column Type Notes
iduuid PKSurrogate PK
employee_iduuidFK → employees.id ON DELETE CASCADE
effective_from / effective_todate'9999-12-31' = open-ended; CHECK effective_to ≥ effective_from
is_activebooleanPartial UNIQUE on (employee_id) WHERE is_active AND effective_to='9999-12-31'
created_by / updated_by / created_at / updated_atuuid / timestamptzStandard audit columns

employee_job_relationship_item

Column Type Notes
iduuid PKSurrogate PK
set_iduuidFK → employee_job_relationship_set.id ON DELETE CASCADE
relationship_codetextref_id from JOB_RELATIONSHIP_TYPE picklist. UNIQUE (set_id, relationship_code).
manager_employee_iduuidFK → employees.id ON DELETE RESTRICT (deactivation goes through trigger fanout, not raw DELETE)

employees (mirror columns added)

ADD COLUMN pm01_manager_id uuid REFERENCES employees(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
ADD COLUMN pm02_manager_id uuid REFERENCES employees(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
ADD COLUMN pm03_manager_id uuid REFERENCES employees(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
ADD COLUMN om01_manager_id uuid REFERENCES employees(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
ADD COLUMN om02_manager_id uuid REFERENCES employees(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
ADD COLUMN om03_manager_id uuid REFERENCES employees(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
CHECK chk_pm01_not_self CHECK (pm01_manager_id IS NULL OR pm01_manager_id <> id) — and equivalents for all 6
INDEX PARTIAL idx_emp_{code} ON employees({code}_manager_id) WHERE NOT NULL — workflow hot path

4. RPC Contracts

WRITE upsert_job_relationship_set(p_employee_id uuid, p_effective_from date, p_items jsonb)

Dual-path (PATH A direct vs PATH B workflow staged). Validates picklist codes, Active status of assignees, no self-assignment, no duplicate codes. Closes prior open set, inserts new slice + items, syncs 6 mirror columns when effective_from ≤ today.

p_items shape
[ { "relationship_code": "PM01", "manager_employee_id": "<uuid>" },
  { "relationship_code": "OM02", "manager_employee_id": "<uuid>" } ]
READ get_current_job_relationships(p_employee_id uuid)

Returns active set + items as JSONB. Returns {ok:true, set:null, items:[]} when no set exists.

READ get_job_relationships_history(p_employee_id uuid)

All sets reverse-chronologically. Gated on job_relationships.history OR .view.

UTIL get_deactivation_impact(p_employee_id uuid)

NEW utility RPC powering the deactivation-time UX warning. Returns the list of OTHER employees where p_employee_id currently appears in any of the 6 mirror columns, plus which codes they hold. Frontend calls this BEFORE submitting the deactivation so HR can see the blast radius.

5. Workflow Integration

A new approver type JOB_RELATIONSHIP joins the existing MANAGER / DEPT_HEAD / ROLE / PROFILE / SELF set. A new column workflow_steps.relationship_code (nullable TEXT) carries which of the 6 codes this step resolves to.

Routing example
Workflow template "Leave Request" with 2 steps:
Step 1: approver_type = MANAGER → resolves to direct line manager (employee_employment)
Step 2: approver_type = JOB_RELATIONSHIP, relationship_code = PM01 → resolves to submitter's PM01 matrix manager
Submitter raises leave → Step 1 task to direct manager → on approve, Step 2 task to PM01 → on approve, instance completes.
Skip semantics (locked)
If the matrix manager is NULL (unassigned) OR Inactive at read time, wf_resolve_approver_ex returns NULL, the step is silently skipped, and a workflow_action_log row is emitted ("step_skipped: matrix manager unassigned / inactive") for forensics. Submitters do not see this in their inbox; audit dashboards can filter on the skip events to detect routing-drift patterns.

6. Deactivation Fanout

When an employee transitions to status = 'Inactive', the existing sync_profile_on_employee_status trigger gets a new pass that scans every OTHER employee where the deactivated person appears in any of the 6 mirror columns and creates new sets removing those references.

Per-affected-employee action
Close current set (effective_to = today − 1 or DELETE if same-day); insert new set effective_from = today containing all items EXCEPT those referencing the deactivated person; mirror sync nulls the affected columns on the master.
Failure handling (locked)
Per-employee BEGIN/EXCEPTION blocks. One failure does NOT block the deactivation status flip. Failed rows logged to job_run_log with status = 'error'; drift view surfaces inconsistencies for manual repair.
Re-activation (locked)
Never auto-restore. Once severed, HR must re-assign manually via the portlet. Re-activation flips status back to Active but does NOT resurrect the closed-out matrix relationships.
Deactivation-time UX warning
Before submitting the deactivation, EmployeeEditPanel calls get_deactivation_impact() and shows a modal: "This person is a matrix manager for N employees: [list]. Deactivating will remove these assignments. Proceed?" HR can Reassign first or Deactivate anyway.

7. Validation & Propagation Rules

Rule Where enforced
No self-assignmentCHECK constraints on 6 mirror cols + RPC validation on items
No cycle checkMatrix relationships are non-hierarchical; mutual assignments (A is B's PM01, B is A's PM01) are valid
Manager must be Active at write timeRPC validation; rejects with clear error
Inactive manager at read time → step skippedwf_resolve_approver_ex; emits workflow_action_log skip entry
No duplicate code per setUNIQUE (set_id, relationship_code) index
All 6 codes available in workflow designerWorkflowTemplates.tsx dropdown populated from JOB_RELATIONSHIP_TYPE picklist active values
Mirror UPDATE → audit_employees trigger firesExisting trigger captures all mirror-column changes in employee_audit_log

8. UI Surfaces

File Change
src/components/shared/JobRelationshipsPortlet.tsxNEW. Set editor with view / draft / pending / history modes. Up to 6 per-code rows; employee picker per row. Mirrors DependentsPortlet pattern.
src/components/shared/JobRelationshipsHistoryPanel.tsxNEW. Timeline view of past sets — left date list, right per-code item grid.
src/components/admin/DeactivationImpactModal.tsxNEW. Confirmation dialog with affected-employees list + Reassign first / Deactivate anyway buttons.
src/components/employee/MyProfile/index.tsxNew read-only "Job Relationships" section below Employment.
src/components/admin/EmployeeEditPanel.tsxNew edit section for HR/admin. Direct-write through upsert_job_relationship_set. Wires DeactivationImpactModal.
src/components/admin/EmployeeDetails.tsxNew Matrix tab/column. Reverse lookup "who has X as PM01?".
src/workflow/screens/WorkflowTemplates.tsxStep editor adds JOB_RELATIONSHIP approver-type option + relationship_code dropdown (populated from picklist).
src/workflow/screens/ApproverInbox.tsx + WorkflowReview.tsxPhase 11 — profile_job_relationships enrichment for workflow-gated JR changes (when applicable).

9. Locked Decisions

Six codes — PM01/PM02/PM03/OM01/OM02/OM03 in JOB_RELATIONSHIP_TYPE picklist. Codes immutable; labels admin-editable.
No cycle check — matrix relationships are non-hierarchical. Self-assign blocked via CHECK.
Active at write, skip at read — RPC rejects assigning Inactive; wf_resolve_approver_ex skips the step if manager goes Inactive later.
Skip silently — NULL or Inactive matrix manager → step skipped, workflow_action_log entry written for forensics.
Deactivation fanout — extends sync_profile_on_employee_status. Per-employee EXCEPTION blocks. Auto-close date = today. Never auto-restore.
UX warning modal — Deactivating HR sees list of affected employees BEFORE the change submits.
All 6 codes usable in workflow — JOB_RELATIONSHIP approver type + relationship_code on workflow_steps.
Effective-from — any date, default today. No 1st-of-month snap, no cutoffs.
MyProfile read-only. Edits gated to HR/admin via job_relationships.edit.
Post-activation only — hire wizard does not collect matrix relationships.
Permissions — job_relationships.view / .edit / .history / .bulk_import / .bulk_export.
Bulk Operations — Plugged into the cross-module Bulk Operations Framework (see §11 below). Workflow always bypassed for bulk uploads regardless of any workflow configured for individual JR changes.
No backfill — net-new feature; existing employees start with empty matrix relationships.

10. Planned Migrations

Mig What
357Schema: 2 new tables + indexes + RLS. 6 new mirror columns on employees with CHECK self-assign + partial indexes. Picklist seed for JOB_RELATIONSHIP_TYPE. Permission seeds (view / edit / history / bulk_import / bulk_export).
358RPCs: upsert_job_relationship_set (dual-path, validation, mirror sync), get_current_job_relationships, get_job_relationships_history, get_deactivation_impact, fn_close_and_replace_job_relationship_set helper.
359Workflow engine extension: ALTER workflow_steps ADD relationship_code; rewrite wf_resolve_approver_ex with new JOB_RELATIONSHIP branch + step-skip log entry.
360Deactivation fanout: extend sync_profile_on_employee_status with the matrix-manager-removal pass. New drift view vw_job_relationships_drift.
361Notification templates: wf.job_relationship_assigned (sent to newly-assigned manager) and wf.job_relationship_removed (sent to removed manager). Existing wf_queue_notification handles delivery.
362apply_profile_pending_change — new profile_job_relationships branch (for when JR changes are themselves workflow-gated).

Migration numbering: set-snapshot rewrite shipped at 320–332; employment effective-dating shipped at 351–356; job relationships continues from 357. Design doc is the source of truth if other parallel work shifts numbers.

11. Bulk Operations Integration

Job Relationships plugs into the cross-module Bulk Operations Framework — a single generic Import/Export screen, async pipeline, and CSV format that serves all 12 effective-dated/master tables. This section captures Job-Relationships-specific aspects; the framework owns the screen, registry, async pipeline, and round-trip rules (separate doc: docs/bulk-operations-framework.md).

11.1 Framework rules that apply (20 locked)

# Rule
1New "Import / Export" sidebar item below Reports in the Admin section
2Sidebar visibility gate: any *.bulk_* permission grants tab access
3Permission naming: <module>.bulk_import / .bulk_export for every registered template
4Date format: strict mm/dd/yyyy for every date field (validation rejects other formats)
5Codes only, never names (employee_code, picklist ref_id, ISO country, currency code)
6CSV only. Template download = .zip containing <template>_template.csv + README.txt
7README auto-generated server-side from the registry; format rules + mandatory-field list + picklist code reference tables (resolved at download time)
8Mandatory fields marked with * in the CSV header row
9Export and template have identical column structure (round-trip-safe)
10Two export buttons: Export current (round-trip-safe) and Export history (full timeline, audit-only)
11Persistent Include inactive records toggle alongside export buttons (default OFF; applies to both)
12Bulk uploads bypass workflow regardless of any per-module workflow configuration
13All processing goes through the existing module RPC (upsert_job_relationship_set for this module) — no separate logic path
14File size: 10,000 rows hard cap per upload; UI warning at 5,000; above 10,000 rejected with "split into smaller files"
15CSV encoding: UTF-8 with BOM, comma delimiter, RFC 4180 double-quote field quoting
16Audit batch id: every audit_log row from one upload shares an upload_batch_id UUID stored in audit_log.metadata->'upload_batch_id'
17Async processing via Supabase Edge Function reading from Storage; status to job_run_log; in-app notification to uploader on completion
18Cancellation: uploader (+System Admin) can cancel in-flight; committed rows stay; remaining rows skipped; job_run_log.status='cancelled'
19Full-record deletion via DELETE_RECORD keyword in Value cell — requires confirmation step on results screen before commit
20Per-value DELETE keyword in Value cell — removes a single code in the new effective-dated slice (existing set-snapshot semantics)
21Export default = user-fillable business fields only. System-generated columns (UUIDs, timestamps, audit user IDs, computed columns, mirror caches) are omitted. Round-trip safe.
22"Include system metadata" toggle alongside the export buttons. When ON: adds row UUIDs, timestamps, audit user IDs, computed columns, mirror cache columns, AND display-name columns (Employee Name, Manager Name, Relationship Label, etc.). Default OFF. NOT round-trip safe.
23Template download never includes system fields. Template = exact column set the user can fill. Matches user-fillable subset of export-default.
24Importer silently ignores unknown / system column headers. If HR exports with system metadata ON, edits business fields, and re-imports, system columns are dropped on parse with an info notice ("Ignored N system columns").

11.2 Job Relationships registry row

{ "template_code": "job_relationships", "display_label": "Job Relationships", "description": "Matrix manager assignments — PM01 through OM03", "icon": "ti-users-group", "permission_view": "job_relationships.bulk_export", "permission_edit": "job_relationships.bulk_import", "workflow_bypass": true, "processor_rpc": "upsert_job_relationship_set", "deleter_rpc": "fn_close_and_replace_job_relationship_set", "schema_definition": { "columns": [ { "name": "Effective Date *", "data_type": "date_mmddyyyy", "mandatory": true, "user_fillable": true }, { "name": "Employee Code *", "data_type": "code_employee", "mandatory": true, "user_fillable": true }, { "name": "Relationship Code *", "data_type": "code_picklist:JOB_RELATIONSHIP_TYPE", "mandatory": true, "user_fillable": true }, { "name": "Value *", "data_type": "code_employee_or_keyword:DELETE,DELETE_RECORD", "mandatory": true, "user_fillable": true }, { "name": "Employee Name", "data_type": "display_name", "mandatory": false, "user_fillable": false, "include_with_system_metadata": true }, { "name": "Relationship Label", "data_type": "display_label", "mandatory": false, "user_fillable": false, "include_with_system_metadata": true }, { "name": "Manager Name", "data_type": "display_name", "mandatory": false, "user_fillable": false, "include_with_system_metadata": true }, { "name": "set_id", "data_type": "uuid", "mandatory": false, "user_fillable": false, "include_with_system_metadata": true }, { "name": "item_id", "data_type": "uuid", "mandatory": false, "user_fillable": false, "include_with_system_metadata": true }, { "name": "manager_employee_uuid", "data_type": "uuid", "mandatory": false, "user_fillable": false, "include_with_system_metadata": true }, { "name": "created_at", "data_type": "timestamptz", "mandatory": false, "user_fillable": false, "include_with_system_metadata": true }, { "name": "updated_at", "data_type": "timestamptz", "mandatory": false, "user_fillable": false, "include_with_system_metadata": true }, { "name": "created_by", "data_type": "uuid_profile", "mandatory": false, "user_fillable": false, "include_with_system_metadata": true } ], "natural_key": ["Effective Date", "Employee Code", "Relationship Code"] } }

11.3 Upload semantics (Job Relationships specific)

Default mode: incremental upsert with carry-forward
Rows grouped by (Employee Code, Effective Date). For each group: carry forward current items NOT in the group; overlay codes with non-DELETE values; remove codes with DELETE. New effective-dated slice created.
DELETE_RECORD keyword
Any row with Value = DELETE_RECORD closes the employee's entire matrix-relationship set as of the effective date (empty new set). Results screen prompts "Confirm N record deletions" before commit.
Warnings (row processes, flagged)
DELETE on an already-unassigned code — no-op. Validation row marked warning. Caller can proceed.
Errors (row blocks commit)
Self-assignment · manager not found · manager inactive · duplicate composite key (Effective Date, Employee Code, Relationship Code) within the same upload.

11.4 Export shape

Export current produces one CSV row per active assignment per employee. Code columns (marked *) are mandatory for re-import. Display-name columns (Employee Name, Relationship Label, Manager Name) are advisory — included for HR readability, ignored on re-import.

Effective Date *,Employee Code *,Employee Name,Relationship Code *,Relationship Label,Value *,Manager Name 06/01/2026,EMP1042,Vijey ASR,PM01,Project Manager,EMP2017,Alice Brown 06/01/2026,EMP1042,Vijey ASR,OM02,Operations Lead,EMP2031,Bob Jones

Export history adds Slice Start and Slice End columns plus every historical slice. NOT round-trip safe. Include inactive records toggle adds matrix relationships where the employee or the manager has status = 'Inactive'.

Part 16 — Bulk Operations Framework (Cross-Module Import / Export)

⚠
Design Phase — Not Yet Implemented
Cross-module framework that owns the Import / Export user experience for every effective-dated and master table. Driven by a bulk_template_registry: modules plug in via a registry row plus their existing processor RPC. Spec: docs/bulk-operations-framework.md. Migrations 365 → 370. ~20 working days.

A single Bulk Operations screen serves all 12 supported templates. Sidebar item below Reports. Permission Matrix grows a new IMPORT / EXPORT section band below NEW HIRE. CSV-only, mm/dd/yyyy strict, codes-only. Export current is round-trip-safe; export history is audit-only. Bulk uploads bypass workflow.

1. Architecture

The single source of truth is bulk_template_registry. UI reads it to drive the dropdown + Permission Matrix band. Edge Functions read it to drive parsing, validation, export queries, and README generation. Adding a 13th module = one registry row + one processor RPC. No code in the framework.

Registry
bulk_template_registry
Job tracker
bulk_upload_job
Storage
bulk-uploads/
Planned migrations
365 · 366 · 367 · 368 · 370

2. Locked Rules (24)

These rules apply uniformly across all 12 supported templates. Same content as JR's §16.1 — replicated here as the framework's own source of truth.

# Rule
1Sidebar item "Import / Export" below Reports
2Visibility gate: any *.bulk_* permission grants tab access
3Permission naming: <module>.bulk_import / .bulk_export
415 modules: personal_info, contact_info, address, passport, identification, emergency_contact, employment, job_relationships, dependents, bank_accounts, employees, department, picklist, project, exchange_rate
5Date format = mm/dd/yyyy strict
6Codes only, never names
7Export = full table contents (filtered by toggles)
8CSV only. Template download = .zip (csv + README.txt)
9Mandatory fields marked * in CSV header
10Export + template have identical column structure (round-trip-safe)
11Two export buttons: Export current + Export history
12Persistent "Include inactive records" toggle (default OFF, applies to both export buttons)
13Bulk uploads bypass workflow regardless of per-module config
14Processing goes through the existing module RPC named in the registry
1510,000 rows hard cap; warning at 5,000
16UTF-8 with BOM, comma delimiter, RFC 4180 quoting
17upload_batch_id UUID on every audit_log row from one upload
18Async via Supabase Edge Function reading from Storage; status to bulk_upload_job; in-app notification on completion
19Cancellation: committed rows stay; remaining rows skipped; status='cancelled'
20DELETE_RECORD keyword removes an entire row; confirmation required
21DELETE keyword removes a single value in a set-snapshot slice
22Export default = user-fillable business fields only. System columns omitted.
23"Include system metadata" toggle adds UUIDs, timestamps, audit IDs, computed columns, display names. Default OFF. NOT round-trip safe.
24Template never includes system fields. Importer silently ignores unknown / system column headers.

3. Database Schema

bulk_template_registry

System-managed metadata. One row per supported template. Seeded by mig 367.

template_code TEXT PRIMARY KEY — e.g. 'job_relationships'
display_label TEXT NOT NULL
description TEXT NOT NULL
icon TEXT NOT NULL (Tabler icon name)
permission_import TEXT NOT NULL
permission_export TEXT NOT NULL
processor_rpc TEXT NOT NULL
deleter_rpc TEXT — for DELETE_RECORD semantics
exporter_query TEXT NOT NULL — SQL template
history_exporter_query TEXT — for Export history
schema_definition JSONB NOT NULL — column list + flags (§5)
workflow_bypass BOOLEAN DEFAULT true
natural_key TEXT[] NOT NULL

bulk_upload_job

In-flight + historical upload tracking. Drives Recent uploads panel and audit.

id UUID PRIMARY KEY (the upload_batch_id)
template_code TEXT FK bulk_template_registry
uploaded_by UUID FK profiles
file_name, storage_path, row_count
valid_count, warning_count, error_count (pre-process)
processed_count, succeeded_count, failed_count, skipped_count (post-process)
status validating | awaiting_user | processing | completed | partial | cancelled | failed
cancelled_at, cancelled_by, completed_at, error_file_path

4. 15 Template Registry Rows

Seeded by mig 367. 12 employee-scoped templates plus 3 reference / admin master tables. Each row references its existing module processor RPC (most already shipped). Per-column schema_definition drives template/export/import behaviour uniformly.

Employee-scoped templates (12)

template_code processor_rpc row_processor natural_key
personal_infoupsert_personal_infoper_row[Effective Date, Employee Code]
contact_infoupsert_contact_infoper_row[Employee Code]
addressupsert_employee_addressper_row[Employee Code, Address Type]
passportupsert_passportper_row[Employee Code, Passport Number]
identificationupsert_identity_recordper_row[Employee Code, ID Type, ID Number]
emergency_contactupsert_emergency_contactper_row[Employee Code, Name]
employmentupsert_employment_infoper_row[Effective Date, Employee Code]
job_relationshipsupsert_job_relationship_setgroup_by_key[Effective Date, Employee Code, Relationship Code]
dependentsupsert_dependent_setgroup_by_key[Effective Date, Employee Code, Dependent Code]
bank_accountsupsert_bank_account_setgroup_by_key[Effective Date, Employee Code, Account Group Id]
employeesupsert_employee_masterper_row[Employee Code]
departmentupsert_departmentper_row[Department Code]

Reference / admin master tables (3)

template_code processor_rpc row_processor natural_key
picklistupsert_picklist_valueper_row[Picklist Id, Ref Id]
projectupsert_projectper_row[Project Code]
exchange_rateupsert_exchange_rateper_row[From Currency, To Currency, Effective Date]

Picklist template supports cascading: Parent Picklist Id + Parent Ref Id columns resolve at import time to parent_value_id (e.g. LOCATION values filtered by ID_COUNTRY). Meta column accepts JSON for picklist_values.meta JSONB (ISO codes, currency mappings).

5. UI Surfaces

Sidebar item
"Import / Export" inserted in App.tsx below Reports. Visibility gate: user_has_any_bulk_permission() RPC — hidden unless user has at least one *.bulk_* permission.
Permission Matrix band
New section band "IMPORT / EXPORT" below "NEW HIRE", above "ADMIN ACCESS". One row per registered template, with paired [ ] Import [ ] Export checkboxes spanning the V/C/E/D/H columns.
Bulk Operations screen
Single page at /admin/import-export. Template Type dropdown (filtered by user perms) drives everything below. Persistent toggles for "Include inactive records" and "Include system metadata". Action bar: [Export current] · [Export history] · [Download template] · [Import data]. Upload flow → validation results table → user confirms → async processing with live status. Recent uploads panel below scoped to selected template + uploader (System Admin sees all).

6. Processing Pipeline

Pre-process (sync, <1s)
Upload → Storage → row bulk_upload_job status=validating → Edge Function bulk_import_validator parses CSV, validates shape/type/refs, composite-key uniqueness → status=awaiting_user with row-level results.
User decision step
User sees the validation table. Options: Cancel · Download error file · Process N valid rows. If file contains DELETE_RECORD, extra confirmation modal: "Confirm N record deletions".
Async processing
Status=processing → Edge Function bulk_import_processor in background task. Sets prowess.bulk_upload_job_id so audit_log rows carry the batch_id. Per-row or per-group BEGIN/EXCEPTION around the processor_rpc. Counts update live. Frontend polls bulk_upload_job every 3s.
Cancellation
Cancel button while status ∈ {validating, processing}. Sets cancelled_at/cancelled_by. Edge Function checks between rows; committed rows stay; remaining rows skipped.
Completion
Status=completed/partial/failed. completed_at set. If any failures, generate _errors.csv with an Error column. In-app notification to uploader. Recent uploads panel refreshes.

7. Planned Migrations

Mig What
365bulk_template_registry, bulk_upload_job, storage bucket, user_has_any_bulk_permission() RPC
36630 bulk permission seeds (15 modules × 2). ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING — JR's bulk perms from mig 359 stay.
36715 registry row seeds with schemas + exporter queries
368Per-module processor RPC alignment — wrappers for contact_info, address, passport, identification, emergency_contact, employees, department, picklist, project, exchange_rate
370Cleanup + indexes after dogfooding

JR shipped at 359–364. Framework continues from 365. ~22 working days total. Edge Functions (4d), per-module RPC alignment (4d — includes the 3 admin master tables), generic screen (3d) are the heaviest phases.

Part 17 — Phone & Contact Module

Flat 1:1 satellite table. Stores mobile, personal email, and business email. Country-aware mobile validation via validateMobile(). Source: employee_contact + mig 410 (business_email).

1. Overview

Table
employee_contact
Flat 1:1 satellite
Permission module
contact_info
Write path
Direct upsert via saveExtendedData()
Key migrations
001 · 020 · 410

2. Table Schema — employee_contact

ColumnTypeemp keyNotes
mobileTEXTemp.mobileEntered WITHOUT country prefix — validated against dial code
country_codeTEXTemp.countryCodeDial code e.g. "+91" — drives validation rule selection
personal_emailTEXTemp.personalEmailPersonal email address
business_emailTEXTemp.businessEmailDenormalized copy of employees.business_email. Written alongside mobile/personal_email in saveExtendedData(). Added mig 410.

3. Mobile Validation — validateMobile(dialCode, mobile)

Source: src/utils/validateMobile.ts. Mobile entered without the country prefix. Dial code selects the rule; falls back to generic 7–15 digit check for unlisted codes.

Dial CodeCountryPatternHintPlaceholder
+91India/^[6-9]\d{9}$/10 digits, starting with 6, 7, 8, or 9e.g. 9876543210
+92Pakistan/^03\d{9}$/11 digits starting with 03e.g. 03001234567
+966Saudi Arabia/^05\d{8}$/10 digits starting with 05e.g. 0501234567
+94Sri Lanka/^07\d{8}$/10 digits starting with 07e.g. 0712345678
+20Egypt/^01\d{9}$/11 digits starting with 01e.g. 01012345678
+44UK/^(07\d{9}|7\d{9})$/10–11 digits starting with 7 or 07e.g. 07911123456
+971UAE/^(05\d{8}|5\d{8})$/9–10 digits starting with 5 or 05e.g. 0501234567
all othersGeneric/^\d{7,15}$/7–15 digits (ITU range)e.g. 9876543210
Helper exports: mobilePlaceholder(dialCode) — sample placeholder for the input field.  |  mobileHint(dialCode) — descriptive format hint shown below the input (null for generic codes).

4. Permissions

Permission codeActionWhat it gates
contact_info.viewviewRead mobile, personal email, business email
contact_info.editeditUpdate any contact field

5. Frontend Placement

MyProfile (ESS)Phone section — mobile + country code + personal email. Business email shown read-only.
EmployeeEditPanel / AddEmployeePhone tab — same fields. Dial code drives validateMobile() on blur.

Part 18 — Passport Module

Flat 1:1 satellite table (one passport per employee). Country-aware number format and validity period validation via validatePassportNumber() and validatePassportValidity().

1. Overview

Table
passports
UNIQUE on employee_id — 1:1
Permission module
passport
Validation util
src/utils/validatePassport.ts
Key migrations
001 (initial schema)

2. Table Schema — passports

ColumnTypeemp keyNotes
idUUID PKemp.passportIdInternal — not displayed
countryTEXTemp.passportCountryUUID ref_id from ID_COUNTRY picklist — resolved to label for validation
passport_numberTEXTemp.passportNumberCountry-specific format — see validation table below
issue_dateDATEemp.passportIssueDateISO YYYY-MM-DD
expiry_dateDATEemp.passportExpiryDateMust be after issue_date. Max validity check applied per country.

3. Number Validation — validatePassportNumber(countryName, number)

Accepts the resolved display name (e.g. "India"), not the UUID. Unlisted countries pass with any non-empty string.

CountryPatternFormat hintPlaceholder
India/^[A-Z]{1}[0-9]{7}$/i1 letter + 7 digitse.g. V6543578
Pakistan/^[A-Z]{2}[0-9]{7}$/i2 letters + 7 digitse.g. AB1234567
Saudi Arabia/^[0-9]{10}$/10 numeric digitse.g. 1234567890
Sri Lanka/^[A-Z]{1,2}[0-9]{7}$/i1–2 letters + 7 digitse.g. N1234567
All others—Any non-empty stringe.g. AB1234567

4. Validity Validation — validatePassportValidity(countryName, issueDate, expiryDate)

Always checks expiry > issue. Then applies country-specific max-years cap.

CountryMax validityHint shown to user
India10 yearsValid for up to 10 years from the Issue Date
Pakistan10 yearsValid for up to 10 years from the Issue Date
Saudi Arabia10 yearsValid for up to 10 years from the Issue Date
Sri Lanka10 yearsValid for up to 10 years from the Issue Date
All othersNo capOnly expiry > issue enforced
Helper exports: passportNumberPlaceholder(countryName) — placeholder text.  |  passportNumberHint(countryName) — format hint below the input.  |  passportValidityHint(countryName) — validity note beside the expiry field.

5. Permissions

CodeWhat it gates

passport.editUpsert passport record

ColumnTypeemp.idRecords[n] keyNotes

3. Validation Matrix — getIdRule(countryName, idTypeName)

Both args are resolved display-name strings (case-insensitive). Returns null for unrecognised combinations — any non-empty value accepted. Source: src/utils/validateIdentity.ts.

🇮🇳 India

ID TypePatternValidityHintPlaceholder
Aadhaar12 digits (spaces/hyphens stripped)Lifetime (9999-12-31)12-digit number — spaces and hyphens are ignorede.g. 1234 5678 9012
PAN/^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]{1}$/Lifetime (9999-12-31)10 chars — 5 letters, 4 digits, 1 lettere.g. ABCDE1234F
Driving LicenseSS + RTO(2) + YYYY + 7d (spaces/hyphens stripped)20 yearsState code + RTO + year + 7 digitse.g. TN0120121234567

🇵🇰 Pakistan

ID TypePatternValidityHintPlaceholder
CNIC13 digits (hyphens stripped)10 years13 digits in format 12345-1234567-1e.g. 12345-1234567-1
NICOP13 digits (hyphens stripped)10 years13 digits in format 12345-1234567-1e.g. 12345-1234567-1

🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia

ID TypePatternValidityHintPlaceholder
Iqama/^2[0-9]{9}$/1 year (renewable)10 digits starting with 2e.g. 2123456789
Saudi National ID/^1[0-9]{9}$/10 years10 digits starting with 1e.g. 1123456789

🇱🇰 Sri Lanka

ID TypePatternValidityHintPlaceholder
NIC9d+V/X or 12 digitsLifetime (9999-12-31)Old: 9 digits + V or X · New: 12 digitse.g. 123456789V or 199012345678
Helper exports: validateIdentityNumber(country, idType, value) — error string or null.  |  idNumberPlaceholder(country, idType) — placeholder text.  |  idNumberHint(country, idType) — format hint below input.  |  defaultExpiryDate(country, idType) — ISO date string auto-default.  |  idValidityLabel(country, idType) — short validity label beside expiry field.

395 · 396 · 397 · 398 · 405 · 406 · 407

2. Table Schema — employee_education

ColumnTypeNullNotes
idUUID PKNOT NULLInternal — not displayed
education_levelTEXTNOT NULLref_id from EDUCATION_LEVEL picklist — e.g. Bachelor's, Master's, PhD

institutionTEXTNOT NULLFree-form — e.g. "Anna University"

inactive_at / inactive_byTIMESTAMPTZ / UUIDNULLSet when soft-deleted

chk_edu_institution_not_blanklength(trim(institution)) > 0
