Part 23 — Termination Module (Event-Based Lifecycle)

✓
Implemented — 2026-06-04
All 8 phases shipped. Migrations: 482 (schema), 483 (notice_period_days), 484 (picklists + permissions), 485 (backend format validation), 487 (end_date cleanup), 489 (RPCs), 491 (MANAGER_INITIATED), 492 (workflow resolution), 493 (wf_submit guard + wf_sync_module_status branch), 494 (bulk hook). Edge Functions: apply-termination-approval, apply-termination-reversal, process-scheduled-terminations (daily cron). Source of truth: docs/termination-design.md.

Introduces a dedicated employee_terminations event table — NOT effective-dated — alongside its own reversal table employee_termination_reversals and shared employee_termination_attachments. A new termination workflow module joins the engine; wf_submit gains a concurrent guard that blocks all other modules while a termination is PENDING. Post-approval automation runs via Edge Functions: slice closure, status flip (which triggers the existing JR fanout for free), notifications, and a daily scheduler for future-dated terminations. Plugs into the Bulk Operations Framework as the 17th template — `bulk_export` is `CREATE OR REPLACE`-d to 17 WHEN clauses.

1. Overview

Termination is a point-in-time business event. termination_date IS the effective date of separation — no effective_from/effective_to required. The employment satellite handles the timeline (slice closes on termination_date, inactive slice begins on termination_date + 1); the termination row carries lifecycle metadata, workflow state, attachments, and audit trail. Reversal is a separate workflow transaction on its own table — preserves the original termination row unchanged for audit, matches Workday's "Reverse Termination" Business Process.

Event table
employee_terminations
Reversal table
employee_termination_reversals
Workflow module
termination
+ concurrent guard in wf_submit
Planned migrations
382 · 383 · 384 · 385 · 386 · 387

2. Reason Picklists

Visibility is driven by transaction context, not user role. logged_in_employee_id = target_employee_id → RESIGNATION_REASON; otherwise → TERMINATION_REASON. Codes immutable; admin edits labels via standard picklist management.

RESIGNATION_REASON (8 codes)

CodeDefault Label
BETTER_OPPORTUNITYBetter Opportunity
HIGHER_STUDIESHigher Studies
RELOCATIONRelocation
PERSONAL_REASONSPersonal Reasons
FAMILY_COMMITMENTSFamily Commitments
HEALTH_REASONSHealth Reasons
RETIREMENTRetirement
OTHEROther

TERMINATION_REASON (11 codes)

CodeDefault Label
PERFORMANCEPerformance
MISCONDUCTMisconduct
POLICY_VIOLATIONPolicy Violation
PROBATION_FAILUREProbation Failure
ABSCONDINGAbsconding
POSITION_REDUNDANCYPosition Redundancy
ORG_RESTRUCTURINGOrganisation Restructuring
END_OF_CONTRACTEnd of Contract
RETIREMENTRetirement
DEATHDeath
OTHEROther

3. Table Schema

employee_terminations

Column Type Notes
iduuid PKSurrogate PK
employee_iduuidFK → employees.id ON DELETE RESTRICT
separation_datedate NOT NULLEmployee's stated intent. Immutable after submission. SELF: must be ≥ notice_expiry_date. Renamed from termination_date (mig 497).
notice_expiry_datedateAlways computed: submitted_at + notice_period_days_snapshot. Never user-supplied. Renamed from notice_date (mig 497).
notice_period_days_snapshotinteger NOT NULLPoint-in-time copy of employee_employment.notice_period_days at submission. Stored for audit. Added mig 497.
last_working_datedateJob anchor. Defaults to separation_date at submission. HR can override — earlier triggers notice waiver. All post-approval jobs fire on this date.
submitted_attimestamptzStamped once by trigger on DRAFT → PENDING. Base date for notice_expiry_date computation. Added mig 497.
termination_reason_codetext NOT NULLFK to picklist value (RESIGNATION_REASON or TERMINATION_REASON depending on initiation type)
termination_initiation_typetext NOT NULLCHECK IN (SELF, HR_INITIATED, MANAGER_INITIATED, ADMIN_INITIATED, SYSTEM_INITIATED) — auto-derived by RPC
notice_period_waived / notice_period_waiver_reasonboolean / textAUTO: set true if LWD < notice_expiry_date. SELF: hard block. HR/MGR: auto-waive, waiver reason required.
eligible_for_rehire / regrettable_terminationboolean / booleanHR-only fields — stripped from SELF submissions (forced true / NULL).
commentstext NOT NULLCHECK length ≥ 20; CHECK length ≥ 50 when reason = OTHER
workflow_statustext NOT NULLCHECK IN (DRAFT, PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, WITHDRAWN, REVERSED). Denormalized; partial unique index references it.
workflow_instance_id / approved_at / approved_byuuid / timestamptz / uuidDenormalized from wf_instance for query perf
scheduled_executed / scheduled_executed_atboolean / timestamptzIdempotency markers for the daily scheduler. Cron fires when last_working_date ≤ CURRENT_DATE.
final_settlement_processed / final_settlement_dateboolean / datev2 stubs for final settlement
upload_batch_iduuidBulk traceability — every row from one upload shares this
created_by / updated_by / created_at / updated_atuuid / timestamptzStandard audit columns
Partial unique index — only one active termination per employee
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX uq_employee_active_termination
  ON employee_terminations (employee_id)
  WHERE workflow_status IN ('PENDING', 'APPROVED');
REJECTED / WITHDRAWN / REVERSED rows excluded → rehire scenarios work cleanly. Reversing an APPROVED termination unlocks the index for a fresh submission.

Field Behaviour by Initiation Type

Authoritative source for notice period: employee_employment.notice_period_days — read by the RPC at submission time for all initiation types. notice_expiry_date is always derived from this; it is never a manual input.
Field SELF HR_INITIATED MANAGER_INITIATED
separation_date ✓ Employee sets. Must be ≥ notice_expiry_date. ✓ HR sets at submission. ✓ Manager sets at submission.
notice_expiry_date AUTO submitted_at + notice_period_days. Read-only. Never user-set. AUTO Same — always computed. AUTO Same — always computed.
last_working_date
JOB ANCHOR		 ✓ Defaults to separation_date. Employee cannot set earlier than separation_date. ✓ HR can override. Earlier → triggers notice waiver. All jobs fire on this date. ✓ Manager can override. Same waiver logic as HR.
notice_period_waived AUTO If LWD < notice_expiry_date → hard block. Employee cannot waive own notice. AUTO If LWD < notice_expiry_date → waiver flagged. Waiver reason required. AUTO Same as HR.
eligible_for_rehire
regrettable_termination		 ✗ Stripped — forced true / NULL. ✓ HR sets explicitly. ✓ Manager sets.
Notice period logic (submit_termination RPC)
notice_expiry_date = submitted_at + notice_period_days_snapshot

SELF:
  separation_date must be ≥ notice_expiry_date   ← form min + RPC guard
  last_working_date defaults to separation_date
  if LWD < notice_expiry_date → HARD BLOCK (no self-waiver allowed)

HR / MANAGER:
  last_working_date defaults to separation_date if not provided
  if LWD < notice_expiry_date → auto-waive, notice_period_waiver_reason required
  all downstream jobs execute on last_working_date
Design principle: separation_date = intent (immutable). last_working_date = reality (HR-adjustable). All jobs run on reality.

employee_termination_reversals

Column Type Notes
iduuid PKSurrogate PK
termination_iduuidFK → employee_terminations.id ON DELETE RESTRICT
reversal_reason / commentstext NOT NULLCHECK comments length ≥ 20
workflow_status / workflow_instance_id / approved_at / approved_bytext / uuid / timestamptz / uuidOwn workflow state (DRAFT/PENDING/APPROVED/REJECTED/WITHDRAWN — never REVERSED)
created_by / updated_by / created_at / updated_atuuid / timestamptzStandard audit columns
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX uq_termination_active_reversal ON employee_termination_reversals (termination_id) WHERE workflow_status IN ('PENDING','APPROVED');

employee_termination_attachments

Shared by both terminations and reversals. CHECK constraint enforces exactly one of termination_id or reversal_id is non-null. Standard pattern (file_name, original_file_name, file_path, file_size_bytes, mime_type, is_active, uploaded_at, uploaded_by). Retained permanently — no purge policy.

employee_employment — schema changes

ADD COLUMN notice_period_days INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 30 CHECK (IN (30, 90, 120))
DROP COLUMN end_date — data migrated to employee_terminations.termination_date
DROP COLUMN termination_reason_code — data migrated to employee_terminations.termination_reason_code
Notice period is effective-dated. Termination reads the slice where effective_from ≤ termination_date < effective_to — not the current active slice — so backdated or future-dated terminations get the contractually correct value.

4. RPC Contracts

WRITE submit_termination(p_employee_id, p_termination_data, p_attachments)

Derives termination_initiation_type from caller context, validates picklist + notice period from the employment slice covering termination_date, enforces self-service notice-period hard block, inserts DRAFT row, calls wf_submit('termination', ..., kind='create'), flips to PENDING. Returns {ok, termination_id, workflow_instance_id, workflow_status}.

WRITE submit_termination_reversal(p_termination_id, p_reversal_data, p_attachments)

Original must be workflow_status='APPROVED'. Inserts row in employee_termination_reversals, calls wf_submit('termination', reversal_id, kind='reversal'). The termination module name means the concurrent guard in wf_submit permits this submission.

READ get_employee_terminations(p_employee_id)

Latest termination + reversal (if any) + attachments resolved. Returns {ok, termination, reversal, attachments}.

READ get_termination_history(p_employee_id, p_include_reversed boolean default true)

All terminations ordered termination_date DESC, including REVERSED. Gated on termination.history OR .view.

UTIL get_termination_deactivation_impact(p_employee_id)

Extends JR's get_deactivation_impact. Adds direct-reports count and JR codes held by the terminating employee. Powers the TerminationImpactModal shown before HR/Admin submission so they can see the full blast radius.

BULK upsert_termination_bulk(p_employee_id, p_termination_data, p_upload_batch_id)

Bulk Operations Framework processor. Bypasses workflow per framework rule §13 — safety enforced by termination.bulk_import permission lockdown. Jumps the row straight to workflow_status='APPROVED', stamps termination_initiation_type='SYSTEM_INITIATED' and upload_batch_id, then invokes the same post-approval Edge Function path used by workflow approvals (slice closure, status flip, notifications).

5. Workflow Integration

New termination workflow module registered alongside existing modules. Standard wf_submit/approve/reject/resubmit/withdraw engine handles the lifecycle — no engine rewrite required. Three default workflow templates seeded (fully configurable, not hardcoded):

Default templates
Self-Service Resignation: MANAGER → HR_APPROVER → FINAL_APPROVER
HR/Admin Initiated: HR_MANAGER → FINAL_APPROVER
Reversal: HR_MANAGER → FINAL_APPROVER
Concurrent workflow guard (inside wf_submit)
IF p_module <> 'termination' THEN
  IF EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM employee_terminations
    WHERE employee_id = p_target_id
      AND workflow_status = 'PENDING'
  ) THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'A termination is pending approval for this employee.';
  END IF;
END IF;
Blocks every other module's submissions while a termination is PENDING. Termination's own submission and its reversal submission both pass p_module='termination' so the guard skips them.
Approval dispatch (locked)
When wf_approve finalises a termination-module instance, a trigger on wf_instance inspects which entity table the instance was created against and invokes apply_termination_approval or apply_termination_reversal via Edge Function. Both functions update the denormalized workflow_status/approved_at/approved_by on the relevant row.

6. Post-Approval Automation (Edge Functions)

All downstream work runs in Edge Functions, never DB triggers — consistent with the spec's "visible, testable, debuggable, retryable" principle and the existing bulk-framework and JR-fanout patterns.

apply_termination_approval
1. If termination_date > today: skip immediate execution; wait for scheduler.
2. Close current employment slice: effective_to = termination_date.
3. Insert Inactive slice: effective_from = termination_date + 1, status=Inactive.
4. Set employees.status='Inactive' → fires sync_profile_on_employee_status, which closes JR sets for free.
5. Stamp scheduled_executed=true, scheduled_executed_at=NOW().
6. Fire downstream integration events (payroll, IdP).
7. Dispatch notifications.
apply_termination_reversal
1. Flip original termination: workflow_status='REVERSED' (unlocks the partial unique index).
2. Delete the Inactive slice (effective_from = original.termination_date + 1).
3. Reopen prior slice: effective_to='9999-12-31'.
4. Set employees.status='Active'.
5. Does NOT auto-restore JR — JR's locked rule "Never auto-restore on re-activation" holds. HR must reassign matrix relationships manually.
6. Dispatch notifications.
process_scheduled_terminations (daily cron)
Supabase scheduled trigger runs once per day. Selects workflow_status='APPROVED' AND termination_date ≤ today AND scheduled_executed=false FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED. Per-row BEGIN/EXCEPTION for failure isolation. Idempotent — reruns on the same day pick up zero rows after the first pass. Audit logged.

7. UI Surfaces

Every surface explicitly clones an existing pattern — not designed from scratch.

File Change Pattern mirrored
src/components/shared/TerminationPortlet.tsxNEW. View/draft/pending/history modes for the current termination.IdentificationPortlet
src/components/shared/TerminationForm.tsxNEW. Self-service variant — limited to employee-visible fields.Bank Account add-form
src/components/shared/TerminationHRForm.tsxNEW. HR/Admin variant with rehire eligibility + regrettable flag.Identification HR block
src/components/shared/TerminationReversalForm.tsxNEW. Reversal submission (HR-only).HR form pattern
src/components/shared/TerminationConfirmDialog.tsxNEW. Confirmation modal before self-service submit (spec §11.1).Standard confirm dialog
src/components/admin/TerminationImpactModal.tsxNEW. Pre-submit warning listing affected reports + JR codes.JR DeactivationImpactModal
src/hooks/useTerminationData.tsNEW. Fetches current + history.useEmployeeData hook family
MyProfile/index.tsxNew <section id="mps-termination">; perm registry entry (~line 1124); data fetch in useEmployeeData.Identification section (mps-identification ~line 1998)
EmployeeEditPanel.tsxNew HR-only termination block (~line 800+); newTerminationForm state; TerminationImpactModal trigger.Identification edit block (newIdForm pattern)
EmployeeDetails.tsxNew Termination tab/column with full history (gated on termination.history).Identification column + tab
ApproverInbox.tsxDictionary entries (lines 824–878); new TerminationEnrichment component; reversal sub-render.BankEnrichment (line 1421)
WorkflowReview.tsxprofile_termination section; FULL_REVIEW_MODULES.add('profile_termination').profile_bank block
PermissionMatrix.tsxEMPLOYEE-band row "Termination" with V / E / H columns. Bulk perms surface in IMPORT/EXPORT band via framework.Education row pattern
AddEmployee.tsxNo changes. Termination is post-hire.—

8. Locked Decisions

Event table — NOT effective-dated. termination_date IS the effective date of separation.
notice_period_days on satellite — lives in employee_employment, effective-dated. Termination reads the slice covering termination_date.
JR cleanup is free — sync_profile_on_employee_status fanout already handles it on Inactive transition. Verify only.
Denormalized workflow_status — required for the partial unique index and for the REVERSED state not in wf_instance.
Concurrent guard in wf_submit — blocks all modules except termination itself while a termination is PENDING.
Reversal = separate table + REVERSED flag on original. Matches Workday Business Process model.
Manager → reports — pre-approval warning modal; post-approval keep manager_id, wf_resolve_approver_ex escalates Inactive.
Future-dated scheduler — daily Edge Function with idempotency flag. No pg_cron.
17th bulk template — permission-gated, SYSTEM_INITIATED, CREATE OR REPLACE bulk_export to 17 WHEN clauses.
Partial unique index — one active termination per employee. REJECTED/WITHDRAWN/REVERSED excluded → rehire works cleanly.
Initiation type auto-derived — self/HR/admin/system, never user-selected. HR self-resignation = SELF.
Notice-period guard — hard block for self-service; HR/Admin exempt but auto-waiver with mandatory reason.
Withdrawal — PENDING only. APPROVED requires reversal transaction.
Permissions — termination.view / .edit / .history / .bulk_import / .bulk_export. No default grants.
Comments min — 20 chars standard, 50 chars when reason = OTHER.
No hire-wizard surface — termination is post-hire. AddEmployee unchanged.

9. Planned Migrations

Mig What
382Schema: employee_terminations, employee_termination_reversals, employee_termination_attachments + indexes + partial unique constraints + audit triggers.
383employee_employment.notice_period_days ADD COLUMN + drop legacy end_date / termination_reason_code after data migration.
384Picklist seeds (RESIGNATION_REASON 8, TERMINATION_REASON 11) + 5 permission seeds + notification templates.
385RPCs: submit / withdraw / reversal / read / deactivation-impact / derive-initiation-type helper.
386Workflow module registration + wf_submit concurrent guard + approval dispatch triggers + default workflow templates.
387Bulk framework hook (must be LAST in set): registry row + upsert_termination_bulk + 2 bulk permission seeds + CREATE OR REPLACE bulk_export with 17 WHEN clauses (Education shipped 16 at mig 381).

Edge Functions deployed separately: apply_termination_approval, apply_termination_reversal, process_scheduled_terminations (daily cron). Migration numbering picks up where Education's 378–381 finishes; design doc is the source of truth if parallel work shifts numbers.

10. Bulk Operations Integration

Termination plugs into the cross-module Bulk Operations Framework as the 17th template (Education was the 16th, mig 381). All 24 framework rules apply — most importantly:

Workflow bypass + permission lockdown
Bulk uploads bypass workflow per framework rule §13. Termination is high-stakes — safety enforced by locking termination.bulk_import to a tiny admin group. No HR or line-manager role gets this by default.
SYSTEM_INITIATED stamping
Every bulk-uploaded row carries termination_initiation_type='SYSTEM_INITIATED' and the upload's upload_batch_id, distinguishing it from per-employee submissions in reporting and audit filters.
Same post-approval automation
Bulk rows still go through the same apply_termination_approval Edge Function path — slice closure, status flip, JR fanout, notifications. Same code path = same correctness guarantees.
Future-dated bulk rows
Bulk rows with termination_date > today wait for the daily scheduler — same Edge Function picks them up alongside individually-submitted future-dated terminations.

Registry row

{ "template_code": "termination", "display_label": "Termination", "description": "Employee termination transactions — workflow bypassed, permission-gated", "icon": "ti-user-x", "permission_view": "termination.bulk_export", "permission_edit": "termination.bulk_import", "workflow_bypass": true, "processor_rpc": "upsert_termination_bulk", "schema_definition": { "columns": [ { "name": "Employee Id *", "data_type": "code_employee", "mandatory": true, "user_fillable": true }, { "name": "Termination Date *", "data_type": "date_mmddyyyy", "mandatory": true, "user_fillable": true }, { "name": "Termination Reason Code *", "data_type": "code_picklist:TERMINATION_REASON", "mandatory": true, "user_fillable": true }, { "name": "Last Working Date", "data_type": "date_mmddyyyy", "mandatory": false, "user_fillable": true }, { "name": "Notice Period Waived", "data_type": "boolean", "mandatory": false, "user_fillable": true }, { "name": "Notice Period Waiver Reason", "data_type": "text", "mandatory": false, "user_fillable": true }, { "name": "Eligible For Rehire", "data_type": "boolean", "mandatory": false, "user_fillable": true }, { "name": "Regrettable Termination", "data_type": "boolean", "mandatory": false, "user_fillable": true }, { "name": "Comments *", "data_type": "text", "mandatory": true, "user_fillable": true }, { "name": "Operation", "data_type": "keyword:CREATE,REVERSE", "mandatory": false, "user_fillable": true } ], "natural_key": ["Employee Id", "Termination Date"] } }

Mig 387 is the LAST migration in the termination set — it CREATE OR REPLACEs bulk_export with 17 WHEN clauses. Any replay must preserve all 17 branches.

Part 24 — Global Employee Search (SuccessFactors-style People Profile Navigation)

⚠
Implemented — 2026-06-05
Adds a header-bar employee search and lets authorised users navigate into another employee's profile. Reuses the existing MyProfile screen with a new ProfileContext — no separate Employee Profile page. Every existing user_can(module, action, employee_id) call inside MyProfile gets threaded with viewedEmployeeId. Design locked (docs/global-employee-search-design.md). Migs 496–497 backend + 7 frontend phases. ~9 working days total.

SuccessFactors People Profile pattern adapted for Prowess. One profile architecture; two contexts. The header search box (gated by new permission employee_search.view) drives navigation to /profile/:employeeId? — when the param is set, MyProfile operates in employee mode against that target. Workflow Subject = viewed employee; Actor = logged-in user. Section visibility, edit actions, and workflows keep flowing through the existing permission engine — no new gating model.

1. Overview

A user with employee_search.view sees a search box in the header. As they type (2-char minimum, 300ms debounce), a search_employees RPC returns up to 10 trigram-similarity-ordered matches. Selecting a result routes to /profile/:id; the existing MyProfile renders with that viewedEmployeeId threaded through every permission check and data hook. Recently Viewed (last 10 employees) is cached in localStorage per device.

New permission
employee_search.view
New RPC
search_employees
New React context
ProfileContext
viewedEmployeeId + isSelf
Planned migrations
496 · 497

2. Schema Changes

Backend footprint is intentionally small. One generated text column on employees, one GIN trigram index, one permission row, one RPC. No new tables.

ALTER TABLE employees ADD COLUMN searchable_text TEXT GENERATED ALWAYS AS ( COALESCE(employee_code, '') || ' ' || COALESCE(full_name, '') || ' ' || COALESCE(email, '') ) STORED; CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm; CREATE INDEX ix_employees_searchable_trgm ON employees USING gin (searchable_text gin_trgm_ops); INSERT INTO permissions (name) VALUES ('employee_search.view') ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;

Storage cost on 500 employees ≈ 15 KB. Trigram index supports sub-50ms ILIKE and similarity() queries.

3. RPC Contract

READ search_employees(p_query, p_limit=10, p_include_inactive=false)

Permission check via user_can('employee_search', 'view', NULL) (target_id NULL because search is a global capability). Trims + sanitises p_query; rejects length < 2. ILIKE filter on searchable_text with similarity() ordering. Returns up to p_limit rows of {employee_id, employee_code, full_name, email, status, manager_id, avatar_url, similarity}.

SELECT id, employee_code, full_name, email, status, manager_id, avatar_url, similarity(searchable_text, p_query) AS sim FROM employees WHERE searchable_text ILIKE '%' || p_query || '%' AND (p_include_inactive OR status = 'Active') ORDER BY sim DESC, full_name ASC LIMIT p_limit;

4. Profile Architecture (Two Modes, One Screen)

Self mode
Route: /profile (or /profile/:id where id = current user). isSelf=true. No banner. Behaves exactly like today's MyProfile — backwards compatible.
Employee mode
Route: /profile/:employeeId where id ≠ current user. isSelf=false. Subtle blue banner with "Viewing EMP000X · Name · (Return to your profile)". Inactive employees show amber banner.
Permission threading (locked)
Every existing user_can(module, action) call inside MyProfile becomes user_can(module, action, viewedEmployeeId). ~30–50 mechanical replacements. Section visibility, edit toggles, workflow submissions all evaluate against the viewed employee — never against the logged-in user.

5. Header Search UX

┌─ EmployeeSearchBox open ────────────────────────────┐ │ [ vi ] ✕ │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ RECENT │ │ ◉ EMP001 · Vijey ASR · vijey@company.com │ │ ◉ EMP012 · Priya Sharma · priya@company.com │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ RESULTS (3) │ │ ◯ EMP001 · Vijey ASR · vijey@company.com │ │ ◯ EMP015 · Vignesh Kumar · vignesh@company.com │ │ ◯ EMP022 · Vinoth Raj · vinoth@company.com │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
2-char minimum + 300ms debounce + max 10 results. Sorted by trigram similarity descending, then full_name ascending.
Keyboard: ⌘K / Ctrl+K focuses from anywhere. Esc closes. Arrow keys navigate. Enter selects highlighted.
Recently Viewed shown when query is empty. localStorage per device. Last 10 entries with cached {id, code, name, email}. Cleared on logout. Self views NOT added.
Mobile (≤768px): header shows an icon only. Tap opens a full-screen modal with the same dropdown experience.
Include inactive toggle (HR-only by default) — Inactive employees still searchable but profile renders read-only with an amber banner.
No search box rendered for users without employee_search.view. UI completely hidden, not just disabled.

6. Workflow & Audit Behaviour

Subject vs Actor (locked)
HR (EMP_B) opens EMP_A's profile, submits a dependent change. The workflow instance is created with target_id = EMP_A (subject), routing to EMP_A's manager + HR chain. created_by = EMP_B (actor) is recorded for audit. No workflow engine change required — wf_submit already takes a target_id; this work just threads viewedEmployeeId through the submit calls.
Audit columns (already correct)
Prowess audit columns are already split — created_by ≠ employee_id on every employee-scoped table. No schema change. Phase 6 adds an integration test: HR creates a dependent for EMP_A while logged in as EMP_B, assert created_by=EMP_B and employee_id=EMP_A.

7. UI Surfaces & File Changes

File Change
src/contexts/ProfileContext.tsxNEW. Provider + useProfileContext hook exposing viewedEmployeeId, isSelf, viewedEmployee, isLoading.
src/hooks/useEmployeeSearch.tsNEW. Debounced wrapper around search_employees RPC. Returns {results, loading, error}.
src/hooks/useRecentlyViewed.tsNEW. localStorage CRUD (add/remove/list/clear), capped at 10 entries, dedupe by employee_id.
src/components/header/EmployeeSearchBox.tsxNEW. Header search input, dropdown, keyboard handling, mobile modal.
src/components/header/SearchResultRow.tsx + RecentlyViewedList.tsxNEW. Row component + list wrapper for the dropdown.
src/components/profile/ProfileEmptyState.tsx + InactiveEmployeeBanner.tsx + EmployeeModeBanner.tsxNEW. Empty state + Inactive banner + employee-mode header banner.
App.tsx / routes.tsxReplace /profile route with /profile/:employeeId?; wrap with <ProfileContextProvider>.
MyProfile/index.tsxMECHANICAL REFACTOR. ~30–50 line-level edits: every currentUser.employee_id → viewedEmployeeId; every user_can(m,a) → user_can(m,a,viewedEmployeeId); every data hook takes employeeId; banners + empty state added.
Header.tsx (or AppShell.tsx)Mount <EmployeeSearchBox /> between logo and notification bell. Conditional render on user_can('employee_search','view',null).
PermissionMatrix.tsxSYSTEM-band row "Employee Search" with single column "View".
src/lib/auth.tsOn logout: clear prowess.recentlyViewed.* keys from localStorage.
EmployeeEditPanel.tsxNo changes. Coexists with MyProfile employee mode — different UX paradigm.

8. Locked Decisions

One MyProfile, two modes — Self vs Employee. No separate page.
Route /profile/:employeeId? — optional param defaults to self.
New permission employee_search.view. No default grants.
Target population scoping DEFERRED to v2. v1 leans on existing user_can(m,a,target_id).
MyProfile + EmployeeEditPanel coexist. Do not merge.
ProfileContext threads viewedEmployeeId through ~30–50 user_can + data hook calls.
Backend = 1 RPC + 1 generated col + 1 index. No new tables.
Recently Viewed = localStorage per device. Last 10. Cleared on logout.
Inactive employees searchable behind toggle. Amber banner. Edit hidden.
Empty-state semantics — zero view perms ⇒ "No permission" panel, not 404.
Workflow subject = viewedEmployeeId; actor = currentUserId. No engine change.
Audit Actor/Subject already correct. Integration test only.
Permission Matrix row in SYSTEM band, single column "View".
Deep-linking works. URLs bookmarkable; permissions re-check on render.
Bulk framework: N/A. Read-only query; not a data mutation.
Header placement between logo and notification bell. Mobile: icon → modal.
Phase 0 Discovery MANDATORY. Grep MyProfile for self-context usage; verify get_target_employees helper. Revise estimates after findings.
"On behalf of" annotation REQUIRED. New column workflow_instances.initiated_by_actor_id. Surfaced in ApproverInbox and subject's view when actor ≠ subject.
EmployeeEditPanel routes each module through its existing path — workflow-gated modules flow through workflow regardless of surface. Do NOT collapse to "no workflow".

9. Planned Migrations & Phase Plan

Mig What
496Generated searchable_text column + pg_trgm extension + GIN trigram index + employee_search.view + employee_search.view_inactive permission seeds + workflow_instances.initiated_by_actor_id UUID column.
497search_employees RPC with permission check + target-population filter + similarity ordering. Plus get_target_employees(p_module, p_action) helper if Phase 0 confirms it's missing.
498wf_submit stamps initiated_by_actor_id = auth.uid() when subject ≠ submitter (powers "Submitted on behalf of" annotation).
Phase Scope Days
0DISCOVERY (mandatory): grep MyProfile for self-context usage; verify get_target_employees helper exists. Revise Phase 1 + Phase 3 estimates from findings.~0.5
1Schema + permissions + RPC + helper (if needed) + initiated_by_actor_id column~0.5–1
2ProfileContext + route~1
3Refactor MyProfile to use ProfileContext (scope unknown until Phase 0)~3–5
4Header search UI (no Recently Viewed)~1.5
5Permission Matrix + "On behalf of" annotation UI + Part 24 status update~1
6Integration tests (target-pop filter, on-behalf-of, audit, banner, empty state)~1
7Cross-browser + a11y polish (keyboard nav, screen reader, mobile overlay)~0.5

Total ~9.5 working days at the optimistic end; Phase 0 findings may push Phase 3 to ~5 days if MyProfile self-context fanout is wider than expected. Backend remains < 1.5 days. Estimate revises after Phase 0 — DO NOT commit to a delivery date before Discovery report.

10. Out of Scope (Future)

  • Target Population (SF-style permission groups) for search scoping
  • Department / Manager / Job Title / Location / National ID search criteria
  • Admin actions surfaced in search ("Terminate Employee", "Transfer Employee", "Promote Employee")
  • Profile view audit log (profile_view_log table)
  • Cross-device Recently Viewed sync (backed table instead of localStorage)
  • Full-text search service when employee count crosses ~5,000
  • Per-field sensitive masking (compensation, performance ratings, etc.)
  • "Recently Edited" panel alongside "Recently Viewed" for HR users
